### 613. External / manual outcome ingestion — P1 (record published work + Rinse backfill)

**What it does:** Lets the operator bring work published *outside* the platform (a manually-posted blog, an edit made directly on the live site) into the outcome ledger, so it can become a measured client win instead of being invisible to the outcome engine. Two pieces on top of the already-hardened manual-record route (`POST /api/outcomes/:workspaceId/actions`, idempotent on `(sourceType, sourceId)`): (1) a **"Record published work"** admin card at the top of the Outcomes dashboard (`RecordPublishedWorkCard`) — fields for page URL, title, type of work, and **"Who published it?"** (Agency → `platform_executed` / Client → `externally_executed`), which records the action with a durable source snapshot (title captured at write time so a later win shows the real headline, B11) via the existing `useRecordOutcomeAction` hook; (2) a one-off `scripts/backfill-rinse-outcomes.ts` that records a workspace's already-published posts (`listPosts` → those with a `publishedAt`) as `platform_executed` manual actions — idempotent (dedups on `manual-backfill:<postId>`), `--dry-run`-able, operator-run only (not CI). The client wrapper `outcomesApi.recordAction` gained the optional `source` field the route already accepted.

**Why it matters to the agency:** The B13 outcome seams only capture work the platform performs automatically; manual/agency posting was a blind spot, so real work never showed up as proof. This closes the gap without a new pipeline — it reuses the hardened record path — and does it **honestly**: agency-published work is `platform_executed`, client-published work is `externally_executed` (never silently over-credited to us, the exact trust line C4 drew). Auto-detection of new pages is deferred to P2.

**Why it matters to clients:** Manually-published work now shows up in the client's wins/results with its real title, so the proof-of-results reflects everything done for them — and work their own team published is framed as theirs, not claimed by the agency.

**Tests:** `tests/component/RecordPublishedWorkCard.test.tsx` (attribution mapping agency→platform_executed / client→externally_executed, source snapshot, disabled-until-valid); `tests/unit/backfill-rinse-outcomes.test.ts` (pure mapper — published-only, skip-no-title FM-2, URL building); a manual-path honesty test in `tests/integration/outcomes-client-routes.test.ts` (a manual `platform_executed` win surfaces client-side with its real snapshot title). Typecheck, `vite build`, pr-check, full suite green. **Spec/plan:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-external-manual-outcome-ingestion-spec.md`, `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-02-external-ingestion-p1-plan.md`.

**Files:** `src/components/admin/outcomes/RecordPublishedWorkCard.tsx`, `src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomeDashboard.tsx`, `src/api/outcomes.ts`, `scripts/backfill-rinse-outcomes.ts`, + tests.

### 612. Reconcile closeout — whole-arc holistic review + attribution-honesty remediation (Reconcile C4)

**What it does:** Closes the Reconcile migration (R0–R12, 24 tickets) with a whole-arc holistic review that per-ticket reviews structurally cannot do — five independent reviewers swept the full staging delta (`e5745db2b..staging`) hunting *cross-ticket integration* defects, and every finding was fixed in one closeout across five themes. **(A) Attribution honesty at every client seam:** the client monthly digest (`getWinsWithValueByWorkspace`), the public win-rate scorecard (`computeScorecard`), and the "What we shipped" wins surface each independently skipped the `not_acted_on` exclusion that the rest of the platform enforces, so unexecuted proposals — and, via `externally_executed`, the client's OWN work — could surface as dollar-valued agency wins in emails and dashboards. `attribution` is now threaded through `TopWin`/`OutcomeWinEntry`; the digest + public scorecard exclude `not_acted_on`; the wins surface renders `externally_executed` honestly ("Wins we called / implemented on your side," never "we shipped"); striking a completed recommendation now neutralizes its completion-time outcome so it can't resurface as a win; Keep buttons pass explicit `not_acted_on`. **(B) struck≠completed payload safety:** migration 168's cleanup fixed only the `status` column, but `recommendation_items` reads/writes are payload-JSON-authoritative — a legacy struck+completed payload was still served as completed AND would abort the delete-then-reinsert on the next regen. A write-choke-point coercion (covers the backfill path too) + `migrate-json` demotion + migration 171 (one-time `json_set` cleanup) close it. **(C) Mirror/respond integrity:** the generic deliverable `/respond` route accepted approve/decline for a recommendation mirror with no `respondToSource` (a dead client greenlight → now 409'd + the verbs hidden), and MCP `apply_recommendation('send')` never mirrored the rec to the client feed (now mirrors + observes the `MirrorResult`). **(D) Snapshot/archive/sweep infra:** rebuilt archive twins shipped UNINDEXED (the `RENAME TO` carried the original index names onto the `_r11_old` copies, so later `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS` no-opped — migration 172 adds `_v2` indexes); the B12 integrity sweep was blind to B13's two newer sourceTypes (`gbp_review_response` row-backed, `audit` not-checkable — now classified + a warn when any type is unclassified); C1's `workspace_id` was backfilled but never written forward by the three snapshot writers (now populated via exact 1:1 site→workspace match); insight-backfill missing the B11 source snapshot; an archive/delete boundary race. **(E) Guards/hygiene:** workspace-less system jobs (incl. the integrity-sweep cron) were cancellable/readable by any non-owner member (now owner-scope-gated); the admin outcome-feed filter derives from the canonical action catalog (restoring 7 missing types); the pr-check ActivityType-minting guard's two silent-pass regex gaps closed; `cancelJob` routed through `validateTransition`; a factually-wrong GLOSSARY entry (phantom flag) corrected.

**Why it matters to the agency:** All mechanical gates were green (28k tests, `verify:platform`, lexicon, pr-check) while these defects sat in the integration surface — the exact "fixture-masked broken feature" failure mode the holistic tier exists to catch. The headline cluster is a trust landmine: a platform that claims credit for work the client did themselves, in an unattended email channel, is worse than one that claims nothing. Fixing it before the production promotion means the first thing clients see from the reconciled data layer is honest.

**Why it matters to clients:** Wins, win-rates, and monthly digests now count only work that was actually done, and externally-executed results are framed as "we called it," not "we shipped it." The recommendation approve flow no longer silently does nothing.

**Tests:** ~150 added/extended across the five themes (digest + public-scorecard `not_acted_on` exclusion, strike neutralization, external-framing render, write-path coercion + migration 171, respond-409 + MCP send-mirror, twin-index PRAGMA + archive/delete boundary, sweep classification + unclassified-warn, snapshot `workspace_id` assertion, job owner-scope, pr-check regex). Controller-owned full-suite gate + `verify:platform` green on the merged tree. **Migrations:** 171 (rec payload cleanup), 172 (archive twin indexes).

**Files:** `server/outcome-tracking.ts`, `shared/types/outcome-tracking.ts`, `server/routes/outcomes.ts`, `server/recommendation-lifecycle.ts`, `src/components/client/Briefing/WinsSurface.tsx`, `src/components/client/OutcomeSummary.tsx`, `src/components/strategy/{TopicClusters,ContentGaps}.tsx`, `server/domains/recommendations/storage.ts`, `server/db/migrate-json.ts`, `server/db/migrations/171-*.sql`, `server/domains/inbox/send-to-client.ts`, `server/mcp/tools/recommendation-actions.ts`, `src/components/client/inbox/UnifiedInbox.tsx`, `server/outcome-source-integrity-sweep-job.ts`, `server/{reports,redirect-store,performance-store,outcome-backfill}.ts`, `server/db/migrations/172-*.sql`, `server/{auth,jobs}.ts`, `server/routes/jobs.ts`, `src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomeActionFeed.tsx`, `scripts/pr-check.ts`, `GLOSSARY.md`, + tests.

### 611. Ephemeral-source snapshot ref — durable win titles survive source regeneration (Reconcile R6-PR1)

**What it does:** Captures an outcome's SOURCE IDENTITY at write time so client-facing win titles stop degrading to generic labels when the ephemeral source is regenerated. Adds two additive, nullable columns to `tracked_actions` (and its archive twin) — `source_label` (flat resolved title) + `source_snapshot` (JSON `{ title?, type?, page? }` identity blob, `parseJsonSafe`-read). `recordAction()` gains an optional `source?: { label; snapshot? }` param, threaded through ~16 production call sites (published posts, briefs, content requests, approvals, client actions, keyword-strategy pairs, resolved insights, completed-recommendation backfill), each snapshotting the source's identity from data it already holds — never fabricating a title (FM-2). Sites with no ephemeral titled producer (schema deploy, brand-voice calibration, strategy regen, decay page ref) intentionally thread no source. The "We Called It" title resolution (`resolveWinTitle`, `server/routes/outcomes.ts`) becomes **snapshot → live → generic**: the write-time snapshot wins first, the existing live lookup and honest generic per-action-type fallback (`WIN_FALLBACK_LABELS`) stay intact behind it (fallback demotion is deferred to R6-PR2). Same pattern as the `predicted_emv` snapshot (migration 116). The migration is twin-compliant with the B10 archive-twin parity assert: it rebuilds `tracked_actions_archive` in canonical column order (live cols + trailing `archived_at`) rather than ADD-COLUMN-ing it, so the boot-time `assertArchiveTwinParity()` still passes and the archive sweep round-trips both new columns. Named + rationale in `docs/adr/0008-ephemeral-source-snapshot-ref.md`; `client_deliverable.source_ref` is the second application site (future).

**Why it matters to the agency:** Outcomes are designed to outlive their sources — recommendation sets are rebuilt on every audit, briefs/posts are edited, approval items are re-minted — so the majority of ledger refs no longer resolve to a live title by the time an outcome is measured. Before this, the client wins surface silently fell back to "Published new content" instead of the specific headline the client saw at send time, eroding the exact trust the wins surface exists to build. Snapshotting identity at write time makes the durable ledger self-sufficient for display, and the snapshot columns give the upcoming integrity sweep (R6-PR2) a typed basis for classifying dangling refs per ref-kind class.

**Why it matters to clients:** The "We Called It" wins now show the real, specific work ("How to choose a local plumber," the exact keyword or page) rather than a generic action label — honest, recognizable proof of results even months later when the underlying recommendation or brief has long since been regenerated.

### 541. Enforced lexicon — GLOSSARY promoted from reference to contract (Reconcile R1-PR1)

**What it does:** Promotes `GLOSSARY.md` from a flat, drift-prone reference into an **enforced vocabulary contract**. A new machine-readable registry (`shared/types/lexicon.ts`) mirrors every glossary term with a governance tag — a **word class** (`canonical` / `externally-mirrored` / `historical` / `proposed`) — and grandfathers the duplicate-exported-type-name census (30 names verified across `shared/` + `server/`, each tagged with the ticket that will resolve it: `R2` for the brand-artifact `Deliverable*` pair; `permanent` for the GA4/GSC/ROI mirror + server-internal twin pairs). `npm run verify:lexicon` (`scripts/lexicon-registry.ts`, modeled on the feature-flag lifecycle verifier) checks GLOSSARY ↔ registry parity in both directions, runs a live scan that fails on any unregistered duplicate exported name, and enforces allowlist hygiene (every entry has a `resolvingTicket`). The glossary is restructured into the four word-class sections plus a PROPOSED section that intakes the redesign mockup vocabulary (client-thread kinds `request`/`instruction`/`approval`, `promotable`, thread `new/ack/handled`, the four cockpit rail names, "promote to strategy signal") snapshotted from the untracked design folder. Wired as a pr-ci-blocking gate (package.json, ci.yml, `verify-platform.ts`, governance registry). Full contract: `docs/rules/lexicon.md`.

**Why it matters to the agency:** This is the root ticket of the Reconcile migration — every later ticket's vocabulary hangs off it. The lexicon closes the class of silent bugs where a barrel importer resolves the wrong `DeliverableStatus`, where a new `ActivityType` is minted freehand with no definition, or where a duplicate type name drifts apart across two files. New collisions are now caught in CI (and nightly `pr-check --all`) instead of shipping. The word classes encode a rename policy the compiler can't: never rename a Stripe/GBP/Webflow word, never rename a persisted activity-log value.

**Why it matters to clients:** Indirect but foundational — a governed vocabulary is what lets the platform's data/type layer stop moving under the P2 client-UI rebuild. Fewer silent data-shape mismatches means fewer client-visible regressions when the redesign lands.

---

### 540. Admin MCP API key management (Settings → MCP API Keys)

**What it does:** Adds the admin surface for the per-workspace MCP API keys whose store + scope enforcement shipped in PR #1411. A new section in global Settings (`McpApiKeysSettings.tsx`) lists every per-workspace key across all workspaces (workspace name, label, created / last-used, Active/Revoked status) and shows whether the env `MCP_API_KEY` master key is configured. The operator mints a key by picking a workspace + label; the plaintext is revealed **exactly once** in a copy-to-clipboard callout (only a sha256 hash is stored — a lost key is rotated, not recovered) and revokes a key via a confirmation dialog. Backed by a global admin route (`server/routes/mcp-api-keys.ts`, HMAC-only `requireAdminAuth` — JWT client users are fully excluded) with GET (list — metadata only, never the hash/plaintext), POST (mint), DELETE (revoke; 409 if already revoked, 404 unknown). Mint/revoke are recorded as two admin-only activity types (`mcp_key_created` / `mcp_key_revoked`, deliberately kept out of `CLIENT_VISIBLE_TYPES`). From the 2026-06-26 MCP surface audit follow-up (`mcp-admin-key-minting-ui`).

**Why it matters to the agency:** Until now the per-workspace keys could only be minted in code (the all-workspace master key was the only key in real use). The agency can now hand a scoped, revocable key to any MCP client (a teammate's Claude desktop, an automation) that may act on exactly one workspace — and rotate or kill it instantly from the dashboard if it leaks — without ever exposing the master key. One-time reveal + hash-only storage means the platform never holds a recoverable secret.

**Why it matters to clients:** Indirect but real — a leaked or over-broad agency credential is a cross-tenant data risk. Per-workspace scoping plus instant revocation keeps any key an operator issues blast-radius-limited to a single client's workspace, and the activity log records who minted/revoked it.

---

### 539. MCP content-loop closure (`advance_content_status` + `publish_post`)

**What it does:** Adds the two MCP tools that close the content production loop — the third stateless agent loop (after analytics and inbox). `advance_content_status(workspaceId, requestId, status)` moves a content request through the operator workflow (`in_progress` → `delivered`) via the transition-validated `updateContentRequest`. `publish_post(workspaceId, postId)` publishes a post to the client's **live Webflow site** via the shared `publishPostToWebflow` service (the same path the operator UI uses — it owns the field map, `CONTENT_PUBLISHED` broadcast, activity log, and outcome tracking). Both tag their activity `source: 'mcp-chat'`. From the 2026-06-26 MCP surface audit (P1). Before this, an agent could draft and send content but could never take it past `client_review` or publish it.

**The trust guardrails (owner decisions):** `publish_post` is **approved-only** — it refuses unless the post is status `approved`, so an autonomous agent can never push an un-reviewed draft to a client's live site (even though the underlying service permits draft/review for an admin override). And `advance_content_status` is restricted to the two operator-workflow states that have no client-facing side effects — it deliberately **cannot** set `client_review`/`post_review` (those email the client and go through `send_to_client`), `approved`/`changes_requested` (the client's own decisions, made in their portal), or `published` (that's `publish_post`). So the agent drives production forward without impersonating client decisions or sending surprise client emails.

**Why it matters to the agency:** Completes the autonomous content pipeline — an operator driving Claude can now take content all the way from research → brief → post → review → **published**, with the irreversible live-publish step gated to reviewed-only content. Combined with the analytics and inbox loops (also closed), the agent can run a workspace end-to-end rather than handing off at each terminal step.

**Why it matters to clients:** The client's review authority and live site are protected: only content the client (or operator) has approved can be published by an agent, and the agent can never make the client's approve/decline decision for them. Faster delivery without surrendering the approval gate.

**What it does:** Adds two MCP write tools that let a Claude agent *act on* the pending work `get_pending_work` surfaces, instead of only reading it. `respond_to_client_action(workspaceId, actionId, status, clientNote?)` updates a client action's status (completed / archived / approved / changes_requested / pending) — routed through the admin `updateAdminClientAction` service so it validates the transition, logs activity, broadcasts, and feeds the insight + outcome learning loop. `respond_to_approval_item(workspaceId, batchId, itemId, clientNote?)` is **decline-only by design**: it always requests changes (status `rejected`) on an approval item and attributes the action honestly to the agent (`actor: 'MCP agent'`), notifying the team. The agent **cannot** approve approval items on the client's behalf — approval is the client's own review decision (and triggers "approved" client emails), so that path is deliberately withheld. From the 2026-06-26 MCP surface audit (P0).

**Why it matters to the agency:** Closes the second of the two stateless agent loops (the first being analytics insights). An operator driving Claude can now sweep the inbox — mark client actions done/archived, request changes on proposed SEO items — so the queue actually shrinks instead of re-surfacing every pass. The decline-only guardrail on approvals is the safety design: autonomous agents can triage and push back, but the irreversible, client-facing "approve" decision stays with a human, preventing an agent from silently auto-approving live-site changes on a client's behalf.

**Why it matters to clients:** The client's review authority is protected — an agent can never approve changes *as* the client, only request revisions (clearly attributed to the agency's agent). Combined with faster operator follow-through on the inbox, clients get more responsive service without surrendering their approval gate.

---

### 537. MCP analytics-loop closure (`resolve_insight` + `bulk_resolve_insights`)

**What it does:** Adds two MCP write tools — `resolve_insight(workspaceId, insightId, status, note?)` and `bulk_resolve_insights(workspaceId, insightIds[], status, note?)` — that let a Claude agent mark analytics insights `resolved` or `in_progress` after acting on them. Before this, the MCP analytics surface was read-only: an agent could see `get_unresolved_insights` but never close the loop, so the same queue re-surfaced every pass with no way to reach a terminal state (the audit's "stateless analytics loop"). Both tools wrap the existing `resolveInsight` store function, tag the resolution `source: 'mcp-chat'`, fire the `INSIGHT_RESOLVED` broadcast + activity log, and — on `resolved` — record an outcome-learning baseline snapshot via a newly-extracted shared helper (`recordInsightResolutionOutcome` in `server/outcome-tracking.ts`) that the admin resolve route now also uses, so agent-driven and operator-driven resolutions feed the learning system identically. From the 2026-06-26 MCP surface audit (P0).

**Why it matters to the agency:** Closes the highest-value gap for autonomous operation — an agent triaging analytics can now actually *clear* what it has handled instead of re-reporting it, and (via `bulk_*`) sweep a batch at once. Because resolutions are tagged `mcp-chat` and recorded for outcome learning, chat-driven triage is fully auditable and contributes to the "did this action improve metrics?" measurement loop rather than being invisible to it.

**Why it matters to clients:** Faster, more consistent follow-through on the issues the platform surfaces — work an agent resolves is tracked and measured the same as work an operator resolves, so outcome reporting stays complete regardless of who did the closing.

**What it does:** Adds a dedicated admin **Local Presence** page at `/ws/:workspaceId/local-seo` under the visible **Strategy** nav group. The page re-homes the workspace-level local SEO operating surface into four tabs: Overview (posture, markets, freshness, GBP review aggregates), Visibility (the existing local visibility workflow), Reviews (the existing `local-gbp` aggregate reviews panel), and Setup (entry points back to existing Business Footprint location management). Keyword Hub now shows a compact Local Presence handoff while preserving keyword-level local annotations, filters, row data, and drawer evidence. This is an IA foundation only: no GBP OAuth, raw review sync, reply publishing, provider expansion, tables, migrations, jobs, or new Google scopes.

**Why it matters to the agency:** Gives operators a clean home for local market visibility and GBP aggregate review context without turning Keyword Hub into a second workspace settings page. It also fixes the previously-dead MCP local SEO job link to `/local-seo`.

**Why it matters to clients:** No direct client-facing change in Phase 1; it prepares a clearer admin workflow for future authenticated GBP connection, review storage, and approval-based review-response automation.

---

### 535. Brand intelligence slice (P1 read-only foundation + P2 MCP wiring + P3 agent read tool + P5 agent write tool + P4 invariant lock)

**What it does:** Registers a new `brand` workspace-intelligence slice (`BrandSlice` in `shared/types/intelligence.ts`, assembled by `server/intelligence/brand-slice.ts`) that unifies the brand's authority-resolved voice block and approved brand-identity deliverables (mission, vision, values, tagline, elevator pitch, positioning) into one structured, prompt-ready shape retrievable via `buildWorkspaceIntelligence(ws, { slices: ['brand'] })`. **P1** is deliberately **dark/behavior-neutral**: the slice is registered and assembled-on-request only — it is NOT added to `PROMPT_FORMATTABLE_INTELLIGENCE_SLICES`, has no `formatters.ts` case, and is in no default `baseSlices` set, so no generated prompt or content changes (mirroring the precedent `generationQuality` slice). Voice is read by importing the unchanged `buildEffectiveBrandVoiceBlock` (`seo-context-source.ts`); identity is read through a newly-extracted pure read-model (`server/brand-deliverable-read-model.ts`) so the slice imports only leaf modules and never `brand-identity.ts` — keeping the zero circular-dependency ratchet intact. A cycle-kill boundary test guards the leaf-import contract.

**What P2 adds (MCP wiring — the payoff):** The slice gains a Layer-2 `voiceDnaBlock` (voice DNA + guardrails, populated **only** for calibrated profiles — non-calibrated already carry their DNA inside `voicePromptBlock`, so emitting it here would double-inject). The two render functions (`voiceDNAToPromptInstructions`, `guardrailsToPromptInstructions`) moved verbatim from `prompt-assembly.ts` into a new cycle-safe types-only leaf `server/voice-dna-layer2.ts` (single source of truth; `prompt-assembly` re-exports them, `buildSystemPrompt` output byte-identical). The MCP content path — `prepare_brief_context` / `prepare_post_context` (`server/mcp/tools/content-actions.ts`) — now fetches the brand slice separately and appends **only** `voiceDnaBlock` + `identityPromptBlock` to `prompt_context` (the Layer-1 `voicePromptBlock` already lives inside `context.promptContext` via `seoContext` — appending it again would double-voice), plus surfaces structured `brand_identity` (object|null) + `voice_status` payload keys so an agent can selectively emphasize identity fields per page type. P2 deliberately does **not** touch `baseSlices`/`PROMPT_FORMATTABLE`/`formatters.ts`: server-side generation already gets full voice via `buildSystemPrompt`; only the external MCP/agent path was brand-blind.

**What P3 adds (agent read tool):** A new read-only MCP tool `get_brand_identity(workspaceId, includeDeliverables?)` in a domain-scoped `server/mcp/tools/brand.ts` (mirrors `tools/intelligence.ts`/`tools/content.ts`). It reads the same `brand` slice via `buildWorkspaceIntelligence(ws, { slices: ['brand'] })` and returns an identity-focused payload `{ availability, identity (BrandSlice.identity verbatim — no re-derivation), voice_status, identity_prompt_block }`; with `includeDeliverables: true` it also returns the full deliverable list (draft + approved, all types) via the P1 leaf `brand-deliverable-read-model.listDeliverables` for deeper admin inspection. It deliberately does **not** expose `voicePromptBlock`/`voiceDnaBlock` — voice content is delivered through the P2 content path; this tool is identity-scoped. Pure DB read: no paid-call counter, no broadcast, no activity log, no feature flag, no HTTP route — admin/MCP-auth gated (same posture as `get_workspace_intelligence`; never exposed to a client-facing endpoint). Lockstep-registered in `ALL_TOOLS`, the server dispatch branch, and the contract-test/routing-test tool sets so a missing registration fails typecheck or a contract test.

**What P5 adds (agent write tool):** A new MCP write tool `update_brand_deliverable(workspaceId, deliverableId, content, expectedVersion?)` in `server/mcp/tools/brand.ts`, completing the read **+ write** loop of the original ask. It wraps the existing transactional `updateDeliverableContent` (`server/brand-identity.ts`): snapshots the prior version, bumps `version`, resets `status → 'draft'`. Mirroring the established MCP-write safety pattern of `update_brief`, it accepts an optional `expectedVersion` optimistic-concurrency guard (pass the `version` from `get_brand_identity(includeDeliverables:true)`; a mismatch is rejected as a conflict so a blind agent can't clobber a concurrent human edit). It enforces the full data-flow contract on a real change — `addActivity('brand_deliverable_refined', { source: 'mcp-chat', … })` + `broadcastToWorkspace(BRAND_IDENTITY_UPDATED)` + `invalidateIntelligenceCache` — and **suppresses** those side effects on a no-op (byte-identical content) so the activity log never shows a phantom "Edited" entry. Update-only (a missing deliverable returns a clear not-found; creation stays out of scope since deliverables are AI-generated). Workspace-scoped (cross-workspace writes return not-found), admin/MCP-auth gated, no HTTP route, no feature flag. Auto-routed via `brandTools.some(...)` dispatch (no `server.ts` change); a real-DB unit test (`tests/unit/mcp-brand-write.test.ts`) covers success / no-op / conflict / not-found / cross-workspace isolation / invalid-args / unknown-tool.

**What P4 adds (invariant lock — Option A):** The original P4 plan ("retire the legacy `effectiveBrandVoiceBlock` field") was reframed after grounding: that field is **not** a vestigial shim — it is load-bearing for the prompt formatter (`formatSeoContextSection` receives only the seoContext slice), and its value already comes from the **same** `buildEffectiveBrandVoiceBlock()` function as `brand.voicePromptBlock`, so the single source of truth already exists at the function level. A physical merge would force `brand` into `baseSlices` (assembling brand identity on every prompt build) and refactor the most AI-sensitive code for zero correctness gain. Instead, P4 **locks the invariant**: a behavior-neutral contract test (`tests/contract/voice-block-slice-parity.test.ts`) asserts the two slice fields stay byte-identical across voice states (legacy-present and empty), backed by cross-reference JSDoc on both `SeoContextSlice.effectiveBrandVoiceBlock` and `BrandSlice.voicePromptBlock` and producer-site comments so no future agent "consolidates" the apparent duplication into a silent voice regression. No formatter, `baseSlices`, or consumer changes.

**Why it matters to the agency:** Gives downstream AI/recommendation surfaces a single, authority-correct source for brand voice + identity instead of each consumer re-deriving it. After P2, agents (and scheduled fleets) generating content through the MCP receive the brand's distilled voice DNA + mission/values — closing the verified gap where MCP-generated content was close to brand-blind — without any risk to the server-side generation path. P3 lets an agent (or operator) *inspect* a workspace's structured brand identity and deliverable status directly before content work; P5 lets an agent *revise* brand deliverables (e.g. tighten a mission statement, refresh values) directly via the MCP with optimistic-concurrency safety — the read+write loop the brand-slice effort set out to deliver.

**Why it matters to clients:** More on-brand AI output (copy, briefs, posts) when content is produced through the agent/MCP path, because the brand's calibrated voice rules and identity now reach the generator instead of being dropped.

---

### 534. Client diagnostic root-cause cards

**What it does:** Surfaces completed deep diagnostic reports in the client Overview as plain-language "what changed" cards. The cards use the report's client-safe summary, top root-cause titles, affected page, and remediation priorities while excluding admin reports, raw diagnostic context, evidence arrays, provider internals, and failed/running diagnostics.

**Why it matters to the agency:** Turns a hidden admin investigation into a client-visible explanation, making it easier to answer "why did traffic drop?" without manually rewriting diagnostic findings.

**Why it matters to clients:** Replaces vague traffic anxiety with a concise explanation and a clear path back to the Health tab for next steps.

---

### 533. AI-visibility / LLM citation tracking (SEO Decision Engine P8 — FINAL)

**What it does:** Closes the AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) loop with **measurement** — it answers *"are we actually cited by LLMs?"*, the payoff metric for all the answer-first content + author/E-E-A-T work the platform already produces. The retired `ai-visibility` product flag is no longer a runtime gate; Growth/Premium tier and provider gates remain. It queries DataForSEO's LLM-mentions database for the client's domain and computes an **AI-visibility KPI**: the headline **share-of-voice** (the client's brand vs the competitor brands LLM answers co-mention in their category — a like-to-like comparison, distinct from raw citation volume), the **mention volume** (how often the client's content is cited in AI answers), a **mentions-over-time trend** (the before/after proof of the AEO work), the **co-mentioned competitor** breakdown, and the **source domains** LLM answers pull from when citing the client (the off-site AEO targeting list). A `llm-mentions-refresh` background job (ported from the P6 national-SERP job — cancel-safe, observe-only budget) writes a dated `llm_mention_snapshots` row each run; the trend accrues over time. The data surfaces as an admin KPI panel (share-of-voice MetricRing + mention StatCard + trend sparkline + competitor/source breakdowns) on the Keyword Hub, in the rebuilt shell's dedicated AI Visibility home, and into the workspace intelligence (`SeoContextSlice.aiVisibility`) so the AI advisor sees it. The deliverable is **measurement only** — no new recommendation/insight; the existing `aeo-*` recs remain the actions. Aggregates only — the platform never captures raw LLM answer text. Where the client's brand can't be identified among the co-mentioned set, share-of-voice reads **"not measured"** rather than a misleading 0%. The **eighth and final phase** of the SEO Decision Engine program.

**Why it matters to the agency:** Turns "we did AEO work" into a defensible, trending KPI — *"your AI share-of-voice went from 15% to 38% since we started, and here are the source sites getting you cited"* — which is the single most future-facing thing an SEO agency can show right now. It also makes the AEO recommendations the platform already generates provably worth doing.

**Why it matters to clients:** Answers the question every business is suddenly asking — *"do ChatGPT and AI answers ever recommend us?"* — with a real number, a trend, and the specific competitors winning the AI conversation, instead of a hand-wave.

---

### 532. GBP + reviews local layer (SEO Decision Engine P7)

**What it does:** Turns local SEO from local-pack-presence-only into a real local product by capturing Google Business Profile health + review counts/ratings — the client's own and local competitors' — and surfacing **review-gap** and **GBP-completeness** findings as actionable recommendations. Behind the `local-gbp` flag (Growth + Premium). Two data paths (validated against live DataForSEO calls): a **free** half that extracts each business's star rating + review count from the `local_pack` results the local refresh *already* fetches (the parser previously dropped them — zero new cost, like P3's AI-Overview extraction), and a **paid** half (`business_data/business_listings_search`) that pulls the full category review landscape sorted by review count + the client's own GBP profile completeness. A separate `local-gbp-refresh` background job (ported from the P6 national-SERP job — concurrency-1, memory-headroom-gated, cancel-safe incremental persistence) runs a category search for competitors plus a **business-name search for the client's own listing** (who is often too review-poor to appear in a review-sorted search — that *is* the gap), stores both in a new `business_listing_snapshots` time-series table (own vs competitor distinguished by `is_owned`), and the recommendation engine then mints `local_visibility`-type recs: a review-gap rec (*"close the gap: 12 reviews / 3.9★ vs the local leader's 80 / 4.6★"*) and a GBP-completeness rec (*"complete your profile — missing photos / attributes / category"*). The findings flow through the existing curate→send→greenlight→outcome recommendation spine and feed the workspace intelligence (`LocalSeoSlice.reviewSummary`) so the AI advisor sees them. Aggregates only — never individual reviews or author data. Seventh phase of the SEO Decision Engine program; `local-gbp` OFF leaves the paid surface byte-identical.

**Why it matters to the agency:** Answers the local client's most visceral question — *"why are my competitors getting all the calls?"* — with side-by-side review evidence and a concrete, sellable action (a review-generation program + GBP cleanup), not a vague "improve your reviews." The cost is trivial (~weekly, per market) and the free half adds review-count trends for the local pack at no cost.

**Why it matters to clients:** Makes the invisible visible — they can see exactly how their reviews and Google Business Profile stack up against the specific local competitors winning the map pack, and get a clear plan to close the gap.

---

### 531. National SERP rank + AI-Overview citation tracking (SEO Decision Engine P6)

**What it does:** The first *paid* phase of the program — true national SERP position + SERP-feature tracking per keyword, behind the `national-serp-tracking` flag (Growth + Premium only). A new background job (`runNationalSerpRefreshJob`, ported from the local-SEO refresher: platform-wide concurrency-1, memory-headroom-gated, cancel-safe with incremental persistence, 100-keyword cap) calls DataForSEO `serp/google/organic/live/advanced` per tracked keyword and parses — against a **live-validated ground-truth fixture**, not guessed field names — the client's true organic rank (`rank_group` among `organic` items), the ranking URL, the present SERP features, and crucially whether the client's domain is **cited in the AI Overview** (owner domain ∈ the aggregated `ai_overview.references[].domain`). Results land in a new `serp_snapshots` time-series table (migration 153), kept strictly **parallel** to the GSC `rank_snapshots` table — the admin keyword drawer now shows the live-SERP rank, ranking URL, SERP-feature badges, and an **AI-Overview citation badge** (emerald = cited / zinc = not cited, never purple) *alongside* the GSC average position, never overwriting it. When an AI Overview answers a query the client ranks for but does **not** cite them, a `serp_feature_opportunity` insight fires (ownership-guarded — it never tells a client to "capture" a result they already hold) and reaches the client portal as a plain-language narrative ("Google's AI Overview is answering *X* without citing you — we're optimizing the page to earn that citation"). The route is gated flag → Growth+ tier → observe-only credit budget (P5) → global + per-workspace job serialization; OFF is byte-identical to pre-P6. Sixth phase of the SEO Decision Engine program (first Group C paid layer). Featured-snippet *capture* and historical backfill are deferred (P7+).

**Why it matters to the agency:** Turns "we fetched the SERP" into a sellable, defensible answer to the #1 question clients now ask — *"are we showing up in Google's AI answers?"* — with per-keyword evidence (cited vs not, ranking URL, position) instead of a hand-wave. The cost is trivial ($0.002–0.003/call, <0.2% of the Growth budget at 100 keywords weekly) but the perceived value is high, and the observe-only budget wiring means the paid layer can scale to enforcement with a one-line flip.

**Why it matters to clients:** Answers the AI-search anxiety directly — they can see, keyword by keyword, where they rank in the real SERP and whether Google's AI Overview is citing them or a competitor, with a clear "here's what we're doing about it" narrative rather than a raw metric.

---

### 530. Provider reliability + cost governance (SEO Decision Engine P5)

**What it does:** Hardens the DataForSEO integration before paid Group C phases. Three reliability/cost mechanisms: (1) **Bounded retry-with-backoff** in `server/external-fetch.ts` — a transient `429`/`500`/`502`/`503`/`504` or network error is retried (exponential backoff + jitter, 2 retries by default on `fetchProviderJson`) instead of collapsing to an empty result; permanent errors (`402` credit-exhausted, `401`, other 4xx, timeouts) fail fast. (2) A **capability circuit breaker** — a `40204` "no subscription" error on backlinks now trips an in-memory breaker for 6h so `getBacklinksProvider()` short-circuits to null and callers degrade the optional backlink fields, instead of re-hitting the unsubscribed (paid) endpoint on every request. (3) A **per-tier credit-budget gate** (`server/credit-budget-gate.ts`): `CREDIT_BUDGETS` (free 0 · growth 2,000 · premium unlimited) + `evaluateCreditBudget()` + the `assertCreditBudget()` cross-phase contract that P6–P8 call before each paid fetch. Launched **observe-only** (owner decision): the gate computes and logs would-blocks and drives the DataForSEO health-card quota status (ok/warning/critical) but never blocks a call — flipping one constant turns on hard enforcement (429s + job messages). Also surfaces the previously **served-but-dark** DataForSEO credit usage in `AIUsageSection` (credits, calls, cache-hit rate, daily trend). Fifth phase of the SEO Decision Engine program (Group B foundation; no feature flag — reliability/cost infrastructure).

**Agency value:** Transient provider blips no longer silently produce empty keyword/SERP data (the #1 "fetched-and-discarded" failure mode); unsubscribed endpoints stop quietly burning the request budget; and per-workspace credit consumption is finally visible (health card + usage section) with a margin-protection gate ready to enforce once budgets are validated.

**Client value:** More reliable research data (fewer silently-empty results) and the foundation for cost-sustainable paid features (national SERP, GBP, AI-visibility) without runaway provider spend.

### 529. Geo-correct domain research — national/international SERP targeting (SEO Decision Engine P4)

**What it does:** Fixes a silent correctness bug where every domain/competitor/keyword-gap research call was hardcoded to the US SERP (`location_code` 2840) in English, corrupting results for non-US clients. The six DataForSEO domain-analysis methods (`getDomainKeywords`, `getUrlKeywords`, `getDomainOverview`, `getCompetitors`, `getKeywordGap`, `getKeywordsForKeywords`) now accept and thread `locationCode` + `languageCode`. A new workspace `targetGeo` field (`{ locationCode, languageCode, countryCode?, label? }`; migration 152) stores the admin-set national/international SERP target, resolved by `resolveWorkspaceTargetGeo()` (admin target-geo → local primary-market location/language → US/English fallback). The 12 production caller sites (keyword-strategy build, analytics-intelligence insights, recommendations, blueprint-generator, competitor crons, competitive-intel routes) thread geo through a single flag-gated `workspaceProviderGeo(workspaceId)` helper — competitor-domain calls use the **client's** market (apples-to-apples), backlinks calls are geo-agnostic and excluded. Admins set the target via a new **Search Target Geo** editor in BrandHub › Business Footprint (curated DataForSEO country list, country + language pickers), distinct from the local "Primary market". Gated behind the `geo-targeting` flag: OFF is byte-identical to pre-P4 (request body AND the 7–14 day domain cache key stay on the legacy un-versioned `database` token, so no costly cache re-warm); ON keys the cache on `v2:<locationCode>:<language>` so e.g. Canada-EN and Canada-FR can't poison each other. Fourth phase of the SEO Decision Engine program (Group B foundation).

**Agency value:** Non-US and non-English clients finally get research against their real SERP instead of silently-wrong US/English data — the single biggest fix for the audit's D-grade "national/international" dimension. One admin picker controls the target; everything downstream (keywords, competitors, gaps, domain authority, blueprints, insights) follows.

**Client value:** Keyword opportunities, competitor sets, and gap analysis reflect the country and language the client actually competes in, so recommendations and content briefs are grounded in the correct market.

### 528. AI-Overview SERP-feature surfacing (SEO Decision Engine P3)

**What it does:** Surfaces the `ai_overview` SERP-feature signal the platform ALREADY fetches (DataForSEO `ranked_keywords` returns `serp_item_types` per keyword, which includes `ai_overview` as a string label) but previously dropped. `hasSerpOpportunity` (`server/seo-provider-signals.ts`) now recognizes `ai_overview`; it flows into the stored per-page/per-gap `serpFeatures` lists (keyword-strategy-enrichment, both build sites), the `SerpFeatures` intelligence type + workspace aggregation (`seo-context-slice`) + the AI-context formatter, the client-facing `serpOpportunities` count, a new content-brief **AI Overview AEO directive** ("lead with a citable, extractable answer…"), and the SERP-feature chips on admin Strategy + briefing surfaces (`ContentGapRow` descriptive/plain/emoji maps + ordered set). Third phase of the SEO Decision Engine program (the audit's move #2 "free" half). **Zero new endpoint cost** — pure extraction of data already on hand. This is the AI-Overview *trigger* signal ("this query shows an AI Overview → write extractable content"), NOT citation tracking ("are WE cited") — that needs the paid national advanced-SERP fetch deferred to P6.

**Agency value:** Content briefs for AI-Overview keywords now carry explicit AEO guidance, and admin Strategy/keyword surfaces flag which targets trigger an AI Overview — closing a dark-output gap where the signal was fetched and discarded.

**Client value:** The client `serpOpportunities` count and Strategy content-opportunity chips now include AI-Overview triggers, and briefs produce answer-engine-optimized content for those keywords.

**Mutual:** Pure activation of already-fetched data — no new provider calls; the chip/formatter/count render surfaces already existed and just needed `ai_overview` enumerated in.

**Tests:** `tests/unit/seo-provider-signals.test.ts` (ai_overview recognition), `tests/unit/seo-context-slice.test.ts` (aggregation counts ai_overview), fixture `tests/fixtures/rich-intelligence.ts` extended. typecheck/build/pr-check green; 363 SerpFeatures-dependent tests pass.

**Files:** `server/seo-provider-signals.ts`, `server/keyword-strategy-enrichment.ts` (2 build sites), `shared/types/intelligence.ts` (`SerpFeatures.aiOverview`), `server/intelligence/seo-context-slice.ts`, `server/intelligence/formatters.ts`, `server/routes/client-intelligence.ts` (`countSerpOpportunities`), `server/content-brief.ts` (AEO directive), `src/components/shared/ContentGapRow.tsx` (chip maps + order). Spec `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-23-seo-decision-engine-design.md`.

### 527. Recommendation effort priors — measured time-to-implement (SEO Decision Engine P2)

**What it does:** Threads each workspace's MEASURED median time-to-implement per action type (`getEffortPriorDays`, from the weekly EMV-calibration cron) into the Opportunity Value scorer's `effortDays` at all 16 `computeOpportunityValue` call sites in `server/recommendations.ts`, via a per-cycle `effortDaysFor(type, source)` helper (resolved through `recommendationOutcomeActionType`). The OV ROI divisor (`roiPerEffortDay`) now reflects how long that kind of action ACTUALLY takes this workspace, instead of the hardcoded per-branch `DEFAULT_EFFORT_DAYS` guesses (quick_win 1d, content_gap 5d, …). Inert until ≥ `MIN_EFFORT_SAMPLES` (3) completed outcomes of an action type accrue — below that, `getEffortPriorDays` returns nothing and the scorer falls back to the branch default, byte-identical to before. Second phase of the SEO Decision Engine program (the audit's move #4 "close the calibration loop"). **The realized-vs-predicted calibration-BASIS swap (the other half of move #4) was deliberately DEFERRED** to the GA4 revenue-grounding work: `ov-calibration.ts` documents that the basis flip is gated on GA4 `estimatedRevenue` (today `attributed_value` is a CPC proxy), and the program already deferred GA4 grounding — so the basis flip rides with that item rather than shipping on a proxy basis (owner decision, 2026-06-24).

**Agency value:** Recommendation prioritization self-corrects on REAL effort — a workspace whose technical fixes actually take 10 days stops having them ranked as if they were 0.5-day quick wins. Better ROI ordering with no new data and no client-visible $ change (predictedEmv is unaffected — effort only moves the divisor).

**Client value:** Indirect — the client's recommendation queue is ordered by more honest effort/ROI, surfacing the genuinely high-leverage work first.

**Mutual:** Pure activation of dead-but-built infrastructure (`getEffortPriorDays` had zero non-test callers); the weekly cron already computes the priors, the OV scorer already has the `input.effortDays` override seam, and the change is inert until outcomes accrue.

**Tests:** `tests/integration/recommendations-effort-priors.test.ts` (NEW) — A/B through the real `generateRecommendations` pipeline: a measured 12-day `audit_fix_applied` prior lowers the diagnostic rec's `roiPerEffortDay` vs the 2-day branch default. Full typecheck/pr-check green; 70 adjacent rec/scoring tests pass.

**Files:** `server/recommendations.ts` (the 16-site threading + the `effortDaysFor` helper + `getEffortPriorDays` import), `server/scoring/opportunity-value.ts` (DEFAULT_EFFORT_DAYS comments), `tests/integration/recommendations-effort-priors.test.ts`, `data/roadmap.json`. Spec `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-23-seo-decision-engine-design.md`.

### 526. Keyword value scoring — promoted to default (SEO Decision Engine P1; `keyword-value-scoring` flag retired)

**What it does:** Makes the value-first keyword opportunity score the **only** Hub sort path and retires the `keyword-value-scoring` feature flag (Phases 0–2 had shipped it dark behind that flag). The admin Keyword Hub list + drawers and the client Strategy keyword/content lists now rank by the commercial-value score (`computeKeywordValueScore`: commercial-intent × CPC × demand × winnability × posture-driven local multiplier) instead of the crude `volume×0.45 + ease×0.45` blend, and the human-readable `valueReasons` chips ("Transactional intent · $9 CPC", "Winnable · KD 24", "Local boost ×1.5") render unconditionally (raw `$X CPC` text stays admin-only via the preserved `exposeCpc` gate). The crude `computeOpportunityScore` Hub-sort accessor branches were deleted; the function is kept only as the value-first signal-gate fallback and the content-gap backfill + briefing/public-projection basis. First phase of the **SEO Decision Engine** program (the 2026-06-23 15-agent SEO audit's #1 "flip the loops already built" move). Coordinated retirement: flag removed from `FEATURE_FLAGS`/catalog/groups; 7 test files updated (flag-OFF cases dropped, former flag-ON cases unconditionalized); a pr-check anti-reintroduction guard added (the `keyword-value-scoring` key joins the Retired Keyword Hub flag rule); the deprecation registry advanced `hidden → removed`; and the previously-undocumented `keyword-universe-full` dark-launch added as the registry's `hidden` exemplar.

**Agency value:** Money keywords now surface above low-value informational ones for every workspace by default — the single highest-leverage "maximize SEO value" change, with zero new endpoint cost (the scorer was already built and tested, just dark).

**Client value:** Client Strategy surfaces lead with the keywords that actually drive revenue, explained in plain `valueReasons` language.

**Mutual:** Pure activation of existing infrastructure — no new data, no new UI components (the re-rank + reasons render through the existing admin Hub and client Strategy drawers).

**Files:** `server/keyword-command-center.ts` (collapse dual sort accessors → value-first), `server/keyword-strategy-enrichment.ts` + `server/keyword-strategy-ux.ts` (unconditionalize), `shared/types/feature-flags.ts` (flag removed), `scripts/pr-check.ts` + `docs/rules/automated-rules.md` (anti-reintroduction guard), `scripts/deprecation-lifecycle.ts` (registry advance), 7 test files, `docs/rules/keyword-hub.md`. Spec `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-23-seo-decision-engine-design.md`; audit `docs/superpowers/audits/2026-06-23-seo-engine-p1-value-scoring-audit.md`; plan `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-23-seo-engine-p1-value-scoring.md`.

### 525. The Issue — curation cockpit + curated-POV engine + closed send-loop + V2 client feed + weekly push cron + trust ladder + four-jobs lenses + competitor page (Phase 0–6, behind `strategy-the-issue`, WIP)

**What it does:** First two phases of the Strategy "reimagination" — ONE curated recommendation artifact that becomes both the admin curation cockpit and (Phase 2+) the V2 client dashboard feed. **Phase 0 (shared contracts):** the `strategy-the-issue` master flag (composes `commandCenterEnabled && flag`); an exhaustive `Record<RecType,Archetype>` 6-bucket map (`shared/types/strategy-archetype.ts`); `isCuratedForClient` (the client-seen set `clientStatus∈{sent,approved,discussing}` minus struck — deliberately OVERLAPS `isActiveRec` on `discussing`); the `StrategyPov` type + `strategy-pov` AI operation + `admin.strategyPov` query key. **Phase 1 (admin cockpit):** a system-drafted **point-of-view engine** (`strategy-pov-generator` clones the meeting-brief engine but re-points the prompt at the CURATED set via `isCuratedForClient`; content-hash cached on curated-rec content/order + variant + regenerate-nonce so operator edits survive a plain regenerate; admin/client variants) with a store + routes + `STRATEGY_POV_GENERATED` broadcast; an editable **drafted-POV editor** (cut a backing card → its sentence leaves the POV); an archetype **stance bar** ("4 bets · 3 refresh · 2 defend · …" with create/refresh/defend counts); an archetype-grouped **backing-moves queue** reusing the existing keep/cut/park/send single-writer verbs + atomic bulk-apply; and the long-missed **cannibalization operator keeper-override** (survives regen via a `cannibalizationUrlSetKey`-keyed table, wired into the read path so the override actually changes the keeper). Mounted as a third orchestrator branch in `KeywordStrategy.tsx`; **flag-OFF is byte-identical** to the command-center cockpit. **Phase 2 (close the loop + client money-surface):** the long-broken #12c keystone is closed — operator Send now mirrors each rec into the unified deliverable spine (a new `recommendation` deliverable type + adapter) so it actually reaches the client, and a new public **act-on** route turns the client's "Act on this" into a durable **content REQUEST** (`clientStatus → approved` + a `content_topic_requests` row carrying the rec lineage; **NOTHING is generated** — the operator creates the brief later) with greenlight → `TrackedAction` attribution flowing back. The **evergreen V2 client money-surface** (`TheIssueClientPage`, branched into `OverviewTab`) leads with a content-led plan + an ROI "what your SEO is worth" band + a "what's working" proof strip + the loop footer, in plain evergreen language (no dates/"this week"). The restricted client-facing `clientStatus`/`delivered` projection is **per-workspace flag-gated**, so every non-Issue client's public read stays byte-identical (verified by the pre-existing leak tests). **Phase 3 (weekly push cadence + operator doorbell):** a flag-gated hourly cron (`strategy-issue-cron`) pre-bakes the admin-variant POV once per ISO week per eligible workspace (flag ON AND ≥1 **active** rec — the same `isActiveRec` set the admin POV now drafts over), stamps `workspaces.last_issue_pushed_week_of` for cross-process idempotency (a per-workspace single-flight mutex prevents a tick racing a future "push now"), and rings the **operator doorbell** by reusing the EXISTING admin notification rail. The doorbell is server-authoritative: `issue.ready` lights ONLY when the flag is ON, there is ≥1 active rec, `pushedWeekOf === currentWeekOfUTC()`, AND the operator has not yet acted (POV `editedAt` within the current ISO week) — so the bell appears the week the Issue is pushed and CLEARS once the operator edits that week's POV, instead of ringing forever. The admin POV rec-set is **variant-aware** — admin drafts over the active/proposable set (consistent with the cut→sentence contract and the cron's eligibility), client over the curated/sent set. **Phase 4 (trust ladder):** per-archetype auto-send for the **two low-risk buckets only** (`quick_win`, `technical` — the non-monetizable archetypes; content/authority, refresh, defend/cannibalization, and local are never auto-sent). The operator **earns** auto-send for a bucket by manually greenlighting it for **3 consecutive ISO-week cycles**; the streak is **latched-once-earned** (a quiet week can't revoke earned trust, but building the streak requires three back-to-back weeks). Earned + operator-enabled buckets are auto-sent by the weekly cron via the same single-writer (`sendRecommendation` + deliverable mirror), each marked `autoSent` (carried over on regen); the review window is **recall-based** (a struck auto-sent rec leaves the client's curated projection). The streak counter lives in `strategy_autosend_policy` (migration 144), credited at a single chokepoint inside `sendRecommendation` (flag-guarded no-op when off, covers manual + auto, broadcasts `STRATEGY_AUTOSEND_POLICY_UPDATED` on change). The admin **TrustLadderPanel** exposes one earned-gated teal toggle per eligible bucket with cycle progress; a `strategy_autosent` doorbell + `issue.autoSent` overview count notify the operator. **Phase 5 (four-jobs lenses):** the two remaining four-jobs projections (jobs #1/#2 shipped in Phases 1–2) as ADMIN read-projections of the curated set — **no new source of truth** (no migration, no WS event, no flag). **Job #4 keyword targets** — curated `keyword_gap` + `topic_cluster` recs surfaced as targets, each deep-linking into the existing **Keyword Hub** (`buildHubDeepLinkQuery` → `?q=`). **Job #3 content work-orders** — curated `content` + `content_refresh` recs joined (by `recommendationId`) to their `content_topic_requests` for a production **stage** (queued → in-progress → awaiting-client → … → completed), each deep-linking into the **content pipeline** (`?tab=briefs|posts`). Server projection `buildIssueLenses` + the GET `/api/workspaces/:ws/issue-lenses` route; the `useIssueLenses` hook (enabled-gated, refreshed by the existing `RECOMMENDATIONS_UPDATED` + `CONTENT_REQUEST_*` events); the `KeywordTargetsLens` + `ContentWorkOrderLens` cockpit panels. **Phase 6 (full competitor page):** promotes the existing competitor intelligence into a dedicated admin **Competitors page** (new `competitors` `Page`, kept OUT of the global nav via `NON_REGISTRY_PAGES` so flag-OFF nav is byte-identical; reached via a flag-ON deep-link from The Issue cockpit). The page **reuses the existing `StrategyCompetitiveTab` composition** (share-of-voice `ShareBar` + `CompetitiveIntel` + `KeywordGaps` + `BacklinkProfile`) and adds the genuinely net-new **`CompetitorAlertsPanel`** that finally surfaces `competitor_alerts` — the keyword-gained/lost/authority-shift rows the Monday competitor cron has always written but which had **zero UI**. Server `listCompetitorAlerts(workspaceId, limit)` + GET `/api/workspaces/:ws/competitor-alerts` (maps the store `CompetitorAlert` → a wire `CompetitorAlertView`, `insightId` not exposed); the `useCompetitorAlerts` hook refreshes on `STRATEGY_UPDATED`. The client competitor surface (premium-gated `CompetitorGapsSection`) already shipped in Phase 2. **Operator-steering follow-up batch (§11/§12):** the three deeper curation verbs the owner flagged as extremely important, all engineered to survive the weekly regen. **(1) Correct a rec's wording** — inline-edit `title`/`insight`; the override lives in `rec_operator_override` (migration 145), keyed on `recId` so it follows the rec through regen (the carry-over copies the id), with **merge semantics** (each field overridden independently) and applied ONLY at display boundaries (admin GET serialization + the flag-gated public client projection) — **never baked** into the `recommendation_sets` blob, so `loadRecommendations` stays pure and clearing restores the source wording. **(2) Add a rec the system missed** — `POST /manual-rec` mints a `manual:`-sourced rec that survives regen via a new auto-resolve **retention branch** (`isOperatorMintedRec`); the same branch **fixes a live pre-existing bug** where an un-sent `competitor:` rec silently auto-resolved to "completed" on the next regen. **(3) Reorder the client running order** — `sort_order` in the same override table; the public client feed orders by it (flag-gated); a "Client running order" up/down panel. Admin UI: the inline `CockpitRow` editor (optional prop → flag-OFF byte-identical), `AddRecommendationModal`, `ClientRunningOrder`, the `useOperatorSteering` hook. The two-axis invariant holds (the manual mint sets `clientStatus`/`lifecycle`, never `RecStatus`). **Audit-resolution launch PR (88b31d1f7):** a 10-persona advisory audit (`docs/superpowers/audits/2026-06-20-the-issue-persona-audit.md`) surfaced 6 launch-blockers, resolved via a 3-team / 5-judge tournament (`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-20-the-issue-resolution-tournament.md`, owner ratified D1 = Option A tier-aware Act-on pricing). The six fixes: **B1** — server-authoritative tier gate on the client act-on route (Free + monetizable archetype → `403 { requiredTier: 'growth' }` BEFORE the transaction) plus an `actOn` descriptor (`{ mode: 'included'|'priced'|'locked', requiredTier?, monetizable }`) attached only inside the flag-gated client projection; the client verb is "Request this" → confirm. **B2** — the V2 client surface re-sequenced so the content plan leads. **B3** — the trust-ladder auto-send dark-launched behind a NEW `strategy-trust-ladder-autosend` flag (default OFF), so the weekly cron never auto-sends in production until the owner opts in. **B4** — the admin cockpit reduced to ONE canonical 5-beat spine; **`ClientRunningOrder` and `KeywordTargetsLens` were CUT**. **B5** — ONE canonical send model: "Stage for issue" stages a LOCAL set (`useToggleSet`, no client write) and the header "Send issue" is the only commit (the prior Stage buttons were a cosmetic relabel that fired real client sends — caught as the review's Critical). **B6** — fabricated competitor rows removed. Foundation: `shared/recommendation-predicates.ts` is now the single source for `isActiveRec`/`isCuratedForClient`. Scaled adversarial review verified flag-OFF byte-identical + the billing-authority invariant INTACT; full suite (26,546 tests) green, pr-check 0 errors. A second persona audit (`docs/superpowers/audits/2026-06-21-the-issue-persona-audit-round-2.md`) re-runs the panel against the resolved surfaces.

**Agency value:** Flips the operator's job from "read ~195 recs and assemble a point of view" to "curate a finished, system-drafted POV" — the stance bar gives a 10-second read of the cycle's shape, the POV is pre-written and editable, the backing moves are archetype-grouped with fast keep/cut/park/send, and the keeper-override finally lets the operator choose which page survives a cannibalization (specced + missed three prior times).

**Client value:** Indirect this phase (admin-only, dark-launched); Phase 2 turns the curated POV + sent recs into the evergreen V2 client money-surface.

**Mutual:** Maximal reuse — the curation engine (5 single-writer verbs, `isActiveRec`, `applyLifecycleCarryOver`, atomic bulk route), the meeting-brief generator/cache pattern, the deliverable spine, and the cockpit row model are all reused; net-new is the POV fusion + two thin presentation maps + the keeper-override store. Built via a 4-lane parallel workflow + orchestrator integration, then a 6-agent scaled adversarial review with per-finding validation caught + fixed 24 issues (incl. a Rules-of-Hooks crash the parity test masked, a POV-cache overwrite of operator edits, and the keeper-override never being read); full suite (26,375 tests) green. Remote integration to staging deferred (base is the unmerged `strategy-redesign-phase-4` line).

**Files:** Server — `server/strategy-pov-{generator,store}.ts`, `server/routes/strategy-pov.ts`, `server/schemas/strategy-pov-schemas.ts`, `server/cannibalization-keeper-override.ts`, `server/routes/cannibalization-keeper.ts`, `server/cannibalization-issues.ts` (override read-merge), `server/recommendations.ts` (`isCuratedForClient`), Phase 2 — `server/domains/inbox/deliverable-adapters/recommendation.ts`, `server/domains/inbox/recommendation-dual-write.ts`, `server/recommendation-{strategy-card-context,gain-sanitizer}.ts`, `server/routes/recommendations.ts` (act-on + flag-gated projection), Phase 3 — `server/strategy-issue-cron.ts`, `server/workspaces.ts` (`markIssuePushedWeek`), `server/routes/workspaces.ts` (`issue` doorbell summary), `server/startup.ts` (cron wired), migrations 140–143, `server/{ws-events,activity-log,app}.ts`. ### 524. Strategy admin IA redesign Phase 4 — signal-fold, config consolidation, competitor send, orphan cleanup (behind `strategy-command-center`)

**What it does:** The final phase of the admin Strategy IA redesign (the visible-reorg track), in four parts. **(1) Signal fold** (flag `strategy-signal-fold`) — `mintSignalRecs` folds insight-derived `StrategySignal`s into the recommendation set at strategy-gen time (after `applyLifecycleCarryOver`), so the existing `RECOMMENDATIONS_UPDATED` broadcast + lifecycle cover them automatically; dedup via a `signal:<insightId>` merge key; the fold reuses the strict `keywordEvaluationContext` so it suppresses declined/business-misfit keywords exactly as the standalone card did. Signal-sourced recs are **exempted from the auto-resolve sweep** — their producer never re-emits them, so "absent from `newSources`" is always a false positive; without the exemption every un-actioned folded signal would flip to "✓ Auto-resolved" on the next daily/on-mutation recompute. **(2) Config consolidation** (under `strategy-command-center`) — a new `StrategyConfigPanel` collapses `StrategySettings` + Local SEO market config into one disclosure on the flag-ON Overview (wired to `LocalSeoMarketSetupDrawer` + a market summary), removing the outside-tabs config leak; flag-ON de-dups Local SEO **results** to the Keyword Hub. **(3) Competitor send** (flag `strategy-competitor-send`) — adds the `competitor` `RecType` (one-commit 5-map + Zod `recommendationSchema` + `REC_POLICY_REGISTRY` lockstep) and a per-row "Send to client" in `CompetitiveIntel` backed by a new idempotent `POST /:ws/competitor-rec` mint endpoint (mints-then-sends via `sendRecommendation()`; two-axis invariant — `clientStatus` only, never `RecStatus`); the client renderer in `InsightsEngine` ships gated. **(4) Orphan cleanup** — deleted 3 dead components (`OpportunitiesList`, `RequestedKeywordTriage`, `DecisionQueue`) + the cascade orphan `buildOpportunityRows`. **flag-OFF is byte-identical** to today's Strategy page throughout (independently verified): `IntelligenceSignals` and `LocalSeoVisibilityPanel mode="strategy"` are retained for the flag-OFF path; the de-dup and the fold are scoped flag-ON only.

**Agency value:** Finishes the reorg the strategist actually sees — intelligence signals now live as actionable, lifecycle-managed recommendations in the cockpit instead of a passive sidecar card; settings + Local SEO config collapse into one tidy disclosure instead of scattered outside-tab clutter; and competitive gaps gain the same one-click "Send to client" the rest of the Strategy surfaces already have. Dead band-era components are gone, shrinking the surface to maintain.

**Client value:** Indirect until cutover (everything dark-launched), then: folded signals and competitor gaps reach the client as the same curated, lifecycle-tracked recommendations as every other Strategy item, with no separate one-off surfaces.

**Mutual:** Heavy reuse — the fold rides the existing `generateRecommendations` pipeline, broadcast, and `applyLifecycleCarryOver`; competitor send rides the single-writer `sendRecommendation` + the public allow-list; the config panel reuses the `StrategySettings`/disclosure pattern. Net-new substrate is just `mintSignalRecs`, the competitor mint endpoint, and `StrategyConfigPanel`. Three independent review passes (per-lane, holistic end-to-end, release-readiness) caught and fixed: the signal auto-resolve self-destruct, a permanently-disabled competitor Send button (no mint path), an over-broad Local SEO deletion that broke flag-OFF byte-identical, and two test gaps the lanes' scoped runs missed.

**Files:** Server — `server/recommendations.ts` (`mintSignalRecs` + signal auto-resolve exemption + keyword-eval parity), `server/routes/recommendations.ts` (`/competitor-rec` mint), `server/recommendation-lifecycle.ts` + `server/schemas/workspace-schemas.ts` + `shared/types/recommendations.ts` (competitor lockstep). Frontend — `src/components/strategy/{StrategyConfigPanel,CompetitiveIntel,StrategyCompetitiveTab}.tsx`, `src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx`, `src/components/local-seo/LocalSeoVisibilityPanel.tsx`, `src/components/client/InsightsEngine.tsx`, `src/lib/{recCategoryMap,recTypeTab}.ts`, `src/api/misc.ts`; deleted `src/components/strategy/{OpportunitiesList,RequestedKeywordTriage,DecisionQueue,buildOpportunityRows}.*`. Tests — `tests/integration/{strategy-signal-fold,competitor-send}.test.ts`, `server/__tests__/signal-fold-carry-over.test.ts`, `tests/component/strategy/StrategyConfigPanel.test.tsx`, `tests/unit/strategy/orphan-cuts.test.ts`. PR #1286. Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-18-strategy-redesign-synthesized-plan.md` + execution map.

### 523. Strategy v3 — admin curation cockpit: bulk curation + self-managing nudges (Phase 3, behind `strategy-command-center`)

**What it does:** Turns the v3 curation cockpit (the flag-ON Strategy Overview hero — built on the Phase-1 two-axis lifecycle `clientStatus`/`lifecycle` and the Phase-2 single-writer `recommendation-lifecycle.ts` + `StrategyCockpit`) into a **batch-over-~144 tool** and makes the curation loop **self-managing**. Two halves. **(1) Bulk curation.** Per-row selection checkboxes on the faceted list plus a **predicate** "Select all N matching" affordance — selection is a *filter descriptor + exclusion set* (`useCurationSelection`), NOT N mounted checkbox booleans, so "apply to all 144 matching" coexists with the cap-at-8 rendered view without virtualization. A sticky `CurationBulkActionBar` (modeled on `KeywordBulkActionBar`) drives bulk **Send / Throttle (7/30/90d picker) / Strike (arm-then-confirm)**; the server applies all N lifecycle transitions in **one `db.transaction()`** via the single-writer (`POST /api/recommendations/:ws/bulk`), skipping any rec that can't legally transition (savepoint rollback per-rec, never a whole-batch rollback), firing one `curated_recs_sent` doorbell email for a bulk Send, and one `RECOMMENDATIONS_UPDATED` broadcast. Switching lifecycle bucket/category clears an in-flight select-all so a bulk action can never silently re-target the wrong set. **(2) Self-managing loop.** A flag-gated (`strategy-staleness-scan`, default OFF) lightweight pass grafted into the existing 24h `outcome-crons` tick flags **stale sent** recs (sent ≥14d with no client response) and **supersessions** (a newer active-uncurated rec covering the same pages) — idempotent via a type-filtered activity read (no migration), writing one admin-only `rec_nudge_stale` activity + broadcast per new nudge. The cockpit header surfaces a `NeedsAttentionStrip` (an amber warning callout — `stale_sent` / `superseded` / `new_reply`, each a real action button, derived client-side in lockstep with the server's `scanWorkspaceStaleness`) and a `CurationMeter` ("N sent · curate, don't just send" above a healthy ceiling). The `ClientSignalsSlice.recResponses` write (counts + recent responses, keyed on the response-time `updatedAt`) lets AdminChat "see the loop," and a NotificationBell entry surfaces "N client recommendation responses" deep-linking to the cockpit.

**Agency value:** Collapses curation of a ~144-rec backlog from one-at-a-time clicking into select-all-in-filter + a single transactional bulk action, and stops curated recommendations from rotting silently — the operator is nudged when a sent rec goes unanswered or is overtaken by a newer one, and is pinged the moment a client responds, so the curation loop runs itself instead of needing manual polling.

**Client value:** Indirect but real — a bulk Send still fires the same per-client doorbell email, and the self-managing nudges mean stale or superseded recommendations get cleaned up rather than lingering in the client's "Recommended this month" surface (Phase 4). No new client-facing surface in this phase.

**Mutual:** Pure reuse of the Phase-1 single-writer + state machines + `isActiveRec`, the Phase-2 cockpit shell + `cockpitRowModel`, the existing `outcome-crons` scheduler, the `Checkbox`/`SectionCard`/`Button` primitives, and the `KeywordBulkActionBar`/`StrategyStalenessNudges` UI patterns — the only net-new substrate is the bulk endpoint, the staleness scan, and the four UI components. The client staleness derivation (`cockpitAttention.ts`) is a known same-class brittleness as `isActiveRec` (server module is DB-bound, so the pure predicates are mirrored with a cross-reference + unit-test lockstep; fast-follow: extract to `shared/`). Dark-launched behind `strategy-command-center` (default OFF); the nudge engine additionally behind `strategy-staleness-scan` (default OFF) until its on-read/cost behavior is validated.

**Files:** Server — `server/recommendation-staleness.ts` (NEW: `classifyStaleSentRec`/`scanWorkspaceStaleness`/`runSentRecStalenessScan`), `server/outcome-crons.ts` (24h registration), `server/intelligence/client-signals-slice.ts` (`recResponses` write), `server/routes/recommendations.ts` (`/bulk` endpoint), `server/routes/workspaces.ts` (`recResponses` overview payload), `server/activity-log.ts` (`rec_nudge_stale` type + `listActivityByType`). Frontend — `src/components/strategy/{CurationBulkActionBar,NeedsAttentionStrip,CurationMeter,cockpitAttention,StrategyCockpit,CockpitRow}.tsx/.ts`, `src/components/strategy/hooks/useCurationSelection.ts`, `src/hooks/admin/{useRecBulkMutation,useAdminRecommendations,useNotifications}.ts`, `src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx`, `src/components/strategy/index.ts`. Tests across `tests/unit/{recommendation-staleness,client-signals-rec-responses}.test.ts`, `tests/unit/strategy/{useCurationSelection,cockpitAttention,StrategyCockpit,CockpitRow}.test.*`, `tests/unit/admin/{useRecBulkMutation,useNotifications-recResponses}.test.*`, `tests/component/strategy/{CurationBulkActionBar,NeedsAttentionStrip,CurationMeter}.test.tsx`, `tests/integration/{recommendation-staleness-scan,recommendation-bulk-admin,workspace-overview-rec-responses}.test.ts`. Two-stage per-lane review + combined opus/sonnet review caught: a sent-rec doorbell gap on bulk Send, a top-500-all-types dedup window bug, the select-all re-target footgun, and a surface-2-in-surface-2 invisible attention strip.
