### 486. Cross-workspace platform_learnings priors — anonymized win-rate fallback tier (A6, audit #22)

**What it does:** Gives a workspace with no measured outcomes of its own a real signal instead of nothing. A new platform-level store (`platform_learnings_priors`, migration 133) aggregates a single win rate per `actionType` across *every* workspace, computed from the same `tracked_actions` + `action_outcomes` join the per-workspace learnings engine reads (latest qualifying 30/60/90-day conclusive outcome per action; `not_acted_on` excluded per A1). It is anonymized **by construction** — the stored row holds only `(action_type, win_rate, contributing_workspaces, scored_actions, computed_at)`, with no workspace id, page URL, title, or keyword, mirroring the `keyword_metrics_cache` precedent. Two privacy/honesty floors gate publication: a **cohort floor** of ≥3 distinct contributing workspaces (below which a single workspace's data could be reverse-identified) and a **sample floor** of ≥5 scored actions; a type that clears neither is simply absent (FM-2 — never a fabricated baseline). The fallback seam lives in `server/outcome-learning-default-path.ts`: a new `buildPlatformPriorAdjustment()` applies a deliberately *smaller* score nudge than own-history, and `buildOutcomeLearningStatusNote()` appends a clearly-labeled cross-workspace benchmark line — but **only** when the workspace's own `LearningsSlice.availability` is `no_data` or `degraded`. The availability switch stays authoritative: a `ready` workspace keeps its own learnings and never sees a prior, and an admin-`disabled` workspace suppresses priors too. The intelligence slice populates `LearningsSlice.platformPriors` on the same fallback condition, and a weekly cron (`server/outcome-crons.ts`, mirroring A5's EMV calibration block) recomputes the aggregate.

**Agency value:** A brand-new client — or one whose learnings subsystem hiccuped on a run — no longer falls back to pure generic best practices. Keyword recommendation prompts (the first wired consumer — `server/keyword-recommendations.ts` threads `LearningsSlice.platformPriors` through `buildOutcomeLearningStatusNote`) get a measured, clearly-labeled platform-wide prior for each action type as a stopgap until the workspace accrues its own history, all without any per-workspace privacy exposure. The remaining `buildOutcomeLearningStatusNote` callers (content-brief, content-decay, keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis) and the score-adjustment helper are wired as follow-ups on the same contract.

**Client value:** Recommendations for a new account are informed by what has actually worked across the agency's whole book of business, presented honestly as a cross-workspace benchmark — never dressed up as that client's own results.

**Mutual:** Establishes a privacy-safe cross-workspace learning tier (aggregates-only, cohort-floored) that any future consumer can read, with the anonymization contract pinned by a schema-level test and the honesty labeling pinned in the prompt-note helper.

**Files:** `server/db/migrations/133-platform-learnings-priors.sql`, `server/platform-learnings-priors.ts` (store + recompute + floors), `server/outcome-learning-default-path.ts` (`buildPlatformPriorAdjustment`, `buildPlatformPriorPromptNote`, extended status note), `shared/types/intelligence.ts` (`PlatformPriorEntry` + `LearningsSlice.platformPriors`), `server/intelligence/learnings-slice.ts` (fallback-tier population), `server/outcome-crons.ts` (weekly cron), `scripts/pr-check.ts` (KNOWN_UNRENDERED_FIELDS). Tests: `tests/integration/a6-platform-learnings-priors.test.ts`.

---

### 485. InsightsSlice byType cap + pre-cap countsByType rollup (G3, audit 7b)

**What it does:** Bounds the last unbounded payload in the workspace-intelligence bundle. `InsightsSlice.byType` is now capped at the top 25 insights per type ordered by `impactScore` (previously it carried *every* stored insight per type — on a 400-insight workspace the insights slice alone was ~443 KB, dominating AdminChat context, MCP `get_workspace_intelligence` responses, and generation-context payloads). Two new required pre-cap rollups keep count consumers honest: `countsByType` (full per-type totals) and `countsByTypeBySeverity` (full type×severity matrix for jointly-filtered totals). Every `byType` reader was redirected *before* the cap landed: `listAllInsightsFromSlice` now reads the `all` list (top 100 by impact — AdminChat, diagnostic context inherit), meeting-brief metrics/cache-key counts read `countsByType`, the client intelligence summary derives its counts from the matrix (exact admin-only-type + positive-severity exclusion at any workspace size — both pinned contracts hold beyond the 100-cap), and monthly-digest's deterministic wins/resolved rollups read the store directly (documented `intel-builder-ok` exception — full iteration is no longer slice-backed). Measured on a seeded 400-insight workspace: insights slice JSON 452,896 → 213,435 bytes (−52.9%); the byType portion 353,622 → 114,161 bytes (−67.7%).

**Agency value:** AdminChat and AI generation prompts stop dragging hundreds of duplicate insight objects through every assembly; MCP consumers get a bounded, predictable payload. Counts shown in meeting briefs and digests remain exact pre-cap totals.

**Client value:** Indirect — faster intelligence-backed surfaces and unchanged (still exact) client portal insight counts.

**Mutual:** The prompt-size guard and the count-honesty contract now coexist explicitly: lists are bounded, counts are authoritative, and the contract test pins both so no future consumer silently under-reports from a capped list.

**Files:** `shared/types/intelligence.ts` (`InsightsSlice.countsByType` + cap JSDoc), `server/intelligence/insights-slice.ts` (cap + rollup + helper redirect), `server/meeting-brief-generator.ts`, `server/routes/client-intelligence.ts`, `server/monthly-digest.ts`, `scripts/pr-check.ts` (KNOWN_UNRENDERED_FIELDS), `docs/rules/workspace-intelligence.md`. Tests: `tests/unit/insights-slice.test.ts`, `tests/integration/g3-insights-bytype-cap.test.ts`, `tests/contract/intelligence-slice-population.test.ts`, `tests/unit/workspace-intelligence-extended.test.ts`.

---

### 484. Keyword-Level Outcome Bridge + Client Requested-Keyword Rank Trend (A4, audit #15)

**What it does:** Closes the keyword-level outcome loop in three pieces. (1) **Hub actions enter outcome tracking** — Keyword Hub track / promote / add-to-strategy actions (single, bulk, route, and MCP paths, all via B2's `applyKeywordCommandCenterAction` contract point) now record a tracked outcome action through the new `recordKeywordTrackingAction()` (`server/outcome-measurement-keywords.ts`), reusing A3's `strategy_page_keyword` sourceType + `strategyPageKeywordSourceId()` idempotency key so the Hub and strategy-regeneration writers share one dedup space (re-track / decline→re-add never duplicates). Baselines capture the keyword's current position from the freshest rank snapshot (`exact`), or an honestly-empty `estimated` baseline when none exists. (2) **Keyword-level scoring** — the measurement cron scores `strategy_keyword_added` actions that carry a `targetKeyword` against `rank_snapshots` (keyword-level position, ≤14-day freshness via `readKeywordRankSnapshot`) instead of page-aggregate GSC, matching the keyword-level baselines; missing/stale snapshots score `inconclusive`, never fabricated (FM-2). Two inherited A3-review fixes shipped here: a search-metric action whose baseline lacks the PRIMARY metric now scores `inconclusive` (previously a missing baseline position was read as 0, fabricating a loss), and permanently-unmeasurable strategy-level actions (no pageUrl, no targetKeyword, metrics-empty baseline) exit the measurement queue at their first due checkpoint instead of emitting inconclusive noise until day 90. (3) **Client trend card** — the client Strategy tab shows a 180-day rank-trend `ChartCard` for the keywords the client requested themselves (`StrategyRequestedKeywordTrendSection` + `useRequestedKeywordRankTrend`, growth-gated inside the no-keywords check so free-tier clients never see an irrelevant upsell, live-refreshed on `RANK_TRACKING_UPDATED`).

**Agency value:** Keyword work done in the Hub finally feeds the learnings engine — wins and losses on tracked keywords accrue to the same outcome dataset that calibrates recommendations — and the measurement queue stops carrying permanently-dead actions or fabricating losses from partial baselines.

**Client value:** "Add a keyword you care about" now has a visible payoff: a live 180-day ranking trend of exactly the keywords they requested, refreshed as new rank snapshots land.

---

### 483. Server-side grounding for client chat — kill the verbatim-context prompt-injection / token-sink surface (E4, audit #17)

**What it does:** Hardens the public client chat endpoint (`POST /api/public/search-chat/:workspaceId`). Previously the endpoint accepted `context: z.record(z.unknown())` and serialized it VERBATIM into the system prompt (`JSON.stringify(context)`), so any client could inject arbitrary JSON below the guardrails (prompt injection) and there was no size cap (unbounded token sink). Now: (1) the opaque `context` field is removed from the Zod schema — Zod's default strip means the existing frontend keeps working but its `context` payload never reaches the prompt; (2) client input is limited to enum/size-capped HINTS only (`currentTab` from a fixed `ClientTab`-mirrored union, `days` bounded 1–366, plus the already-capped `question`/`sessionId`/`betaMode`); (3) the model's view of workspace data is now SERVER-ASSEMBLED — the data-inventory flags and the grounding block are derived from intelligence slices (`buildSeoPromptContext` with the client-safe slice set `seoContext`/`insights`/`siteHealth`/`learnings`) plus server-owned reads (search/GA4 headline overviews re-fetched server-side, audit-traffic, content plan, approval/request counts read from the DB — never client-claimed). The grounding deliberately EXCLUDES the agency-only `clientSignals` slice (churn risk, intent signals, approval rate) per the D1/EMV precedent, and the standard formatter path already omits admin-only fields like `emvPerWeek`. Slice failure degrades to minimal grounding and still returns 200 (FM-2), never 500. Response shape `{ answer, sessionId, detectedIntent }` is unchanged for the frontend. Reuses the existing `client-search-chat` named operation in the AI operation registry (prose output — no new registry entry needed).

**Agency value:** Closes a real security hole on a public, client-facing endpoint — clients can no longer steer the advisor with injected instructions or poison its answers with fabricated metrics, and can no longer blow up token spend by posting megabytes of "context." The agency, not the browser, now decides exactly what the model sees, scoped to the workspace.

**Client value:** The advisor's answers are grounded in authoritative, server-verified workspace data rather than whatever the browser happened to send, so its numbers are trustworthy and consistent. No behavior change in the chat UI.

**Mutual:** A safer, more predictable advisor that can't be manipulated or made to leak agency-only intelligence, with answer quality preserved via real slice-derived grounding.

**Files:** `server/routes/public-analytics.ts` (`chatSchema` hardened, `CLIENT_CHAT_TAB_HINTS`, server-side grounding + headline-overview re-fetch + approval/request reads, verbatim-context removal). Tests: `tests/integration/client-chat-grounding.test.ts` (injection-never-in-prompt via mocked `callAI` capture, oversized-context drop, client-safe slice set, enum-hint accept + invalid-enum 400, FM-2 minimal-grounding 200, response-shape preservation, 401-without-auth).

---

### 482. Persisted AI review verdicts + scraped source evidence (audit #16)

**What it does:** Makes content QA durable in two places that previously evaporated. (1) AI review verdicts: the post AI-review run (`POST /api/content-posts/:wsId/:postId/ai-review`) now persists its full verdict pack (`StoredAIReview` — the post-provenance-marking review map, evidence snapshot, `reviewedAt`, model) on a new `content_posts.ai_review` column, so verdicts survive editor close and are retrievable on any fresh post GET. Provenance-sensitive items (`factual_accuracy`, `no_hallucinations`) are only ever stored `pass=false` + `humanReviewRequired` — the grounding contract is preserved at the storage layer, not just the response. (2) Scraped source text: the brief generation job now persists C1's enrichment output (`BriefSourceEvidence` on `content_briefs.source_evidence`) — scraped reference-page `bodyText`, real SERP result snippets (previously dropped at the `generateBrief` boundary), fetch timestamps, and style-example pages. Regenerating a brief carries the evidence forward. Both fields are admin-internal and stripped from public client brief/post responses.

**Agency value:** Reviewers stop re-running AI reviews because the verdicts vanished with the editor session, and the saved source pack means a future claim can be checked against the *actual text* that grounded the brief — not just URLs. This is the storage prerequisite for the real-text evidence ledger (#27).

**Client value:** Indirect — stronger, auditable human-verification workflow behind delivered content, with zero new client-facing payload (the scraped competitor text never leaves the admin boundary).

**Mutual:** One review run now produces a durable QA artifact tied to the post, and one scrape now produces a durable evidence pack tied to the brief — both reusable by future features without re-fetching or re-paying for the work.

**Files:** `server/db/migrations/132-c4-persist-review-results.sql`, `shared/types/content.ts` (`StoredAIReview`, `BriefSourceEvidence`, `BriefScrapedSource`), `server/schemas/content-schemas.ts`, `server/content-posts-db.ts`, `server/content-brief.ts`, `server/routes/content-posts.ts` (review seam + activity + broadcast), `server/content-brief-generation-job.ts` (persistence seam), `server/routes/public-content.ts` (client-boundary strips), `server/activity-log.ts` (`post_ai_review`). Tests: `tests/integration/c4-persist-review-results.test.ts`.

---

### 481. Recommendations ↔ Content Pipeline Reconciliation (D2, audit #11)

**What it does:** Closes the loop between the recommendation engine and the content pipeline in three directions. (1) **Generation suppression** — `generateRecommendations` now includes the `contentPipeline` intelligence slice (via the shared `buildRecommendationGenerationContext` builder) and skips content-gap recs whose target keyword already has an in-flight brief or post (new comparison-keyed `ContentPipelineSlice.inFlightTargetKeywords`, populated in `assembleContentPipeline`). Suppression fails open: if the slice degrades, recs mint as before — never a false resolution. (2) **Publish-time resolution** — the C3 publish domain service (`publishPostToWebflow`) makes one best-effort call to the new `resolveContentRecommendationsForPublishedPost()` after a successful publish, completing active content recs whose persisted `targetKeyword` (new optional `Recommendation` field, set at content-gap mint) matches the published post's keyword — with `validateTransition`, summary recompute, intelligence-cache invalidation, and a `RECOMMENDATIONS_UPDATED` broadcast. A resolution failure never fails a publish; a failed publish never resolves a rec. (3) **CTA → brief purchase** — `mapToProduct('content', …)` no longer returns `{}`: content recs carry the real brief-purchase product keyed by the gap's suggested page type (`brief_blog` $125 … `brief_pillar` $200, mirroring `PRODUCT_MAP`), so the client rec card's purchase CTA ("Order Content Brief — $X") routes into the existing cart → `/api/stripe/cart-checkout` brief-purchase flow.

**Agency value:** The rec queue and the content pipeline can no longer contradict each other — the team never sees (and the client is never sold) a "create content for X" rec while a brief or post for X is already in flight, and a publish clears its rec immediately instead of waiting for the GSC-lag-gated regen.

**Client value:** The priority list stays honest: recommendations disappear the moment the work is actually underway or live, and content recommendations finally have a one-click purchase path instead of a dead-end "we should create this" card.

**Mutual:** Suppression + resolution prevent double-selling content the client already paid for; the CTA turns the highest-intent moment (a data-grounded content gap) into a frictionless brief order on existing payment rails.

**Files:** `server/recommendations.ts` (slice in builder call, suppression, `BRIEF_PRODUCT_BY_PAGE_TYPE`, `mapToProduct` pageType param, `resolveContentRecommendationsForPublishedPost`), `server/domains/content/publish-post-to-webflow.ts` (one best-effort hook), `server/intelligence/content-pipeline-slice.ts` + `shared/types/intelligence.ts` (`inFlightTargetKeywords`), `shared/types/recommendations.ts` + `server/schemas/workspace-schemas.ts` (`targetKeyword`), `src/components/client/InsightsEngine.tsx` (CTA label). Tests: `tests/integration/recommendations-content-reconciliation.test.ts` (suppression, publish resolution, FM-2 both directions), `tests/unit/recommendations-pure-logic.test.ts` (brief-product mapping).

---

### 480. Legible + dollarized keyword value — plain-language reasons + per-keyword $/mo + portfolio "Revenue at stake" (PR 1–3)

**What it does:** Makes the consolidated keyword value score legible and dollarized on the surfaces clients and the agency already use, with ONE reasons definition and ONE dollar definition. PR 1 (`kwv-real-cpc`) adds a `cpc` column to `content_gaps` (migration + mapper + enrichment populate) so content-gap value scores reflect real CPC instead of the `CPC_UNKNOWN` 0.5 proxy. PR 2 (`kwv-value-breakdown`) adds the `keywordValueReasons` helper (server-computed plain-language reasons from the Layer-1 components — "Commercial intent · $9 CPC", "Winnable · KD 24", "Strong demand · 2,400/mo", "Local boost ×1.5"), serialized as `valueReasons` onto both the admin KCC row and the client strategy explanation, rendered in both keyword drawers (flag-gated on `keyword-value-scoring`, blue = data). PR 3 (`kwv-dollar-value`) adds the single `keywordDollarValue` helper (`server/scoring/keyword-value-money.ts`): `currentMonthly = clicks × cpc` (identical to `roi.ts` `trafficValue` — enforced by a cross-module equivalence test) and `upsideMonthly = impressions × CTR-uplift × cpc` (realized $ only, no intent weight). `cpc` is joined from `page_keywords` onto both keyword builders; a per-keyword "Revenue potential" $ block renders on both drawers (emerald = success/$), and a portfolio "Revenue at stake" (Σ `upsideMonthly`) is a 4th hero `StatCard` on `ROIDashboard`, computed in `computeROI` by reusing the same helper.

**Agency value:** One reasons formula and one dollar formula across every surface — the Hub, the client strategy drawer, and the ROI dashboard can never show a contradictory "why" or a contradictory "$" for the same keyword. The realized-$ equivalence (`currentMonthly == roi.ts trafficValue`) is locked by test, so there is no second dollar engine to drift. Real CPC on content gaps makes the value score honest before it propagates.

**Client value:** Clients see WHY a keyword is worth pursuing in plain language (not a bare 0–100), the realized $/mo a keyword earns today, the upside $/mo of moving it up, and a portfolio headline of the monthly revenue at stake across tracked keywords with position upside — all behind the existing Growth+ tier gate, the same class of realized $ ROIDashboard already shows.

**Mutual:** Pure reuse — no new endpoint, no new client keyword tab, no second dollar engine. Rides the existing `/api/public/roi` query + the keyword-strategy / KCC serialization. The dollar helper floors to 0 when CPC is unknown (drawers hide the block), so CPC sparsity degrades gracefully.

**Files:** `server/db/migrations/*-content-gap-cpc.sql`, `server/content-gaps.ts`, `server/keyword-strategy-enrichment.ts` (PR 1); `server/scoring/keyword-value-score.ts` (`keywordValueReasons`), `server/keyword-command-center.ts`, `server/keyword-strategy-ux.ts`, `src/components/client/strategy/StrategyKeywordDrawer.tsx`, `src/components/keyword-command-center/KeywordDetailDrawer.tsx` (PR 2); `server/scoring/keyword-value-money.ts` (`keywordDollarValue`), `server/roi.ts` + `shared/types/roi.ts` (`revenueAtStake`), `server/page-keywords.ts` (lite-reader cpc), `src/components/client/ROIDashboard.tsx`, both drawers (PR 3). Tests: `tests/unit/keyword-value-money.test.ts` (incl. the equivalence test), `tests/component/keyword-dollar-value-drawer.test.tsx`, `tests/integration/roi-attribution.test.ts` (`revenueAtStake`), `tests/integration/keyword-strategy-assembler-public-read.test.ts` (cpc + serialized $).

### 527. Keyword value score wiring — content plans, briefs, and client briefing ranking

**What it does:** Completes the `kwv-one-score-everywhere` wiring delta for the highest-impact keyword-ranking read paths. `buildKeywordValueScoringContext()` now owns the shared posture/market/business-address context outside the Keyword Command Center domain. Content-plan keyword recommendations use `computeKeywordValueScore` as the deterministic base score before the existing strategic-fit, outcome-learning, cannibalization, and authority adjustments. Client briefing content-gap recommendations rank by a private Layer-1 sort score when volume/impression/difficulty/CPC signals exist, while preserving the public `opportunityScore` display field and stripping the private sort key from the response.

**Client value:** The weekly briefing and content planning paths now lead with commercial, winnable keywords instead of letting raw volume push broad informational ideas above low-volume revenue terms.

**Mutual:** No new API fields, DB migrations, UI components, or public response-shape changes. Legacy content gaps with only a stored `opportunityScore` continue to fall back to that score.

**Files:** `server/scoring/keyword-value-context.ts`, `server/briefing-client-projection.ts`, `server/keyword-recommendations.ts`, `shared/keyword-opportunity-projection.ts`, `docs/rules/keyword-hub.md`, `tests/unit/keyword-opportunity-projection.test.ts`, `tests/integration/briefing-public.test.ts`, `tests/integration/keyword-recommendations-gsc.test.ts`.

### 479. Keyword value score consolidation — one intent classifier + Layer-1 component interface (PR 1 + PR 2)

**What it does:** Establishes the keystone foundation for the `kwv-one-score-everywhere` sprint. PR 1 makes `deriveValueIntent` (`server/scoring/keyword-value-score.ts`) the single keyword intent classifier for both Layer 1 (Hub value score) and Layer 2 (recommendation OV scorer), retiring `toOpportunityIntent` and the inline copy in `keyword-strategy-enrichment.ts`. Closes the `comparison`-intent drift: a `comparison` keyword was weighted 0.7 in the Hub but 0.5 in recs — both now map to `commercial` (0.7) via the shared classifier. PR 2 (this entry) adds `computeKeywordValueComponents(input, ctx): { score, components }` — a sibling to `computeKeywordValueScore` that exposes the internal `KeywordValueComponents` interface (`commercialValue / demand / winnability / localMultiplier / intent`). `computeKeywordValueScore` becomes a thin wrapper (`.score`) so the 4 existing scalar callers are byte-identical. Signal-gate parity is hard: a gated-out input returns `{ score: undefined, components: undefined }` — locked by test.

**Agency value:** One intent classifier means the Hub and recommendation scorer can never disagree on a keyword's value tier again. The component interface gives the upcoming `kwv-value-breakdown` render layer one vocabulary to work from instead of reverse-engineering the scorer's internal math.

**Client value:** Indirect — the comparison-intent correction ensures a keyword like "invisalign vs braces" scores with commercial weight on both the Hub and recommendation surfaces, so high-intent comparison searches surface correctly in prioritized recommendations.

**Mutual:** Purely additive / output-neutral in PR 2. Provides the shared input-resolution seam that `kwv-real-cpc`, `kwv-value-breakdown`, and the surface re-ranking (`kwv-one-score-everywhere`) will build on. No render change; no flag.

**Files (PR 2):** `server/scoring/keyword-value-score.ts` (`KeywordValueComponents` interface + `computeKeywordValueComponents` sibling + thin `computeKeywordValueScore` wrapper), `tests/unit/keyword-value-score.test.ts` (3 new component-interface tests incl. gated-undefined parity).

---

### 478. Tracked-keyword gap provenance pointer (`sourceGapKey`) — Wave 3d-i (ADDITIVE, behavior-preserving)

**What it does:** Persists a content-addressed provenance pointer, `sourceGapKey`, on tracked keywords so the platform can answer *which content-gap / keyword-gap a tracked keyword was approved from*. When a keyword is approved from a `content_gap` / `keyword_gap` feedback surface, the approve path stamps `sourceGapKey = keywordComparisonKey(displayKeyword)` (matching the `content_gaps` PK `(workspace_id, target_keyword)`) in the in-memory mutator that builds the `TrackedKeyword`. The pointer is written to the additive nullable `tracked_keywords.source_gap_key` column (migration 118 — no new migration) and projected by `rowToTrackedKeyword`, so the admin/provenance-bearing read (`listTrackedKeywordRows`, Keyword Command Center) surfaces it. **Behavior-preserving:** the general read path (`getTrackedKeywords`) and every public serializer stay byte-identical to before — the table-first resolver and the blob writer both STRIP `sourceGapKey`, so provenance never leaks into `GET /api/public/tracked-keywords/:id` or the SEO-strategy payload. **FILL-IF-EMPTY:** `replaceAllTrackedKeywordRows` is delete-then-reinsert, so the in-memory value is the only source of truth on reinsert; `withTrackedKeywordsTxn` hydrates `sourceGapKey` from the table before the updater runs, so a later status-only / reconcile write preserves an existing pointer instead of nulling it, and never overwrites a non-empty one. The sibling `sourcePageId` is DEFERRED — `page_keywords` has no stable surrogate id (its PK is the mutable `page_path`, migration 024), so its column is written NULL and intentionally not projected pending a stable page id.

**Agency value:** Strategists get a stable back-reference from a tracked keyword to the gap that produced it, without any change to existing read surfaces or client output — provenance is admin-only and additive.

**Client value:** None directly — the client read path and public endpoints are byte-identical to before (no provenance exposure).

**Mutual:** Lays the provenance foundation for later keyword-explainability waves (the laundering remap, `inferTrackedKeywordSources`, reconcile auto-deprecation, and `strategyOwned` are 3d-ii) while keeping the 3c-i (shadow) + 3c-ii (read-switch) parity invariants green and unchanged.

**Files:** `shared/types/rank-tracking.ts` (`sourceGapKey?` on `TrackedKeyword`), `server/rank-tracking.ts` (Zod `.optional()` field, `AddTrackedKeywordOptions`, blob strip on write, table hydrate in `withTrackedKeywordsTxn`, fill-if-empty in `addTrackedKeywordToConfig`), `server/tracked-keywords-store.ts` (project `source_gap_key`, persist it in `keywordToParams`, resolver provenance strip), `server/keyword-feedback.ts` (`sourceGapKeyForFeedback` on single + bulk approve), `server/keyword-command-center.ts` (`mergeTrackedKeywordProvenance` + tracking-row whitelist), `shared/types/keyword-command-center.ts` (`sourceGapKey?` on the tracking state), `tests/integration/tracked-keywords-row-table.test.ts` (round-trip / fill-if-empty / strip-no-leak / admin-sees-it).

### 469. Unified Client Inbox — PriorityStrip + uniform Approve / Request changes / Decline (built dark — `unified-inbox` flag)

**What it does:** Adds a flag-gated unified client inbox path to `InboxTab` (strangler-fig: when `unified-inbox` is OFF, the existing `new-inbox-ia` + legacy layouts render byte-for-byte unchanged; when ON, the new unified view renders instead). The unified view mounts the previously-orphaned `PriorityStrip` as the single prioritized "Needs your attention" list, backed by a new `GET /api/public/deliverables/:workspaceId` read endpoint that assembles ONE client-facing list from the hybrid `ClientDeliverable` model — physical `client_deliverable` rows PLUS projected `copy_section`/`content_request` entries (via each adapter's `projectFromSource()`), filtered to client-facing statuses (awaiting/changes-requested/partial/approved/declined) and Zod-validated. Each item renders as a `DecisionCard` with uniform Approve / Request changes (+note) / Decline verbs that call the real `PATCH /respond` endpoint, and shows the send age (from `sentAt`). `NormalizedDecision` is generalized to read the unified model (`kind` added alongside the kept `isSingleAction`; `DecisionSource` widened to include `'deliverable'`; `normalizeDeliverable()` adapter added) without changing legacy behavior. A flag-gated React Query hook (`useUnifiedInbox`, client-prefixed key, typed `src/api/deliverables.ts` wrapper, no raw fetch) fetches only when the flag is on. Phase-2a of the unified send-to-client migration (Pillar 2 client inbox).

**Agency value:** One coherent client review surface and one decision contract per item, replacing the five bespoke per-pipeline client surfaces — adding a reviewable work type later renders through the same inbox automatically.

**Client value:** A single prioritized list of everything that needs their attention, with the same Approve / Request changes / Decline choice on every item and a clear "sent N days ago" age — no per-feature inbox dialects.

**Mutual:** Built fully dark behind `unified-inbox` (default off); production with the flag off is unchanged. No flag flip and no dual-write flags touched. The `client_deliverable` table is empty until the Phase-1 send-path cutover, so the endpoint returns only projected copy/content_request entries today — exercised with seeded rows in tests. See roadmap `unified-deliverables-phase-2a-client-inbox` and `docs/designs/2026-06-01-unified-send-to-client-design.md` §5.

---

### 470. Unified Admin "Client Deliverables" Pane — status axis + stale signal + per-item Remind (built dark — `unified-inbox` flag)

**What it does:** Adds a flag-gated unified admin operator pane (`ClientDeliverablesPane.tsx`) mounted as the first sub-tab of the admin `requests` tab when `unified-inbox` is ON (strangler-fig: when OFF, the existing Signals/Requests/Client Actions sub-tabs render byte-for-byte unchanged; `AdminInbox.tsx` and `ClientActionsTab.tsx` are untouched). The pane is backed by a new admin read endpoint `GET /api/deliverables/:workspaceId` (`requireWorkspaceAccess`, never `requireAuth`) that returns ONE list of EVERY deliverable in the workspace — all statuses, physical `client_deliverable` rows PLUS projected `copy_section`/`content_request` — via `listAllWorkspaceDeliverables()` (the client-facing status filter was factored out so both reads share `assembleAllDeliverables`). Each row is annotated server-side (`server/domains/inbox/admin-inbox-read.ts`) with the operator **status axis** (`awaiting_client` · `changes_requested` [incl. `partial`] · `approved` (to apply) · `other`), an `ageDays` derived from `sentAt`, and a derived **`stale`** flag (an `awaiting_client` item whose age ≥ `STALE_AWAITING_DAYS` = 7). The Zod-validated response (`shared/types/admin-deliverable-view.ts`) feeds a flag-gated React Query hook (`useWorkspaceDeliverables`, `admin-`-prefixed key, typed `adminDeliverables` api wrapper, no raw fetch). The pane groups items by axis (oldest-first within each), shows "pending N days" / stale styling and a type badge, and surfaces a **Remind** button on each `awaiting_client` item wired to the existing Phase-0 `POST /api/deliverables/:ws/:id/remind`. `useWorkspaceEvents` invalidates the pane on `DELIVERABLE_*` broadcasts. Phase-2b of the unified send-to-client migration (Pillar 3 admin inbox).

**Agency value:** One operator view of everything sent to a client across all five "send to client" pipelines, with a staleness axis that makes the "client hasn't responded in N days" nudge queue visible for the first time (audit §E1/E2/E6) — and a one-click Remind on every awaiting item.

**Client value:** Indirect — operators can see and chase stalled approvals, so clients get faster follow-up and fewer dropped reviews.

**Mutual:** Built fully dark behind `unified-inbox` (default off); production with the flag off is byte-for-byte unchanged (the new sub-tab and pane never render, the endpoint is never fetched). No flag flip and no dual-write flags touched. The `client_deliverable` table is empty until the Phase-1 send-path cutover, so the endpoint returns only projected entries today — exercised with seeded rows in tests. Deferred to the cutover runbook: operator revise/resend/acknowledge on `changes_requested` items (B18/E7 — needs new mutate endpoints), the notification-bell categorization fix (E3/A2), the workspace-overview B29 double-count, and the read-path slice cutover. See roadmap `unified-deliverables-phase-2b-admin-inbox` and `docs/designs/2026-06-01-unified-send-to-client-design.md` §6.

---

### 471. Unified Inbox Respond Propagation (R2) — client decision writes back to the real source artifact (built dark — `unified-inbox` flag)

**What it does:** Closes the linchpin silent no-op in the unified inbox: `respondToDeliverable` previously wrote ONLY `client_deliverable.status`, so a client "Approve" in the unified inbox never touched the real source artifact (approval batch / client action / schema plan) that the operator and apply logic read. R2 adds an optional `respondToSource(workspaceId, deliverable, decision, opts)` to the deliverable adapter interface; every decision-bearing PHYSICAL adapter implements it and maps the deliverable back to its source id via `payload` — approval-batch family (`seo_edit`/`audit_issue`/`schema_item`/`content_plan_*`) via `payload.legacyBatchId`, client-action family (`redirect`/`internal_link`/`aeo_change`/`content_decay`) via `payload.legacyActionId`, and `schema_plan` via `externalRef`=siteId. Each REUSES the existing per-type source-writing logic (no reimplementation): two route-inline bodies were extracted into shared services — `server/domains/inbox/approval-batch-respond.ts` (`respondToApprovalBatch`: approve→items approved / changes_requested|declined→items rejected, team email + `APPROVAL_UPDATE` broadcast + activity) now drives BOTH the public bulk-approve route AND the unified path, and `server/domains/inbox/schema-plan-respond.ts` (`respondToSchemaPlanFeedback`: approve→`client_approved` / request_changes→`client_changes_requested` + activity + `SCHEMA_PLAN_SENT` broadcast) likewise drives BOTH the public schema-plan feedback route AND the unified path; the client-action family reuses the existing `respondToPublicClientAction` mutation. `respondToDeliverable` calls `adapter.respondToSource` after writing the mirror status and SUPPRESSES its own deliverable-level team email when the source path reports `handled` (no double-notify), always keeping the `DELIVERABLE_UPDATED` broadcast for the unified-inbox UI. Phase R2 of the unified send-to-client review work.

**Agency value:** A client decision made in the unified inbox now actually moves the real work — the approval batch flips to approved/rejected, the client action to approved/changes-requested, the schema plan to client-approved/changes-requested — instead of silently updating only a mirror row the operator never sees. One shared respond service per family means the legacy per-type surface and the unified inbox can never diverge.

**Client value:** Indirect — the Approve / Request changes / Decline they click in the unified inbox is now authoritative on the underlying work, so their decision is acted on rather than lost.

**Mutual:** Built fully dark behind `unified-inbox` (the unified `/respond` endpoint is only reachable when the inbox is on); no flag flip and no dual-write flags touched. R2 propagates only the DECISION/status — the "Apply to Website" Webflow publish stays a separate step (R3, `appliesOnApprove` unchanged/disabled, D-apply). `work_order`/`briefing` (no client decision, R5) and the projected `copy_section`/`content_request` types (R4) get no `respondToSource`; respond on a projected/unknown id still 404s. Proven by `tests/integration/respond-propagation.test.ts` (seeds BOTH the mirror AND its source per family, asserts source-status changed + mirror-status changed + team-notify fired exactly once) and the extended `tests/contract/inbox-route-service-extraction.test.ts`. See roadmap `unified-deliverables-r2-respond-propagation`.

---

### 472. Unified Inbox Deliverable Detail + Per-Item Review (R3a) — surface the substance, flag/subset-approve (built dark — `unified-inbox` flag)

**What it does:** Fixes the owner's complaint that the unified inbox "sends the task, not the content that goes with it." The inbox card's "View N →" now opens a new full-screen `DeliverableDetailModal` (`src/components/client/DeliverableDetailModal.tsx`) that renders the actual SUBSTANCE being reviewed — fed from the unified deliverable's carried data (`NormalizedDecision.items` + `.payload`), not a second fetch. The proven legacy renderers (`ApprovalItemRow` Current/Proposed grid, `AeoRenderer`/`InternalLinkRenderer`/`RedirectRenderer` diff renderers) were EXTRACTED into a shared module (`src/components/client/decision-renderers.tsx`) — including a presentational `ItemDiffRow` that both the legacy `DecisionDetailModal` and the new modal drive (DRY); the legacy modal's behavior is unchanged. Two body shapes by family: the **approval family** (`seo_edit`/`audit_issue`/`schema_item`/`content_plan_*`) renders each typed `items[]` row as an `ItemDiffRow` with PER-ITEM Flag/Unflag and a "Looks good — implement N of M →" subset-approve CTA; the **client_action family** (`redirect`/`internal_link`/`aeo_change`) renders its `payload.items` read-only through the matching renderer wrapper with a whole-action Approve / Request changes / Decline (no per-item flag — matches the legacy, where only the approval family had per-item flag). One respond path: the modal approve forwards the flagged `ClientDeliverableItem.id`s through the EXISTING `useRespondToDeliverable` → `PATCH /api/public/deliverables/:ws/:id/respond` (new optional `flaggedItemIds`), extending the R2 propagation rather than adding a second route. Server-side, `respondToApprovalBatch` gains an optional per-item mode (`itemDecisions`): when present, it drives `updateItem` per legacy item (unflagged → `approved`, flagged → `rejected`) inside the existing `db.transaction()`; absent → today's whole-batch approve-all-pending (back-compat). `respondToApprovalBatchSource` builds `itemDecisions` from `deliverable.items` (mapping each item → legacy `approval_item.id` via `itemPayload.legacyItemId`, skipping items with no mapping) only on `approved` with non-empty flags. The deliverable mirror status stays `approved` ("implement N of M"); `changes_requested`/`declined` ignore flags (whole-deliverable reject). `content_decay` stays inline single-action; projected types keep their read-only deep-link.

**Agency value:** A flag on a single item now becomes a real `rejected` (held) approval item in the source batch while the rest are approved — the operator sees exactly which page-level changes the client held, not an all-or-nothing decision. The activity log reflects "approved N of M (held K)".

**Client value:** The client can finally SEE the content they're approving (current → proposed values, the redirect/link/AEO diffs) inside the inbox, and approve a subset — flag the one title they dislike, approve the other nine — instead of approving a thin task card blind.

**Mutual:** Built fully dark behind `unified-inbox` (the modal is only reachable from the unified inbox card, which only renders when the flag is on); production with the flag off is byte-identical. NOT the "Apply to Website" PR — R3a propagates only the per-item decision/status; the Webflow apply stays the separate R3b follow-on (`appliesOnApprove` unchanged/disabled, D-apply). Per-item flag/subset is APPROVAL-FAMILY ONLY (the client_action family has no typed items). Per-item flag notes are threaded onto each held item's `clientNote` (the modal's `FlaggedItem.note`); the deliverable-level note still rides on whole-deliverable request-changes/decline. Proven by `tests/integration/respond-per-item-propagation.test.ts` (subset approve → unflagged approved + flagged rejected + mirror approved; back-compat approve-all; reject ignores flags; client_action ignores flags) and `tests/unit/DeliverableDetailModal.test.tsx` (deliverable → renderer mapping for each family + per-item flag CTA). See roadmap `unified-deliverables-r3-detail-surfaces`.

---

### 473. Unified Inbox "Apply to Website" (R3b) — explicit post-approve publish step reusing the existing Webflow apply route (built dark — `unified-inbox` flag)

**What it does:** Adds the owner-requested SEPARATE, explicit "Apply to Website" step to the unified inbox: after a client approves a deliverable, an applyable approved deliverable surfaces in a new "Ready to publish" section (and inside `DeliverableDetailModal`) with an "Apply to Website" button that — through a `ConfirmDialog` ("Apply to live site?") — calls the SAME proven legacy route the classic `ApprovalBatchCard` uses, `POST /api/public/approvals/:ws/:batch/apply` (ZERO new apply logic: that route already does the Webflow writes `updatePageSeo`/`updateCollectionItem`/`publishCollectionItems`, the server-side applyability gate, `markBatchApplied`, activity, outcome tracking, and broadcasts). The applyability rule was unified into a single source of truth, `shared/applyability.ts` (`isClientApplyableFields`/`isClientApplyableDeliverableBatch`), keyed on `field`/`targetRef`/`collectionId` — intentionally mirroring the LEGACY ROUTE GATE, NOT the per-item `ClientDeliverableItem.applyable` column (hardcoded `false` for the whole approval family under D-apply); the legacy client predicate (`approvalApplyability.ts`) and the server route gate both now delegate to it (behavior-preserving; the two in-loop `cms-` guards kept as defense-in-depth). A thin server mirror-flip `markDeliverableApplied` (`server/domains/inbox/send-to-client.ts`) moves the unified deliverable to `applied` AFTER the legacy apply (the Webflow write already happened) — idempotent, gated on `APPROVAL_FAMILY_FLAG` (the flag that governs mirror existence, NOT `unified-inbox`), and wrapped in a log-and-swallow try/catch so a mirror miss never fails the apply response. The flip fires ONLY on a FULLY-successful apply (`failed === 0 && appliedIds.length === approved.length`): on a partial/total Webflow write failure the legacy batch keeps the failed items `approved`/retryable, so the mirror stays `approved` (remains in "Ready to publish") and the client can re-click Apply to retry only the failed items — matching the legacy batch's retry semantics. The client `confirmApply` branches on the result body (the legacy route returns HTTP 200 with `applied:0/failed:N` even on a total runtime write failure, so success is never shown unconditionally — FM-2 guard): full success → success toast, partial (`applied>0 && failed>0`) → "N applied, M failed — please retry" error toast, total failure (`applied===0`) → "Failed to apply changes" error toast. The disabled `applyDisabledStub` stays wired (D-apply holds; the mirror-flip does NOT call `adapter.applyDeliverable`). Client: `publicDeliverables.applyApproval` wrapper + `useApplyDeliverable` hook (invalidates the unified inbox key). Scope: `seo_edit` + `audit_issue` get the button; `schema_item`/`schema_plan`/`content_plan_*`/the whole `client_action` family do NOT (the shared predicate excludes them and the route gate rejects them).

**Agency value:** Approve and publish are now two distinct, auditable steps in one inbox — the operator's proven Webflow apply path is reused verbatim (no parallel apply logic to drift), and the unified deliverable mirror flips to `applied` in lockstep so the unified inbox and the legacy approvals surface stay consistent. Held/subset-rejected items are never published (the route's `status==='approved'` filter excludes them).

**Client value:** After approving, the client clicks one clear "Apply to Website" button to push the approved changes live themselves — with a plain-language confirmation and a "N changes applied to your website" toast — instead of waiting for the team to publish. Once applied, the item leaves the inbox (the `applied` status is filtered out of the client list) and the modal auto-closes; the toast is the confirmation.

**Mutual:** Built fully dark behind `unified-inbox` (the surfaces only render when the flag is on) AND `APPROVAL_FAMILY_FLAG` (the mirror-flip); production with both off is byte-identical (the route response is unchanged and no mirror is touched). Proven by `tests/integration/r3b-apply-to-website.test.ts` (applyable `seo_edit` flips the mirror to `applied` with `appliedAt`; a subset-held item is not applied while the mirror still flips because the held item is `rejected`/not in the `approved` set; `schema_item` 400s at the gate with no flip; flag-off keeps the response byte-identical and the mirror untouched; a TOTAL Webflow write failure returns HTTP 200 `applied:0/failed:1`, leaves the legacy item `approved`, and SKIPS the mirror flip — mirror stays `approved`; `markDeliverableApplied` idempotency), `tests/component/InboxTabUnified.test.tsx` (the "Ready to publish" section + Apply button render for an approved applyable `seo_edit`; Apply → ConfirmDialog → mutation fires + success toast; `applied:0/failed:1` → error toast, no success toast), `tests/unit/applyability-shared.test.ts` (incl. the documented divergence — `applyable:false` items still yield `canApply=true` because the predicate mirrors the route, not the column), and `DeliverableDetailModal` Apply-footer cases. See roadmap `unified-deliverables-r3b-apply-to-website`.

---

### 474. Unified Inbox In-Shell Projected Review (R4) — mount the bespoke copy/content review surfaces inside the inbox (built dark — `unified-inbox` flag)

**What it does:** The two PROJECTED unified-deliverable types (`copy_section` / `content_request`) have NO physical `client_deliverable` row and 404 on the uniform `/respond` verbs; R3a rendered them as a read-only "Review →" deep-link that navigated the client OUT to `?tab=reviews`. R4 mounts the PROVEN bespoke review surfaces IN-SHELL so the client reviews + responds without leaving. New `src/components/client/inbox/ProjectedReviewModal.tsx` is a thin full-screen modal wrapper (chrome + Escape-to-close copied verbatim from `DeliverableDetailModal`, INCLUDING the `isContentEditable`/input/textarea/select guard so the `RichTextEditor` + `useAutoSave` inside `PostReviewCard` never lose in-progress typing on a stray Escape; WIDE `max-w-5xl` panel because `ContentTab` renders a `PageHeader` + a 4-column stat grid + the pipeline list). It branches on TYPE (never on deliverable status): `copy_section` → `<ClientCopyReview initialExpandedEntryId={externalRef}/>`; `content_request` → `<ContentTab {...passThrough} initialExpandedRequestId={externalRef}/>` (mounted whole, `ContentTab` self-selects brief actions vs `PostReviewCard` from the LOCAL `ClientContentRequest.status` — both the `client_review` brief and `post_review` post map to canonical `awaiting_client`, so the modal passes ONLY the request id + pass-through props and never inspects status). The ONLY edits to the shared bespoke components are two additive OPTIONAL legacy-safe props (default = current behavior, so legacy `InboxTab` call sites are unchanged): `ClientCopyReview.initialExpandedEntryId` (threaded outer wrapper → inner → seeds `expandedEntryId` `useState`) and `ContentTab.initialExpandedRequestId` (threaded into `useContentRequests`, seeds `expandedContentReq`); `PostReviewCard` is unchanged (reached transitively) and `DecisionCard`'s `onReview` contract is unchanged (only the callback body swapped in `UnifiedInbox`). `UnifiedInbox` now holds `reviewProjected` state; the projected `onReview` opens the modal (reading the raw `d.type` + `d.externalRef` off the deliverable — `normalizeDeliverable` drops both) instead of the deleted `goToReviews` (whose now-dead `navigate`/`betaMode` locals + `useNavigate`/`useBetaMode`/`clientPath` imports were removed — `tsc` `noUnusedLocals` confirms zero unused); the modal mounts near `DeliverableDetailModal`; `UnifiedInboxProps` was extended with the `ContentTab` pass-through props (`Omit<ContentTabProps,'workspaceId'|'setToast'|'initialExpandedRequestId'>`, threaded from `ClientDashboard` → `InboxTab` → here, NOT `hasCopyEntries` which is a `ClientCopyReview` gate). MANDATORY unified-list refresh: the bespoke respond routes broadcast `COPY_SECTION_UPDATED` / `CONTENT_REQUEST_UPDATE` / `POST_UPDATED`, which `UnifiedInbox` did not listen on, so 3 additive flag-ON-only handlers were added, each invalidating `queryKeys.client.unifiedInbox` so after an in-shell respond the card leaves the list and the modal can be dismissed.

**Agency value:** Projected copy/content reviews now resolve "once and for all" inside the unified inbox — the operator's proven copy-pipeline / content-request / posts respond paths are reused verbatim (no parallel review logic to drift), and the unified list refreshes on the bespoke domain events so the inbox stays consistent without the projected respond ever hitting (and 404ing on) the unified `/respond` route.

**Client value:** Instead of being bounced out to a separate Reviews tab, the client clicks "Review →" and reviews + approves/suggests/requests-changes on copy sections, content briefs, and written posts right inside the inbox modal — typing in the post editor is protected from a stray Escape — and the item disappears from the list when they're done.

**Mutual:** Built fully dark behind `unified-inbox` (the modal is only reachable from the unified inbox card, which only renders when the flag is on); production with the flag off AND the legacy `?tab=reviews` surfaces are byte-identical (the additive props default to no-auto-expand). Respond NEVER reaches the unified `/respond` for a projected id (`getDeliverable` returns null → `SendToClientError('Deliverable not found', 404)`). `BetaProvider` is already above the modal (`ClientDashboard` wraps the dashboard in it). Proven by the updated `tests/component/InboxTabUnified.test.tsx` (the two projected tests now assert "Review →" OPENS the in-shell modal and does NOT navigate, with real `externalRef`s), `tests/component/ProjectedReviewModal.test.tsx` (copy → `ClientCopyReview` auto-expanded; content → `ContentTab` auto-expanded + pass-through forwarded; Escape guarded in an editable element, closes elsewhere; backdrop close), and `tests/integration/projected-review-respond-routes.test.ts` (content-request approve emits `CONTENT_REQUEST_UPDATE`, copy approve emits `COPY_SECTION_UPDATED`, and the NEGATIVE `content_request:`/`copy:` projected ids 404 on the unified `/respond`). `tests/integration/broadcast-handler-pairs.test.ts` stays green (the 3 events are already broadcast + handled elsewhere). See roadmap `unified-deliverables-r4-projected-review`.

---

### 475. Unified Inbox Work-Order Track Lane (R5) — read-only "Work in progress" order-progress lane, NO decision verbs (built dark — `unified-inbox` flag)

**What it does:** Surfaces `work_order` deliverables (`kind:'order'`) in the unified client inbox as a dedicated READ-ONLY "Work in progress" TRACK lane — STATUS the client FOLLOWS (work the agency does FOR them), NOT a decision they make (owner decision: "Work orders need to LIVE HERE, but as a read/TRACK lane — NO approve/decline verbs"). (`briefing` stays OUT of the inbox — it is surfaced on the dashboard homepage.) **Critical pre-fix:** work_order rows were NOT client-facing today — their statuses (`ordered`/`in_progress`/`completed`/`cancelled`) were none of `CLIENT_FACING_STATUSES`, so they were filtered out of the client list; R5 needed BOTH a server read change AND a client render path. **Server** (`server/domains/inbox/unified-inbox-read.ts`): a new `CLIENT_FACING_ORDER_STATUSES = {ordered, in_progress, completed}` (`cancelled` excluded) plus a KIND-AWARE predicate `isClientFacingDeliverable(d)` that admits the order-lifecycle statuses ONLY for `kind:'order'` and falls back to the unchanged `isClientFacingDeliverableStatus` for every other kind; `listClientFacingDeliverables` filters on it. Gating on `kind` (not `type`) keeps the order statuses scoped so no review/decision/batch row can leak an `ordered`/`in_progress`/`completed` status into the client list. `isClientFacingDeliverableStatus` stays exported + unchanged (other callers) and `listAllWorkspaceDeliverables` (the admin pane) is untouched (it already returns all statuses). The `work_order` adapter + the create/update dual-write needed NO change — they already produce correct `kind:'order'` rows; they were just being filtered out. **Client** (`src/components/client/inbox/UnifiedInbox.tsx`): a module-level `ORDER_STATUS_CHIP` (Four Laws colors — `ordered`=amber, `in_progress`=blue + spinning `Loader2`, `completed`=emerald; never teal) and an `OrderTrackStepper` over the canonical steps `[ordered, in_progress, completed]` (the completed-step dots/connectors use teal to mark completed PROGRESS, a data affordance consistent with the legacy `OrderStatus` stepper). A `workOrders` `useMemo` (`deliverables.filter(kind === 'order')`) drives a new read-only `<section>` "Work in progress" (mirrors the R3b "Ready to publish" container: `surface-2` + `--radius-signature-lg` with the brand-radius hatch). Each card shows the status chip, title, summary, the stepper (SKIPPED for `completed`), the send age, and a COUNT-ONLY page summary ("N pages") — the raw `payload.pageIds` (raw Webflow ids) are NEVER rendered (CLAUDE.md "never surface raw IDs"). The card is PLAIN markup: it does NOT call `normalizeDeliverable`, does NOT construct a `DecisionCard`, and wires ZERO mutations (`onApprove`/`onFlagWithNote`/`onDecline`/`onReview`/`onApply` — structural verb-safety, there is no verb to wire). Orders never enter `actionable` (`ACTIONABLE_STATUSES` has no order status) so they never reach the `PriorityStrip`. The orders-only empty-state was fixed: the "Nothing needs your attention" line is now guarded on `actionable.length === 0 && workOrders.length === 0` so it does not show when there are orders to track.

**Agency value:** Work orders the agency is fulfilling now live in the same inbox as everything else the client looks at, as a clean read-only progress lane — no risk of a client being shown approve/decline verbs on work that isn't theirs to decide, and no parallel order-status logic (the kind-scoped server predicate keeps order statuses from leaking onto any other deliverable type).

**Client value:** The client can FOLLOW the fix/schema work they paid for right inside the inbox — an amber "Ordered" → blue "In Progress" → emerald "Completed" chip + a 3-step progress stepper + a plain "N pages" scope — without any confusing action buttons on work that the agency, not they, completes.

**Mutual:** Built fully dark behind `unified-inbox` (the section only renders in the flag-ON unified view) + the `unified-deliverables-rest` mirror flag (both default off); no new flag was added. **Intentional, out-of-scope:** the legacy `src/components/client/OrderStatus.tsx` and its route `/api/public/work-orders/:ws` stay LIVE — R5 introduces a SECOND client-facing surface for work-order data (the unified track lane) and intentionally does NOT retire the legacy component/route; reconciliation is a future follow-up. Proven by `tests/integration/work-order-track-lane-read.test.ts` (port 13878: `kind:'order'` in `ordered`/`in_progress`/`completed` INCLUDED, `cancelled` EXCLUDED, CROSS-LEAK guard — a `seo_edit`/`batch` row in `in_progress`/`ordered` EXCLUDED, review-status rows still included, client-portal auth still 401s) and the extended `tests/component/InboxTabUnified.test.tsx` (track section renders title/summary/chip/stepper, ZERO verbs render + the respond/apply mutations are never called, an order is absent from the PriorityStrip, a `completed` order renders the chip but no stepper, orders-only empty-state is suppressed, flag OFF → no section). See roadmap `unified-deliverables-r5-work-order-lane`.

---

### 477. Work-Order Conversation + Explicit `closed` State + Admin Conversation/Close Panel (PRODUCTION data model/routes/admin; client thread dark behind `unified-inbox`)

**What it does:** Adds a client↔team **conversation** to work orders + an operator-only **`closed`** lifecycle state, with a focused admin conversation/close **panel**. **Dark split:** the data model, state machine, migration, store, routes, notifications, the mirror exhaustive-switch arm, and the **admin** panel are PRODUCTION (not flag-gated). Only the CLIENT-facing conversation thread + comment input inside the "Work in progress" track-lane card is DARK behind `unified-inbox`. **State machine** (`server/state-machines.ts`): `WORK_ORDER_TRANSITIONS` `completed → ['closed']` (was `[]`), `closed: []` terminal; `'closed'` added to `WorkOrderStatus`. One-way — NO `completed → in_progress` reopen, NO `closed → *`. **Shared types** (`shared/types/payments.ts`): `'closed'` added to `WorkOrder.status`; optional `closedAt?`; new `WorkOrderComment` + `WorkOrderCommentAuthor`. **Migration** `113-work-order-comments.sql`: `CREATE TABLE work_order_comments` (FK → `work_orders` + `workspaces`, both `ON DELETE CASCADE`) + 2 indexes + `ALTER TABLE work_orders ADD COLUMN closed_at` (the runner detects `ADD COLUMN` and switches to per-statement exec with duplicate-column tolerance, so mixing CREATE + ALTER in one file is safe; append-only). **Store:** `closed_at` lockstep in `server/work-orders.ts` (row/insert/update/mapper/create/update). New sibling module `server/work-order-comments.ts`: `listWorkOrderComments` + `addWorkOrderComment` (returns `null` if the parent order is missing; the read + insert + parent `updated_at` bump are wrapped in `db.transaction(() => …).immediate()` — BEGIN IMMEDIATE takes the write lock up front to avoid the SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT read-then-write race). Comments are NOT a field on `WorkOrder`/`OrderRow` — served OUT-OF-BAND from the deliverable payload. **Routes** (`server/routes/work-orders.ts`): admin PATCH gained `'closed'` in the Zod enum + a `closed` branch (sets `closedAt`, logs `order_closed`; deliberately does NOT re-enter the `completed` page-state/email block); admin comment `POST` (author HARDCODED `'team'`) + `GET`. Client public comment `POST` (`server/routes/public-content.ts`, behind the router-level `requireClientPortalAuth` prefix gate, author HARDCODED `'client'`, **409 when closed**) + public `GET`. New `server/schemas/work-orders.ts` `workOrderCommentSchema` (`.strict()`). **Events:** `WS_EVENTS.WORK_ORDER_COMMENT='work-order:comment'` (server + frontend mirror). **Activity:** `work_order_commented` + `order_closed` added to `ActivityType` + `CLIENT_VISIBLE_TYPES`. **Email:** `notifyTeamWorkOrderComment` (client→team) + `notifyClientWorkOrderComment` (team→client) + 2 templates + throttle-category entries; best-effort, never blocks. **Mirror** (`server/domains/inbox/deliverable-adapters/work-order.ts`): the new `'closed'` case maps to `'cancelled'` (leaves the client lane — ∉ `CLIENT_FACING_ORDER_STATUSES`; raw status preserved in `payload.workOrderStatus`), satisfying the exhaustive `never` guard; `completed` stays `'completed'` (stays in lane). **Admin panel:** `src/components/admin/WorkOrderPanel.tsx` — a focused full-screen modal (mirrors `DeliverableDetailModal` chrome) opened from `WorkspaceHome`'s "purchased fixes awaiting fulfillment" attention item (the item now carries an `onClick` to open the panel instead of navigating to `workspace-settings`). Lists orders → select → order detail + client↔team conversation + reply box (team) + **Start work / Mark complete / Close out** (Close out via a destructive `ConfirmDialog`). No purple (not an AI surface). New `src/api/work-orders.ts` + `src/hooks/admin/useWorkOrders.ts` + `queryKeys.admin.workOrders`/`workOrderComments`. **Client lane (DARK):** the "Work in progress" card's map body is extracted into a module-level `WorkOrderTrackCard` (so a hook can run per card) that renders the existing verb-free card PLUS the conversation thread (public GET) + a comment input (public POST, author `'client'`). The order id is extracted from `sourceRef` (`work_order:<id>`); the input is gated on `workOrderStatus !== 'closed'`. The lane stays verb-free — NO approve/decline/apply; the conversation input is the only interactive element. `queryKeys.client.workOrderComments`. **WS handlers:** `WORK_ORDER_COMMENT` handled on BOTH the admin side (`useWsInvalidation`) and the client side (`UnifiedInbox` wsHandlers) so `broadcast-handler-pairs.test.ts` stays green.

**Agency value:** Operators can hold a real conversation with the client on a paid work order and explicitly CLOSE it out when done — the closed order cleanly leaves the client's conversation lane (one-way, no accidental reopen), and the whole conversation/close flow lives in one focused panel reachable straight from the workspace's "awaiting fulfillment" attention item.

**Client value:** The client can ask questions and get replies on the fix/schema work they paid for, right in the "Work in progress" lane — still with NO confusing approve/decline buttons on work that isn't theirs to decide.

**Mutual:** The model/state-machine/routes/notifications/mirror/admin-panel ship in PRODUCTION; only the client conversation thread + input are dark behind `unified-inbox`. Proven by `tests/unit/state-machines.test.ts` + `state-machine-pure.test.ts` (`completed → closed` legal, `closed` terminal, illegal `in_progress → closed`/`closed → *` rejected), `tests/unit/work-order-comments.test.ts` (insert/null-missing-parent/ordered/scope/`closedAt`), `tests/unit/work-order-adapter.test.ts` (`closed → cancelled`), `tests/integration/work-order-conversation-close.test.ts` (port 13409: admin PATCH→closed sets `closedAt`+activity, admin comment POST author `team` + GET, client public comment forces `'client'` + 409-when-closed + 401-unauth-on-pw-workspace + `.strict()`-rejects-`author`, illegal close 400), and `tests/component/InboxTabUnified.test.tsx` (conversation thread + input render for a non-closed order with a `sourceRef`, posting calls the mutation, the input is hidden when closed, still no decision verbs). See roadmap `work-order-conversation-close`.

---

### 476. Unified Inbox Finalize UX — inline approval card, blind-schema fix, single-item solo review, acted-on items leave + fetch-failure/banner correctness (built dark — `unified-inbox` flag)

**What it does:** Six finalize-UX fixes from the owner's flag-on test pass, all DARK behind `unified-inbox`. **(1) Inline approval-review card** (`src/components/client/inbox/InlineApprovalCard.tsx`, mounted only from `UnifiedInbox`): the approval family (non-projected `kind:'batch'` deliverables with typed `items[]`) previously hid its current→proposed substance behind the full-screen `DeliverableDetailModal` ("View N →"); it now renders the substance INLINE so the client quick-approves in place. It reuses the SAME presentational `ItemDiffRow` from `decision-renderers.tsx`, grouped by page (single page → group sub-header suppressed) like the legacy `ApprovalBatchCard`, with the proven `Map<itemId,note>` → `FlaggedItem[]` per-item flag/hold pattern and the "Looks good — implement N of M →" / "implement K of M →" subset-approve CTA. No "High priority" element (`normalizeDeliverable` hardcodes `priority: undefined`). The card never opens the modal. Wired in `UnifiedInbox.actionable.map` via `const inlineApproval = !projected && d.kind === 'batch' && (d.items?.length ?? 0) > 0;` — capturing EXACTLY the approval family and excluding `client_action` (empty `d.items`, sub-items ride in `payload.items`), `content_decay` (`kind:'decision'`), projected, and `schema_plan` (`kind:'review'`), all of which keep their `DecisionCard` path. NO server/hook/shared-type change — the `FlaggedItem[]` subset-approve contract was already end-to-end. **(1b) `ItemDiffRow` additive `expandable?` prop** (default `false` → existing modal caller's `line-clamp-2` byte-identical): when `true` a "Show full ↓ / Show less ↑" toggle swaps the clamp for `overflow-y-auto max-h-[200px] font-mono` (mirrors the legacy schema preview); `InlineApprovalCard` passes it only for `field === 'schema'` (no inline `JSON.parse`). **(A1) schema_plan reviewed BLIND → fixed:** the uniform-mode "View" gate in `DecisionCard` changed from `itemCount > 1` to `>= 1` (label drops the count when `itemCount===1` → "View →") so single-item modal-routed types get a View affordance — this gate is inside the `uniformActions` block (uniform mode only); the legacy bulk-mode "Review N changes →" path is untouched. `DeliverableDetailModal` gained an additive read-only `schema_plan` branch (renders `payload.pageRoles` + `payload.canonicalEntities` mirroring the legacy `SchemaReviewTab`) so the whole-site schema strategy is reviewable instead of blind; the existing Approve/Request/Decline footer + respond wiring is unchanged. **(2) Single-item "solo" mode:** `ProjectedReviewModal` previously mounted the ENTIRE `ContentTab` (pipeline header + stat grid + topic form + every brief/post) / `ClientCopyReview` (all entries + stats); two additive optional props — `ContentTab.soloRequestId` + `ClientCopyReview.soloEntryId` (legacy mounts pass nothing → byte-identical) — now show ONLY the opened item. `ContentTab` guards all chrome under `{!isSolo && …}`, narrows the mapped list to `visibleRequests`, seeds the brief preview on mount (a self-guarded `loadBriefPreview` effect that is provably inert without a seed), and renders a "Loading review…" fallback when the seeded id isn't in the prop array yet. `ClientCopyReview` filters fetched entries to `visibleEntries`, hides the header/summary-stats/per-blueprint `h3`, and has a solo-not-found branch (entries>0, none match) mutually exclusive with the whole-empty `EmptyState`. The modal panel narrowed `max-w-5xl` → `max-w-3xl` (solo removes the wide chrome). **(C1) acted-on items leave the inbox:** `ACTIONABLE_STATUSES` dropped `'changes_requested'` → `{awaiting_client, partial}` (owner decision "items just leave"): after Request changes the mirror flips to `changes_requested`, which now matches no render bucket → the item leaves the inbox until the team re-sends (server unchanged). **(F1) no false "all caught up" on a failed fetch:** `UnifiedInbox` now destructures the hook's `isError`/`refetch` and, after the loading guard, renders the shared `ErrorState` (empathetic message + Retry) instead of the green empty state. **(F2) no "all caught up" banner above live work:** `PriorityStrip showAllCaughtUp` is now `readyToApply.length === 0 && workOrders.length === 0` so the banner never shows above a populated Ready-to-publish / Work-in-progress lane.

**Agency value:** The client can approve SEO/schema work in a single inline glance (no modal round-trip), the whole-site schema strategy is no longer approved blind, and a content/copy review opens focused on the one item the client clicked instead of the entire pipeline — fewer mis-approvals, faster turnaround, and no false "you're all caught up" hiding live work or masking a failed load.

**Client value:** Approval cards show the actual before/after change inline with per-item hold; long schema JSON has a Show-full toggle; "Request changes" cleanly moves the item out of their queue (the ball is with the team); a failed inbox load shows a retry, not a misleading "all caught up"; and reviews open straight to the item they tapped.

**Mutual:** Built fully dark behind `unified-inbox` — `InlineApprovalCard` mounts only from `UnifiedInbox`, the `DeliverableDetailModal` schema_plan branch + the `DecisionCard` `>=1` View gate are reachable only in uniform mode (unified inbox), and the `ContentTab`/`ClientCopyReview` solo props default undefined so legacy `?tab=reviews` mounts are byte-identical. DecisionCard's legacy bulk-mode + non-uniform `InboxTab` mounts are unchanged (the gate edit lives inside `uniformActions`, gated on `uniformVerbs`). No server, hook, shared-type, or feature-flag change. Proven by new `tests/component/InlineApprovalCard.test.tsx` (per-item rows, no-flag/one-flag subset approve + CTA labels, request-changes/decline note paths, multi-page vs single-page group headers, schema expand-toggle gating), `tests/component/ItemDiffRow.test.tsx` (default `line-clamp-2` regression guard + expandable toggle), `tests/component/ContentTabSolo.test.tsx` (solo narrows to one request, chrome absent, not-found fallback, legacy full chrome + `loadBriefPreview` not called without a seed), `tests/component/ClientCopyReviewSolo.test.tsx` (solo single entry, chrome hidden, not-found, legacy all entries), the updated `tests/component/InboxTabUnified.test.tsx` (approval family → `InlineApprovalCard`, `client_action`/`content_decay` → `DecisionCard`, projected content_request solo hides "Content Pipeline", F2 no false all-caught-up above an order), and the extended `tests/component/ProjectedReviewModal.test.tsx` (solo ids forwarded). Deferred IA polish (completed-order lane decay + `WORK_ORDER_UPDATE` realtime gap, `?tab=` deep-link under the flag, PriorityStrip↔section-heading taxonomy) captured in roadmap `unified-inbox-deferred-ia-polish`.

---

### 477. Unified Inbox UX Batch — submit-a-request chooser, edit-before-approve, resized review modals, styleguide + heading polish (built dark — `unified-inbox` flag)

**What it does:** Six owner-UX-review + audit fixes for the flag-on client inbox, all DARK behind `unified-inbox`. **(1) Submit-a-request chooser:** flag-on clients previously had NO way to initiate a request (the inbox early-returns `<UnifiedInbox>`; `RequestsTab` only mounted on the legacy paths). A persistent "Submit a request" primary button at the top of `UnifiedInbox` (shows even when the queue is empty) opens a centered chooser (`SubmitRequestChooserModal`) offering BOTH paths the owner chose. "Send a request" REUSES the EXISTING task-request form — extracted from `RequestsTab` into a reusable `SubmitRequestForm` (the proven Content Update / Design Change / Bug Report / SEO / New Feature / Other category form → `POST /api/public/requests/:ws`); `RequestsTab` now renders the same component so the legacy tab is byte-identical (additive extraction). "Ask for content" REUSES the existing content-topic + pricing flow: a small topic form (topic / keyword / notes / brief-vs-full-post) calls the SAME `setPricingModal({ source:'client', … })` `ContentTab` uses → the global `PricingConfirmationModal` confirms + `POST /api/public/content-request/:ws/submit`. **(2) Edit-before-approve for SEO titles/metas:** the Webflow apply path already honored the edited value (`item.clientValue || item.proposedValue`); the gap was the unified `/respond` path dropped the edit. Server now threads an optional `editedItems:[{itemId,value}]` from `respondSchema` → `RespondToDeliverableInput` → `RespondToSourceOptions` → `buildItemDecisions` (resolving `legacyItemId` from `itemPayload.legacyItemId`) → `ApprovalItemDecision.clientValue` → `updateItem(..., { status, clientNote, clientValue })` (the ONLY persistence change; apply already prefers `clientValue`). `buildItemDecisions` now builds per-item decisions when EITHER flags OR edits exist (so an edit with no flag still persists). Client adds optional `onEdit`/`editedValue` to `ItemDiffRow` (an inline "Edit" affordance → input/textarea seeded with the proposed value) gated to `seoTitle`/`seoDescription` ONLY (never `schema`) AND behind the non-free tier (`editable = effectiveTier !== 'free'`, the legacy `ApprovalsTab` gate); `InlineApprovalCard` + `DeliverableDetailModal` track edits and forward them as `editedItems` in `onApprove` → `handleRespond`. Three verbs: **Edit** ("I'll fix it and approve"), **Flag** ("hold this one"), **Request changes** ("send the whole thing back"). **(3) Resized review modals:** `DeliverableDetailModal` + `ProjectedReviewModal` (byte-identical full-bleed shells) + the new chooser converted to a centered `w-[90vw] sm:w-[75vw] max-w-[1200px] max-h-[90vh] rounded-[var(--radius-xl)]` panel with a `bg-[var(--brand-overlay)]` backdrop (also fixes styleguide SG-3). **(4) SG fixes:** SG-1 the Ready-to-publish "Approved" pill → `<StatusBadge domain="approval" status="approved" />` (emerald); SG-2 the client's own work-order chat bubble → blue (`bg-blue-500/10 border-blue-500/30`, client context = data). **(5) Cheap polish + headings:** `ItemDiffRow` diff grid `grid-cols-2` → `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2` (stacks on phones); the per-item Flag toggle gained `aria-pressed`; one canonical approve CTA (`approveCtaLabel()` — "Looks good — implement N →" / "… N of M →") unified across `InlineApprovalCard`/`DeliverableDetailModal`/`DecisionCard` uniform mode (legacy single-action + bulk modes unchanged); the actionable section gained a visible "Decisions" heading + subtitle (was aria-only) so the PriorityStrip "Decisions" chip and the section it scrolls to agree, and all three sections gained one-line "what is this" subtitles via a shared `SectionHeading`.

**Agency value:** Flag-on clients can finally start work themselves (free-form request OR content topic) without leaving the inbox, and can correct an SEO title/meta before approving instead of bouncing it back for a round-trip — fewer "send it back" cycles and faster turnaround. The resized centered modals read as focused dialogs, not full-screen takeovers.

**Client value:** A clear "Submit a request" button with two obvious choices; the ability to tweak a proposed title/description and approve in one step; success toast confirmation; cleaner, centered review windows; and section headings that say what each list is for.

**Mutual:** Built fully dark behind `unified-inbox`. The `SubmitRequestForm` extraction leaves the legacy `RequestsTab` byte-identical (same `SectionCard` + fields + handler). `ItemDiffRow` `onEdit`/`editedValue` are optional (default → no editor → byte-identical for existing read-only callers); `editedItems` is optional on every server/client layer (absent → R2/R3 behavior unchanged); the free-tier gate matches legacy `ApprovalsTab`. The approve-CTA change is scoped to uniform mode (unified inbox); DecisionCard's legacy single-action + bulk labels are unchanged. Apply needed NO change. Proven by new `tests/integration/respond-edited-items-propagation.test.ts` (clientValue persists; edit+flag together; back-compat; reject discards edits), `tests/component/SubmitRequestForm.test.tsx`, `tests/component/RequestsTab.test.tsx` (legacy byte-identical guard), extended `tests/component/ItemDiffRow.test.tsx` (editor for seoTitle/seoDescription, NOT schema, readOnly suppresses), `tests/component/InlineApprovalCard.test.tsx` (edit→editedItems, free-tier gate, schema not editable), `tests/unit/DeliverableDetailModal.test.tsx` (centered modal classes, brand-overlay backdrop), and the extended `tests/component/InboxTabUnified.test.tsx` (Submit-a-request button + chooser both options + Send-a-request form + Ask-for-content→setPricingModal, free-tier edit gate, visible Decisions heading + chip↔section vocab agreement). See roadmap `unified-inbox-deferred-ia-polish` (PriorityStrip↔heading taxonomy item now closed).

---

### 468. Unified Opportunity Value Recommendation Model

**What it does:** Replaces the recommendation engine's per-producer magic-constant scores (severity 60/35/15, quick-win 75/55/35, ranking-opp flat 60/40, `impressions/50`, …) with one shared, data-grounded `computeOpportunityValue()` (`server/scoring/`) that every producer branch calls — technical, quick-win, content-gap, striking-distance, decay, freshness, diagnostic. Each rec gets a commensurable Opportunity Value: expected-value-grounded (demand × winnability-vs-authority × commercial intent ÷ effort), confidence-discounted so an ungrounded LLM "high" can never outrank a grounded opportunity, per-workspace outcome-calibrated (realized `attributed_value`), and lifted by a decaying timing boost when an opportunity event fires (competitor overtake, content decay, rank decline). Surfaced coherently to the AI advisor, the client `#1` card (relative ROI badge + a "why this is #1" component breakdown — **never raw dollars**, owner decision), and the public payloads. Built across 7 phase PRs (#1001–#1007): the pure scorer + CTR-curve, persistence (Zod lockstep), Spine-A wiring behind a temporary legacy↔OV selector, shadow divergence logging (`ov_divergence`: legacy-#1 vs OV-#1), real referring-domains authority + outcome calibration + calibrated weights (migrations 108/109), advisor+client coherence, and the `opportunity_events` ledger + detectors (migration 110). The temporary rollout flags were retired in Feature Audit #452 on 2026-06-04, so Opportunity Value and timing events are now part of the canonical runtime path.

**Agency value:** The ranked queue stops surfacing plausible LLM/heuristic guesses and orders by grounded expected value that re-prioritizes when the world changes — and every `#1` is explainable to the client with the exact drivers.

**Client value:** The single "do this next" reflects real winnability + demand + commercial intent + timing with a transparent ROI rationale — not a title fix on a dead page.

**Mutual:** One scoring spine the dashboard, the advisor, and the client all read, self-correcting from real outcomes. The shadow-divergence log remains as a historical validation/debugging surface, but the runtime cutover is complete and no longer waits on a feature-flag flip.

**Event regen single-flight hardening (2026-06-05):** Opportunity-event timing regens and delayed keyword-strategy follow-ons now share one per-workspace runtime authority via `server/recommendation-regen-scheduler.ts`. The 90s `triggerOpportunityRegen()` debounce still collapses event bursts, but its execution now routes through the same single-flight path the follow-on queue uses, preventing overlapping `generateRecommendations()` runs for the same workspace when event bursts and post-update refreshes collide. This is cleanup/hardening only: no public API or UI change, no new recommendation minting, and no feature-flag reintroduction.

**OV Divergence Dashboard (2026-06-02):** The shadow-divergence review that gated the flag flip has a UI. `OvDivergencePanel` (`src/components/admin/OvDivergencePanel.tsx`) is an admin-only, per-workspace diagnostic mounted as a collapsible (default-collapsed) section at the bottom of the Meeting Brief (`MeetingBrief/MeetingBriefPage.tsx`, which receives `workspaceId`). It reads the `ov_divergence` shadow log via `useOvDivergence` → `ovDivergenceApi.list(workspaceId, 50)` (query key `admin.ovDivergence`; admin endpoint `GET /api/ov-divergence/:workspaceId` under the global APP_PASSWORD gate).
