### 455. Schema Plan Durable Generation + Realtime Invalidation Hardening
**What it does:** Moves the last inline schema-plan AI path onto the shared background-job platform. Schema plan generation now has a first-class durable job type (`schema-plan-generation`) with duplicate-active attachment, a worker-owned orchestration path in `server/schema-plan-generation-job.ts`, and a compatibility wrapper for the legacy `POST /api/webflow/schema-plan/:siteId` route that now returns `{ jobId, deprecated: true }` instead of a fully generated plan. The admin `SchemaPlanPanel` now reads the stored plan through React Query, starts regeneration through `useBackgroundTasks`, and refreshes from the persisted plan when the job completes. A new workspace event, `SCHEMA_PLAN_UPDATED`, keeps schema-plan mutations aligned across admin/client caches and intelligence readers for generation, save, send-to-client, activate, delete, and client-feedback transitions.

**Agency value:** Aligns schema planning with the same durable-job and realtime contracts already used for recommendations, page analysis, Local SEO, and other long-running admin workflows. That removes a restart-on-navigation failure mode, gives operators duplicate-run protection, and keeps background-task observability honest.

**Client value:** Client schema review remains on the same public plan endpoint, but schema-plan status changes now propagate more reliably. Admin-side regenerate/save/send flows no longer depend on a single long-lived request staying open.

**Boundaries:** This PR does not change the underlying schema-plan AI contract in `server/schema-plan.ts`, and it does not add a new client-facing schema workflow. The legacy route remains available as a job-start compatibility surface so existing callers do not break during the migration.

**Tests:** Added lifecycle coverage for the new schema-plan job path, including `/api/jobs`, the legacy compatibility wrapper, duplicate-active attachment, background-job metadata/coverage contracts, and schema-plan websocket invalidation contracts. Updated the client workflow smoke path to wait for the persisted plan after starting generation.

**Files:** `server/schema-plan-generation-job.ts` (NEW); `server/routes/jobs.ts`; `server/routes/webflow-schema.ts`; `server/domains/inbox/schema-plan-respond.ts`; `server/ws-events.ts`; `shared/types/background-jobs.ts`; `shared/types/schema-plan.ts`; `src/api/schema.ts`; `src/components/schema/SchemaPlanPanel.tsx`; `src/hooks/useWsInvalidation.ts`; `src/lib/queryKeys.ts`; `src/lib/wsEvents.ts`; `tests/integration/schema-plan-generation-job-mutation-safety.test.ts` (NEW); `tests/contract/background-job-coverage-contract.test.ts`; `tests/contract/mutation-safety-route-matrix.test.ts`; `tests/contract/schema-snapshot-invalidation.test.ts`; `tests/unit/background-jobs.test.ts`; `tests/unit/shared-types-validation.test.ts`; `tests/e2e/client-workflow-smoke.spec.ts`; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 457. C2: Sync AI Routes → Background Job Platform (Review-Pass Fix)

**What it does:** Closes four code-review findings on the C2 migration (PR core-c2-sync-ai-to-jobs):

- **C-1 (CRITICAL):** Added the four missing `switch` cases to `POST /api/jobs` dispatcher (`copy-entry-generation`, `blueprint-generation`, `llms-txt-generation`, `aeo-site-review`). The route-specific `/generate` endpoints already worked, but `useBackgroundTasks.startJob()` posts to `/api/jobs` — these types fell through to the default 400. Now the generic dispatcher delegates to the same job runners. Integration tests added that drive all four types via `/api/jobs` (the real frontend path).

- **I-1 (IMPORTANT):** Implemented the stored-read path for LLMs.txt GET routes. Added migration `130-llms-txt-stored-result` (new `llms_txt_stored_result` table), `storeResult()`/`getStoredResult()` functions, and persistence in the job runner. GET routes now serve the stored blob (returning 404 when none exists) instead of calling `generateLlmsTxt()` inline and triggering a full Webflow crawl on every tab visit.

- **I-2 (IMPORTANT):** Dropped the two unused WS broadcasts (`LLMS_TXT_GENERATED`, `AEO_SITE_REVIEW_COMPLETE`) from their job runners. `useJobProgress` already handles UI refresh via React Query invalidation on job completion; the broadcasts had no frontend consumers.

- **M-1:** Added pr-check rule `C2-migrated generators must not be called synchronously in route handlers` that flags direct calls to `generateLlmsTxt`, `generateBlueprint`, `generateCopyForEntry`, or `reviewSitePages` inside `server/routes/` files. Prevents regression to the sync-AI pattern.

**Boundaries:** No new client-facing surfaces. Migration 130 is additive (no existing rows touched). The AEO GET route (`GET /api/aeo-review/:workspaceId`) is unchanged — it already loads from disk.

**Files:** `server/routes/jobs.ts`; `server/legacy-jobs-runner-registry.ts`; `server/llms-txt-generation-job.ts`; `server/aeo-site-review-job.ts`; `server/llms-txt-generator.ts`; `server/routes/llms-txt.ts`; `server/db/migrations/130-llms-txt-stored-result.sql` (NEW); `scripts/pr-check.ts`; `docs/rules/automated-rules.md`; `tests/integration/c2-ai-to-jobs.test.ts`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 456. Keyword Strategy Generation-Quality Telemetry Persistence
**What it does:** Persists the `GenerationQuality` telemetry computed on every keyword-strategy generation run (pool size, AI-returned count, suppressed count, backfilled count, deterministic-floor hit) into a new workspace-scoped `generation_quality` table (migration 129) via `server/generation-quality-store.ts` — previously log-only, so generation-health history evaporated. Also upgrades the `keyword-site-synthesis` operation (the platform's highest-leverage AI call) from `gpt-5.4-mini`/3000 tokens to `gpt-5.4`/4500 tokens, and re-attaches `siteKeywordMetrics` in the admin strategy GET (paid metrics had silently vanished from the admin UI after the table-strip migration; the guard test now uses divergent fixtures so the omission can never mask again).

**Agency value:** Generation health is now queryable per workspace (was: grep the logs). Silent quality degradation — e.g. the AI returning few keywords and the deterministic floor backfilling the rest — becomes visible and trendable. Strategy synthesis quality rises with the model upgrade.

**Boundaries:** Internal telemetry only — no client-facing surface, no public-portal serialization. Intelligence-slice wiring (Data Flow Rule 6) is deliberately deferred and tracked in `data/roadmap.json:generation-quality-intelligence-wiring`.

**Files:** `server/ai-operation-registry.ts`; `server/keyword-strategy-ai-synthesis.ts`; `server/keyword-strategy-generation.ts`; `server/generation-quality-store.ts` (NEW); `server/db/migrations/129-generation-quality.sql` (NEW); `server/routes/keyword-strategy.ts`; `shared/types/` (StoredGenerationQuality); `tests/contract/keyword-site-synthesis-operation.test.ts` (NEW); `tests/integration/generation-quality-persistence.test.ts` (NEW); `tests/integration/keyword-strategy-admin-assembler.test.ts`.

**PR:** core-features remediation F1 (audit #6 + #7).

### 457. Admin Recommendations Surface (D1 — audit #19)
**What it does:** Replaces the borrowed client `InsightsEngine` component (mounted with `tier="premium"` hardcoded) in `WorkspaceHome` with a purpose-built `AdminRecommendationQueue`. The new admin surface shows: (1) full rec queue across all statuses (pending/in_progress/completed/dismissed) with priority grouping and OV-score sorting; (2) a Dismissed tab with per-rec Un-dismiss action (uses `validateTransition('recommendation', RECOMMENDATION_TRANSITIONS, rec.status, 'pending')` — the `dismissed → pending` backward edge was already defined in the state machine); (3) full OV breakdown per rec including `emvPerWeek` and confidence (admin-only fields stripped on client routes); (4) activity logging on client PATCH/DELETE (`rec_status_updated` / `rec_dismissed`) so admins can see client triage in the activity feed. New admin API endpoints: `GET /api/recommendations/:workspaceId` (full set, all statuses) and `PATCH /api/recommendations/:workspaceId/:recId/undismiss`.

**Agency value:** Admins now see the actual full recommendation queue — including what clients have dismissed — and can restore dismissed items without touching the client portal. The OV breakdown and EMV data help prioritize conversations with clients about which items to act on.

**Client value:** None directly — this is an admin-only surface. Indirectly, admins who can see and act on dismissed recs can have more informed conversations with clients.

**Files:** `src/components/admin/AdminRecommendationQueue.tsx` (NEW); `src/hooks/admin/useAdminRecommendations.ts` (NEW); `src/components/WorkspaceHome.tsx` (mount swap); `server/routes/recommendations.ts` (activity logging + admin endpoints); `server/activity-log.ts` (ActivityType: `rec_status_updated`, `rec_dismissed`); `src/lib/queryKeys.ts` (`admin.recommendations`); `src/lib/wsInvalidation.ts` (RECOMMENDATIONS_UPDATED → admin key); `tests/integration/admin-recommendations-surface.test.ts` (NEW); `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-10-d1-admin-recs-surface.md` (NEW).

**PR:** core-features remediation D1 (audit #19).

## The Issue (Client) P0 — Lane A: outcome/snapshot/segment data substrate (flag-gated, OFF byte-identical)

### 526. GA4 conversion-snapshot substrate + engagement baseline anchor
**What it does:** Net-new daily GA4 key-event conversion snapshot store (`ga4_conversion_snapshots` table, migration 146) modeled on `roi_snapshots`. One row per workspace per pass, `by_event` JSON breakdown parsed via `parseJsonSafeArray` at the read boundary. A 90-day rolling prune is guarded so it NEVER deletes the earliest (engagement-anchor) row, which is back-anchored to `workspace.createdAt` so the client baseline is "since we started," not "since first query." A daily cron (`runGa4ConversionSnapshots`, sibling to `startRankTrackingScheduler`, wired into `startup.ts`) persists current conversions per GA4-connected workspace and ensures the engagement anchor via `ensureEngagementAnchor` (GA4 historical backfill at `createdAt`; degrades honestly — never throws — on GA4 error). `computeOutcomeBaseline` returns the workspace/engagement-start-anchored `OutcomeBaseline` (`establishing` → `ready`); `aggregatePinnedOutcomes` sums ONLY the workspace's pinned `eventConfig` events (label = `displayName`), falling back to all key-events when none pinned.

**Agency value:** Persistent, baseline-anchored outcome history per workspace — the substrate for a dollarized, honestly-labeled client verdict. Reuses the existing `eventConfig` pin model (no new config surface) and the GA4 Data API.

**Client value:** None directly in P0 Lane A (data substrate only; surfaced by Lanes B/C). All new fields are additive/optional and unread on the flag-OFF path.

**Files:** `server/db/migrations/146-ga4-conversion-snapshots.sql` (NEW); `server/db/migrations/147-workspace-segment-config.sql` (NEW — adds `segment_config` + `outcome_value` columns); `server/ga4-snapshots.ts` (NEW — `saveGa4Snapshot`/`loadGa4SnapshotHistory`/`getEarliestGa4Snapshot`); `server/the-issue-outcome.ts` (NEW — `aggregatePinnedOutcomes`/`computeOutcomeBaseline`/`ensureEngagementAnchor`/`backfillGa4SnapshotsFromHistory`); `server/ga4-conversion-snapshot-scheduler.ts` (NEW — `runGa4ConversionSnapshots`/`start*`/`stop*`); `server/startup.ts` (cron wiring); `shared/types/the-issue.ts` (NEW — `IssueVerdict`/`IssueOutcomeCount`/`OutcomeBaseline`/`Ga4ConversionSnapshot`); `shared/types/outcome-tracking.ts` (`OutcomeProvenance`).

### 527. Per-workspace outcome value + segment profile + flag-gated public lockstep
**What it does:** Net-new optional `Workspace.outcomeValue` (`valuePerOutcome`/`unitLabel`/`currency`/`basis` precedence `client_provided`→`agency_estimate`→`ai_enriched`/`monthlyRetainer`) and `Workspace.segmentConfig` (admin segment override), with full six-site column lockstep in `server/workspaces.ts` (`WorkspaceRow`, `rowToWorkspace` via `parseJsonSafe`, `workspaceToParams`, `columnMap`, `updateWorkspace` Pick widening) + Zod schemas (`outcomeValueSchema`/`segmentConfigSchema`). `resolveSegmentProfile(ws)` (sibling to `computeEffectiveTier`) returns one pre-resolved `ResolvedSegmentProfile`: the local/multi axis is deterministic + authoritative from `getClientLocations().length`; the non-local 3-way is advisory (admin-confirmed `segmentConfig`, safe non-local default). `computeROI()` is extended (not forked) to hydrate an additive `ROIData.outcomeVerdict` block ONLY when `the-issue-client-spine` is ON for the workspace AND `outcomeValue` is set AND a GA4 snapshot exists (`provenance` always `estimate_ga4` in P0). Public lockstep: `toPublicWorkspaceView` attaches a flag-gated `segmentProfile`; `outcomeVerdict` rides `GET /api/public/roi` automatically (flag-gated inside `computeROI`). A single AI op `the-issue-lead-value-enrich` (named `callAI` operation, Zod-validated, returns `null` on failure) powers the `ai_enriched` value fallback.

**Agency value:** A per-workspace dollar value powering an honestly-labeled (`estimate_ga4`) outcome verdict, with segment-adaptive resolution that swaps the outcome noun/altitude/inserts without forking the layout. Single AI op for the lowest-confidence value fallback.

**Client value:** None directly in P0 Lane A (consumed by Lanes B/C). Flag-OFF byte-identical: `computeROI` omits `outcomeVerdict`, `toPublicWorkspaceView` omits `segmentProfile`.

**Files:** `shared/types/workspace.ts` (`ClientSegment`/`SegmentConfig`/`ResolvedSegmentProfile`/`Workspace.outcomeValue`/`Workspace.segmentConfig` + `AdminWorkspaceView` mirror); `shared/types/roi.ts` (`ROIData.outcomeVerdict`); `server/roi.ts` (`computeROI` extension); `server/workspaces.ts` (column lockstep + `resolveSegmentProfile`); `server/schemas/workspace-schemas.ts` (schemas); `server/serializers/client-safe.ts` (`PublicWorkspaceView.segmentProfile`, flag-gated); `server/routes/public-portal.ts` (pass flag); `server/routes/stripe.ts` (public ROI comment); `server/ai-operation-registry.ts` (`the-issue-lead-value-enrich`); `server/the-issue-lead-value-ai.ts` (NEW — `enrichLeadValue`); `shared/types/feature-flags.ts` (`the-issue-client-*` family). Tests: `tests/unit/{outcome-provenance,the-issue-types,workspace-segment-types,the-issue-client-flags,resolve-segment-profile,compute-roi-outcome-verdict}.test.ts`, `tests/contract/the-issue-types.test.ts`, `tests/integration/{ga4-snapshots,the-issue-outcome,the-issue-lead-value-ai,ga4-conversion-snapshot-cron,ga4-conversion-snapshot,the-issue-client-roi-public}.test.ts` (all NEW).

**PR:** The Issue (Client) P0 — Lane A + D0 + D2 (pre-dispatch contract commit).

## The Issue (Client) P1a — Lane A: website-native measured-capture substrate (flag-gated, OFF byte-identical)

### 529. measured_action provenance + event-typed outcomes + Webflow form-webhook capture substrate
**What it does:** Graduates the P0 GA4 *estimate* into a *measured* outcome behind a new flag `the-issue-client-measured-capture` (default OFF; OFF = P0 estimate-only spine, byte-identical). (1) **Provenance tier:** adds `measured_action` to `OutcomeProvenance`, ordered between `estimate_ga4` (P0) and `actual_reconciled` (P3); the two P0 closed-union tests are updated to the three-value form. (2) **Event typing:** `OutcomeType` (`form_fill`/`call`/`booking`/`email`/`directions`/`chat`/`other`) + `OutcomeTypeBreakdown` + `IssueOutcomeCount.byType` (required) + `units[].outcomeType` + `EventDisplayConfig.outcomeType` (Zod lockstep). `classifyOutcomeType(eventName, ws)` resolves a type (admin pin override wins over a name heuristic, `other` fallback); `aggregatePinnedOutcomes` now stamps each unit's type + builds `byType`. (3) **Webflow named-lead capture:** `form_submissions` table (migration 148, `UNIQUE(workspace_id, submission_id)` + `INSERT OR IGNORE` → idempotent re-delivery) + a typed store; pure HMAC-verify/parse/resolve helpers (`server/webflow-form-webhook.ts`, timing-safe SHA-256, casing-tolerant payload parse). (4) **Workspace config:** migration 149 adds `webflow_form_webhook_secret` (admin-only), `webflow_form_sources` (JSON `WebflowFormMapping[]`), `conversion_tracking_confirmed_at`, with full six-site `server/workspaces.ts` lockstep + `webflowFormMappingSchema`. (5) **Provenance seam:** `selectOutcomeProvenance(ws, periodFormCount)` returns `measured_action` ONLY when the flag is ON AND setup is confirmed (`conversionTrackingConfirmedAt` + ≥1 pinned typed event, OR ≥1 captured lead in the period) — never claims "measured" the instant the flag flips. `computeROI().outcomeVerdict` consumes it + attaches `outcomeTypeBreakdown` + anonymous `outcomeReconciliation` (GA4 vs captured counts) when ON. The dollar math (count × lead value) is unchanged — only the COUNT's confidence graduates. (6) **WS event + activity:** `WS_EVENTS.FORM_SUBMISSION_CAPTURED` + `'form_submission_captured'` ActivityType for the webhook write (route + frontend handler are Lane C). (7) **PII boundary (D7):** `leadName`/`leadEmail`/`leadMessage` + the webhook secret are admin-only, NEVER serialized into any public/client payload; the public payload carries only anonymous counts + a flag-gated `formCaptureConnected` boolean.

**Feasibility (per audit):** site auto-instrumentation (GTM/script-inject) is NOT built; GA4 measured tier = operator-pinned typed events; Webflow capture = a signed webhook the operator pastes (no programmatic registration). Forms-API polling + named-lead public click-through (P1b) + push/export (P1b/c) + CRM reconciliation (P3) are deferred.

**Agency value:** Turns "~$11,000 estimated" into "23 form fills · 41 calls, measured on your site" using only the website + analytics the agency already accesses — a client-verifiable trust upgrade with no CRM dependency.

**Client value:** None directly in Lane A (data/contract substrate; surfaced by Lanes B/C). Flag-OFF byte-identical: `computeROI` selects `estimate_ga4` and emits no reconciliation/typed-verdict fields even when P1a data is present.

**Files:** `shared/types/outcome-tracking.ts` (`OutcomeProvenance += measured_action`); `shared/types/the-issue.ts` (`OutcomeType`/`OutcomeTypeBreakdown`/`IssueOutcomeCount.byType`/`units[].outcomeType`); `shared/types/form-submission.ts` (NEW — `FormSubmission`/`WebflowFormMapping`); `shared/types/workspace.ts` (`EventDisplayConfig.outcomeType` + `Workspace`/`AdminWorkspaceView` form-capture fields); `shared/types/roi.ts` (`outcomeVerdict.outcomeTypeBreakdown`/`outcomeReconciliation`); `shared/types/feature-flags.ts` (`the-issue-client-measured-capture` flag + catalog + group; `the-issue-client-reconciliation` reserved-for-P3 text); `server/form-submissions.ts` (NEW — store); `server/webflow-form-webhook.ts` (NEW — pure helpers); `server/the-issue-outcome.ts` (`classifyOutcomeType`/`selectOutcomeProvenance`/typed `aggregatePinnedOutcomes`); `server/roi.ts` (provenance seam + reconciliation); `server/schemas/workspace-schemas.ts` (`eventDisplayConfigSchema.outcomeType`/`webflowFormMappingSchema`); `server/workspaces.ts` (six-site lockstep); `server/serializers/client-safe.ts` (`formCaptureConnected`, flag-gated); `server/serializers/admin-workspace-view.ts` (form-source/confirmation fields + secret denied); `server/ws-events.ts` (`FORM_SUBMISSION_CAPTURED`); `server/activity-log.ts` (`form_submission_captured`); `server/db/migrations/148-form-submissions.sql` + `149-workspace-form-capture.sql` (NEW). Tests (NEW): `tests/unit/{outcome-provenance-measured,the-issue-outcome-type,form-submission-types,event-display-config-type,webflow-form-webhook-helpers}.test.ts`, `tests/integration/{form-submissions-store,compute-roi-measured-action,the-issue-roi-public-measured,the-issue-p1a-flag-off}.test.ts`; updated `tests/unit/{outcome-provenance,the-issue-client-flags}.test.ts` + `tests/contract/the-issue-types.test.ts`.

**PR:** The Issue (Client) P1a — Lane A + flag pre-dispatch (Lanes B/C/D build on this substrate).

### 530. The Issue (Client) P1a — capture refactor: Webflow webhook → Data-API POLLING (2026-06-20)
**Status:** Source-swap of the P1a outcome-capture ingest. Owner directive: Webflow's signed-webhook model required an operator-pasted per-workspace secret that didn't match reality, so capture switched from an HMAC webhook receiver to **polling the Webflow Forms Data API**. Contained swap — the storage (`form_submissions`), provenance (`selectOutcomeProvenance`, `conversionTrackingConfirmedAt`), and the entire client render are source-agnostic and unchanged; only the ingest path moved. Still flag-gated behind `the-issue-client-measured-capture` (OFF default, flag-OFF byte-identical).

**What it does:** (1) **Webflow Forms client** — `server/webflow-forms.ts` (NEW): `listWebflowForms(siteId, token?)` (GET `/sites/{site_id}/forms` → forms picker) + `listWebflowFormSubmissions(siteId, formId, token?)` (GET `/sites/{site_id}/forms/{form_id}/submissions`, both via `paginateWebflow`). Two pure helpers re-homed from the retired webhook file: `resolveOutcomeType` + `mapWebflowSubmission` (maps a raw Data-API submission → the existing `FormSubmission` shape, extracting name/email/message PII case-insensitively from the dynamic `formResponse` map; `submissionId` = the Webflow submission id for idempotency). (2) **Daily poller** — `server/webflow-form-poller.ts` (NEW, clones the GA4 conversion-snapshot scheduler): for each workspace with the flag ON + ≥1 tracked form, lists each tracked form's submissions, dedup-ingests via `saveFormSubmission`, and on a genuinely-new insert flips `conversionTrackingConfirmedAt` (first lead, D6), broadcasts `FORM_SUBMISSION_CAPTURED` ({ workspaceId, outcomeType }, PII-free), and logs the PII-free `form_submission_captured` activity. Per-workspace + per-form try/catch (FM-2 honest degradation); flag-OFF workspaces skipped before any Webflow call. Registered in `server/startup.ts`. (3) **Admin router reworked** — `server/routes/the-issue-conversion-tracking.ts` is now PURE ADMIN (no public receiver): `GET conversion-tracking-status` (kept), `GET webflow-forms` (NEW picker), `PUT form-sources` (NEW — save formId→outcomeType mappings + confirm setup when ≥1 mapped). All `requireWorkspaceAccess`, all flag-gated (404 OFF). (4) **Admin UI** — `ClientDashboardTab` flips from "Enable → paste a signing secret" to "Select forms → map each to a lead type → Save"; `ConversionTrackingReadout` reused as-is. `src/api/conversionTracking.ts` swaps `enable/disableFormCapture` for `getWebflowForms` + `saveFormSources`.

**Removed:** `server/webflow-form-webhook.ts` (HMAC verify/parse), the public webhook route + raw-body mount in `server/app.ts`, the public `handleWebflowFormWebhook` handler, the `webflowFormWebhookSecret` column (migration **150** `DROP COLUMN`) + every site that referenced it (type, row interface, `rowToWorkspace`, `workspaceToParams`, `columnMap`, `updateWorkspace` Pick, `DENIED_KEYS`), and the webhook-enable/disable endpoints + the secret copy-once UI. Tests removed: `tests/unit/webflow-form-webhook-helpers.test.ts`; `tests/integration/the-issue-conversion-webhook.test.ts` (replaced by `the-issue-conversion-tracking.test.ts`).

**PII boundary (D7) unchanged:** `leadName`/`leadEmail`/`leadMessage` stay internal — never broadcast/logged or serialized into any public/client payload. `formCaptureConnected` (client-safe boolean) now resolves to confirmed-setup OR ≥1 selected form (no secret).

**Read-before-write deviation:** the directive sketched `/forms/{form_id}/submissions`, but the live Webflow v2 API is site-scoped (`/sites/{site_id}/forms/{form_id}/submissions`) and wraps items in `formSubmissions` / `forms` with `{ limit, offset, total }` pagination — confirmed against developers.webflow.com and implemented accordingly.

**Files:** NEW `server/webflow-forms.ts`, `server/webflow-form-poller.ts`, `server/db/migrations/150-drop-webflow-form-webhook-secret.sql`, `tests/unit/{webflow-forms-helpers,webflow-form-poller}.test.ts`, `tests/integration/the-issue-conversion-tracking.test.ts`. CHANGED `server/routes/the-issue-conversion-tracking.ts`, `server/app.ts`, `server/startup.ts`, `server/workspaces.ts`, `server/serializers/{client-safe,admin-workspace-view}.ts`, `shared/types/workspace.ts`, `src/api/conversionTracking.ts`, `src/components/settings/ClientDashboardTab.tsx`, `tests/component/conversion-tracking-connect.test.tsx`, `tests/integration/{the-issue-p1a-flag-off,the-issue-roi-public-measured}.test.ts`, `tests/unit/row-mapper-completeness.test.ts`. REMOVED `server/webflow-form-webhook.ts`, `tests/unit/webflow-form-webhook-helpers.test.ts`, `tests/integration/the-issue-conversion-webhook.test.ts`.

## The Issue (Client) P1b — Lane B: admin setup-readiness checklist + named-leads readout (flag-gated, OFF byte-identical)

### 531. Admin front-half — per-client setup-readiness checklist with one-click gap deep-links + captured-leads readout
**What it does:** The admin integrity guard that earns a trustworthy outcome number, mounted in the Strategy "The Issue" cockpit spine behind `the-issue-client-measured-capture` (default OFF → byte-identical cockpit). Consumes Lane A's server work; recreates nothing. (1) **Setup-readiness checklist (`IssueSetupReadiness`)** — reads the PII-free `SetupReadinessState` rollup that Lane A added to `GET /api/workspaces/:id/conversion-tracking-status` (`ConversionTrackingStatus.readiness`), renders an "N steps left" / "Setup complete" headline, and RE-MOUNTS the reusable props-only `ConversionTrackingReadout` (integrity rows + Measured/Estimate provenance pill + the step list). Each open gate becomes a one-click deep-link to its fix surface honoring the `?tab=` two-halves contract (the `WorkspaceSettings` receiver reads `tab` from `useSearchParams`): GA4 → `workspace-settings?tab=connections`; outcome value / segment confirm / pin & type events / connect Webflow forms → `workspace-settings?tab=dashboard`; draft POV → no deep-link (the POV editor lives on this cockpit). The lightweight inline explainer is each step's `description`. Mounted slot-0 ABOVE `IssueHeader` (config chrome the operator sees first). (2) **Captured-leads readout (`AdminLeadsReadout` + `useAdminLeads`)** — the operator's view of captured named leads (PII visible — admin surface, `requireWorkspaceAccess`; D7: the guard, not the shape, enforces the boundary), consuming Lane A's paginated `conversionTrackingApi.listLeads` → `{ leads: NamedLeadView[]; total }` via the new `useAdminLeads` hook (React Query, both-halves WS freshness: invalidates on `FORM_SUBMISSION_CAPTURED`, no-op when disabled). The count badge uses the unbounded `total` (header N ≠ page length) and is BLUE (Law 2: data, not actionable). Mounted inside the collapsed "Supporting detail" disclosure (progressive disclosure → cold cockpit stays decision-first). (3) **Deep-linkable steps (`ConversionSetupStep.onClick`)** — an ADDITIVE optional `onClick` on the shared `ConversionSetupStep`; when present an incomplete step renders as a `ClickableRow` deep-link (label hover-steps + trailing arrow), when absent it stays today's static row → the existing P1a Settings consumer (`ClientDashboardTab`) is byte-identical. Converges `ConversionSetupStep` toward `OnboardingStep` (ALIGNMENT #2).

**Agency value:** The operator sees, per client, exactly what gaps stand between an *estimate* and a *measured* number, and fixes each in one click — and sees the named leads being captured, live. The checklist is the credibility substrate the client-facing one-pager (Lane C) depends on.

**Color (Four Laws):** no purple (admin AI only); the leads count badge + lead-type chips are read-only DATA → blue; the connect CTA is an action → teal; the provenance pill is teal when measured.

**Flag-OFF byte-identical:** with `the-issue-client-measured-capture` OFF, the readiness panel + leads readout are not mounted (both hooks no-op — no fetch, no WS subscription); the cockpit is byte-identical (proven by `issue-cockpit-readiness-flag-off.test.tsx`'s real loading→loaded render, which also guards against a Rules-of-Hooks violation in the new conditional mounts).

**Files:** NEW `src/components/strategy/issue/IssueSetupReadiness.tsx`, `src/components/strategy/issue/AdminLeadsReadout.tsx`, `src/hooks/admin/useAdminLeads.ts`; CHANGED `src/components/settings/ConversionTrackingReadout.tsx` (additive optional `onClick` on `ConversionSetupStep` → `ClickableRow` deep-link), `src/components/KeywordStrategy.tsx` (cockpit mount: readiness slot-0 + leads in Supporting detail, flag-gated). Tests (NEW): `tests/component/{admin-leads-hook,admin-leads-readout,issue-setup-readiness,issue-cockpit-readiness-flag-off}.test.tsx`. Consumes (Lane A, not modified): `conversionTrackingApi.listLeads`, `ConversionTrackingStatus.readiness`, `SetupReadinessState`/`NamedLeadView`, `queryKeys.admin.formSubmissions`, `GET /api/workspaces/:id/form-submissions`.

**PR:** The Issue (Client) P1b — Lane B (admin front-half), parallel with Lane C (client export one-pager); shares zero files.

**Client value:** Removes the brittle operator-pasted-secret setup; the agency now just picks which Webflow forms produce leads and the platform pulls named-lead submissions automatically (daily), graduating the client's outcome number from estimate → measured.

---

### 532. The Issue (Client) P1c — weekly EMAIL return-hook (event-gated pull-back; NO SMS)
**What it does:** When something worth returning for happens for a workspace, sends the client ONE consolidated weekly "what came in this week" email that pulls them back to their Issue dashboard. Email only (owner-locked; no SMS). Gated behind `the-issue-client-return-hook` (default OFF; OFF = byte-identical — no email, no marker, no activity). PURELY ADDITIVE on the existing mature email stack (a 7-area infra discovery confirmed nodemailer + queue + throttle + templates + recipient policies all exist) — no new transport, queue, or template framework was built. (1) **Weekly event-gated cron** (`server/return-hook-cron.ts`, clone of `strategy-issue-cron.ts`): polls hourly, at most ONCE per ISO week per eligible workspace (flag ON + `clientEmail` set). Assembles the digest and sends ONLY when ≥1 section has real content — silent on dead weeks. The per-workspace `last_return_hook_sent_week_of` ISO-week marker is the SINGLE authoritative ≤1/week cap, stamped ONLY on a confirmed enqueue (`notifyClientReturnHook` returns false when SMTP is unconfigured / no recipient → no stamp, so it re-sends once deliverable; no-content → no stamp, re-runnable that week). Logs an operator-only `client_return_hook_sent` activity. The `return` throttle category is EXEMPT (`canSend` short-circuits it + it is excluded from the global daily cap): a rolling-window throttle cannot align with a Monday-anchored ISO week, so it would silently drop a legitimate consecutive-week send — the marker, not the throttle, is the cap. (2) **Digest assembler** (`server/the-issue-return-hook.ts`, pure-read): leads captured in the trailing 7 days (count + up to 3 recent names — the client's OWN customers, so names are intentionally present, distinct from the PII-free public count path) + decisions still waiting on the client (pending `ClientAction`s + `pending`/`partial` approval batches). Money is filled by the cron, which owns the `computeROI` write (computeROI saves a snapshot — not pure-read). (3) **Money section is doubly gated** (DR-8/DR-9): present only when there were NEW leads this week AND the verdict is `measured_action`/`actual_reconciled` with a value — never a static weekly restatement of the same number. (4) **Email contract:** new `client_return_hook` `EmailEventType` + `renderClientReturnHook` (3 conditional sections + a "reply to stop" footer + "See your dashboard" CTA, all interpolated values `esc()`-escaped) + `CLIENT_EMAIL_PAYLOAD_RULES` (`outcomeNoun` required) + a new `return` throttle category (`maxPerWindow:1, windowDays:7`, counts toward the global 5/day cap) + `workspace_primary` recipient policy + `notifyClientReturnHook` helper. (5) **Weekly marker:** migration 151 (`last_return_hook_sent_week_of`) + full 7-site `workspaces.ts` lockstep + `markReturnHookSentWeek`.

**Owner decisions (AskUserQuestion):** weekly digest that only sends when something happened (vs per-event / daily); match-existing opt-out + a "reply to stop" footer (vs build a preference center) — the platform-wide email preference center + CAN-SPAM (List-Unsubscribe / suppression / bounce) is deferred to roadmap item `email-preference-center` (NO existing client email has unsubscribe; it's a platform-wide gap, not return-hook-specific).

**Agency value:** Pulls busy / skeptical / churned clients back to the dashboard on a sustainable cadence — "you got 3 new patients + your number crossed $X + 1 thing needs you" — without inbox fatigue (one email/week max, only when there's real news).

**Color (Four Laws):** server-only email template (literal hex, not src/ tokens — the email-templates convention): teal=new customers, blue=measured money, amber=decision-waiting. No purple.

**Flag-OFF byte-identical:** with `the-issue-client-return-hook` OFF, the cron's global gate early-returns (no per-workspace work) and `runReturnHookForWorkspace` returns `skipped` with no email/marker/activity — proven by `the-issue-return-hook-cron.test.ts`.

**Files:** NEW `server/the-issue-return-hook.ts`, `server/return-hook-cron.ts`, `server/db/migrations/151-workspace-return-hook-marker.sql`, `tests/unit/{the-issue-return-hook,the-issue-return-hook-email}.test.ts`, `tests/integration/the-issue-return-hook-cron.test.ts`. CHANGED `shared/types/the-issue.ts` (ReturnHook* types), `shared/types/workspace.ts` (`lastReturnHookSentWeekOf`), `server/workspaces.ts` (7-site marker lockstep + `markReturnHookSentWeek`), `server/email-templates.ts` (type + payload rule + renderer + switch), `server/email-throttle.ts` (`return` category + limit), `server/notification-recipients.ts` (policy), `server/email.ts` (`notifyClientReturnHook`), `server/activity-log.ts` (`client_return_hook_sent`), `server/startup.ts` (`startReturnHookCron`), `scripts/platform-domain-event-definitions.ts`.

**PR:** The Issue (Client) P1c — email return-hook. Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-20-the-issue-client-p1c-plan.md`. Phase-per-PR: staging soak + legacy cutover come after ALL of P1 is built (owner-locked sequence).

Both flags (`client-briefing-v2`, `client-briefing-v2-ai-polish`) were therefore RETAINED (the server still reads them via `isFeatureEnabled`).

### 598. Client dashboard IA v2 — P1 Overview reframe + real month-over-month delta 2026-06-21
**Status:** Built behind a new master flag `client-ia-v2` (default OFF → client dashboard byte-identical to today's spine). First phase of the owner-ratified client IA restructure (tournament + 7-persona gut-check → 4-tab two-speed shell `Overview · Inbox · Results · Deep Dive` + conditional Locations). The verdict-first spine (`the-issue-client-spine` → `TheIssueClientPage`) already implemented most of the reframe (dollar-lead verdict, health ring demoted, predictions cut, two-speed disclosure); P1 added the three remaining persona-driven enhancements.

**What was built:**
- **Real month-over-month delta** — `computeROI()` re-aggregates the same pinned outcomes from the GA4 conversion snapshot closest to 30 days before the latest (15–45-day window, mirrors `computeGrowthPercent`) → new `ROIData.outcomeVerdict.priorPeriodCount` (added to BOTH the shared contract `shared/types/roi.ts` and the duplicated local decl in `server/roi.ts`). `null` when no qualifying prior snapshot — the client shows an honest "establishing" line, never a fabricated delta. Exported pure helper `findPriorOutcomeSnapshot`. Rides the existing public read path `GET /api/public/roi/:id` (no serializer change).
- **Typed outcome breakdown in the hero** (`IssueVerdictHeadline`) — renders `verdict.outcomeTypeBreakdown` ("41 calls · 12 form fills", emerald counts) so the hero is no longer a blended number (dentist gut-check). Plus the MoM clause (↑/↓/→ vs last month, never a fabricated ↑ on a decline).
- **Named-lead list surfaced from the count** — when `client-ia-v2` ON, `IssueYourLeadsSection` moves from the collapsed "Under the hood" block to slot 2.5 (right under the outcome count) so the verifiable "receipts" are one tap away (check-signer gut-check); `namedRecordsAvailable` now reflects reality (`client-ia-v2 && the-issue-client-return-hook`). `!iaV2Enabled` guard prevents a double-mount.

**Flags:** `client-ia-v2` (master, P1→P4) added to `FEATURE_FLAGS` + `FEATURE_FLAG_CATALOG` + the `'The Issue (Client)'` group; roadmap item `client-dashboard-ia-restructure`. (`client-locations` (P5) was declared but never consumed by any `isFeatureEnabled`/`useFeatureFlag` — RETIRED as a phantom flag in Reconcile R12b/C3: removed from the registry + catalog + group, override rows deleted via migration 170, added to pr-check `RETIRED_FLAG_GROUPS`. Zero behavior change.)

Built subagent-per-task → 3-lens scaled review (correctness + flag-OFF parity + test quality): zero Critical, zero real code bugs; 3 Important test-coverage gaps closed + proven non-vacuous.

### 599. Client dashboard IA v2 — P2 collapse to the 4-tab two-speed shell 2026-06-21
**Status:** Built behind `client-ia-v2` (default OFF → nav + every tab byte-identical to today's 11-tab layout). Collapses the client nav to **Overview · Inbox · Results · Deep Dive** (+ a reachable **Settings** home) when the flag is ON.

**What was built:**
- **DeepDiveTab** (new) — the single opt-in depth tab with two named sub-tabs **Analytics** (folds `PerformanceTab` GSC/GA4 + the `HealthTab` fix-list, pinned) · **Rankings** (folds `StrategyTab`). Slot-based: ClientDashboard composes the existing data-heavy tabs (verbatim props) and passes them as ReactNode slots, so prop-threading stays where the data lives. Deep-links its sub-tab via `?sub=` (`tab-deeplink-ok` for the internal bar; it still reads `searchParams`).
- **ResultsTab** (new) — the promoted ROI surface (evergreen `ROIDashboard`).
- **SettingsTab** (new) — account home grouping Brand + (conditional) Plans; `plansSlot` omitted when billing is hidden (betaMode/external).
- **Nav flag-branch** — `buildClientDashboardNav` early-returns the 4-tab set when `clientIaV2` is ON (overview label "Insights"→"Overview"); the OFF return is unchanged. `ClientTab` union + `KNOWN_CLIENT_TABS` + panels gain `deep-dive`/`results`/`settings`. `roi` deliberately resolves to itself (NOT aliased to results — a pure helper can't read the flag; an unconditional alias broke the flag-OFF ROI tab). The spine's strategy CTA lands in Deep Dive › Rankings (`?sub=rankings&tab=rankings`) under IA v2.

**Contracts/tests:** new lockstep contract (`client-tab-panel-lockstep` — KNOWN_CLIENT_TABS == panels keys, robust brace-walk parse, fails on drift); nav-builder flag-ON/OFF byte-identical tests; routing unit tests; 3 component tests; and **`ClientDashboard.iaV2`** — a flag-ON real-render guard that mounts the new tabs through the REAL lazy+Suspense panel path (rule 13), non-vacuous (React 19 silently retries lazy-without-Suspense, so it gates the import + asserts the Suspense fallback/header stay mounted; proven to fail when a panel's wrapper is removed). Built subagent-per-task → 3-lens scaled review: **1 Critical** (flag-OFF roi→results regression) + **2 Important** (strategy CTA interior-tab, missing flag-ON render guard) all fixed.

**Deferred (documented):** landing-in-shell polish for the secondary/legacy nav links (they resolve correctly via retained panels — no dead ends) → built-surface persona review; DeepDiveTab `?sub=` re-sync on already-mounted (unreachable today — sender is cross-tab).

**Verification:** `npm run typecheck` clean; `npx vite build` ok; full `npx vitest run` green; `npx tsx scripts/pr-check.ts` 0 errors; `npm run lint:hooks` 0; `npm run verify:feature-flags` exit 0. Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-21-client-ia-p2-nav-shell.md`. Phase-per-PR: P2 → staging → P3 (content into Inbox). P5 deferred (owner).

**Status:** Built behind `client-ia-v2` (flag-OFF byte-identical — the standalone `content-plan` tab is untouched; the slot is populated only under the flag + a real paid plan). The P2 nav collapse removed `content-plan` from the nav; this re-homes its roadmap (matrix) + per-cell flag surface so a hands-on client can still reach it.

Schema review unchanged. The ONLY orphan was the content-plan roadmap+cell-flag (cells are flag-only; no approve/decline action), so P3 is a *view re-home*, NOT a new Inbox-deliverable projection.

**What was built:**
- `DeepDiveTab` gains an optional `contentPlanSlot` rendered as a **default-collapsed "Content roadmap" `<details>`** under the Rankings sub-tab (reuses the under-the-hood `<details>` chrome; tokens only; teal "View plan" affordance).
- `ClientDashboard` composes `ContentPlanTab` (verbatim props) into that slot when `clientIaV2 && effectiveTier !== 'free' && contentPlanSummary.totalCells > 0` (restores the legacy nav's `isPaid` tier gate — a focused review caught its omission). `ContentPlanTab` is lazy + Suspense-wrapped.

Built controller + 1 test subagent → focused review: 0 Critical, 1 Important (dropped isPaid gate) fixed. Non-vacuousness verified.

### 601. Client dashboard IA v2 — P4 export gate-D (band money by provenance) 2026-06-21 — **completes the single-site IA v2 track (P1–P4)**
**Status:** Behind `client-ia-v2` / `the-issue-client-return-hook` (no behavior change when off). Pre-plan scan confirmed the tournament grafts A (Overview Share/Export = `IssueExportBar`, P1b) + B (per-piece attribution = `ROIDashboard.contentItems` table) were ALREADY built — the only open, release-blocking item was **gate D**: the forwardable one-pager export printed exact `count × rate` dollars for every provenance.

**What was built:**
- New single-source `shared/format-money.ts` (`bandEstimateMoney` = legacy `~$` 2-sig-fig logic, `exactMoney` = whole-dollar, `formatOutcomeMoney` = **band UNLESS `actual_reconciled`**). Client `src/utils/formatNumbers.ts` now delegates to it (ONE banding definition — authority rule).
- `OnePagerExportPayload` gains a pre-resolved `estimatedValueLabel`; the assembler bands the `verdictSentence` (drops the `≈` hedge) + the field; the renderer prints it verbatim. `adSpendEquivalent`/`monthlyRetainer` stay exact (not count×rate).
- Reconciled the pre-existing discrepancy where `fmtOutcomeMoney` wrongly EXACTED `measured_action` (no production caller — the hero uses `resolveProvenanceRender`, which already banded it); now band-unless-reconciled everywhere.

**Gate D verified:** export shows `~$11,000` (banded) for estimate_ga4/measured_action and exact `$11,200` only for actual_reconciled; no exact count×rate dollar (or the `≈` hedge) leaks for a non-reconciled verdict (integration test on the real `GET /api/public/export/:id/one-pager` route; ratio back-out defeated by rounding). Controller + 1 build subagent → 2-lens focused review (gate-D correctness + exact-dollar leak-hunt): **both CLEAN, 0 findings**.

**Verification:** typecheck clean; vite build ok; full `vitest` green; pr-check 0; lint:hooks 0; verify:feature-flags ok; coverage-ratchet 0 failing. Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-21-client-ia-p4-export-gate-d.md`.

**IA v2 single-site track (P1–P4) COMPLETE on staging behind the dark `client-ia-v2` flag.** Remaining: owner validates flag-ON on staging → release to main; **P5 (multi-location)** is a separate deferred session (owner). Tournament `docs/superpowers/audits/2026-06-21-client-ia-tournament.md`.

### 602. Google Business Profile Phase 2A — authenticated connection + location mapping 2026-06-29
**Status:** Built behind `gbp-auth-connection` (default OFF) as the first authenticated GBP phase. `local-gbp` remains the separate DataForSEO aggregate competitor/review benchmark layer.

**What it does:** Adds encrypted first-party Google Business Profile OAuth foundation (`business.manage`), signed single-use OAuth state, account/location discovery via Google Business Profile APIs, normalized storage for connections/accounts/locations/workspace mappings, and admin routes under `/api/google-business-profile/*`. Workspace Settings is the connection source; Business Footprint maps discovered GBP locations to existing `client_locations`; Local Presence Setup/Reviews shows connection and mapping health.

**Boundary:** No raw review sync/storage, no review text, no response drafting, no client approval flow, no reply publishing, no review reply scopes, and no background sync job in Phase 2A. Existing DataForSEO aggregate GBP review panel remains in Local Presence Reviews for benchmark context.

**Tests:** Unit coverage for integration encryption, production missing-key failure, OAuth state tamper/expiry/replay/wrong-intent rejection, and refresh-token preserve/rotate behavior. Integration coverage for auth URL scope/state, invalid callback safety, stored discovery reads, workspace mapping, and cross-workspace access enforcement. Component coverage for Workspace Settings GBP flag-gated connection UI and Local Presence authenticated mapping health.

**Files:** `shared/types/google-business-profile.ts`; `shared/types/feature-flags.ts`; `server/db/migrations/159-google-business-profile-oauth.sql`; `server/integration-encryption.ts`; `server/google-business-profile-oauth-state.ts`; `server/google-business-profile-store.ts`; `server/google-business-profile-client.ts`; `server/routes/google-business-profile.ts`; `server/app.ts`; `server/ws-events.ts`; `src/api/googleBusinessProfile.ts`; `src/hooks/admin/useGoogleBusinessProfile.ts`; `src/components/google-business-profile/*`; `src/components/settings/ConnectionsTab.tsx`; `src/components/settings/BusinessFootprintTab.tsx`; `src/components/local-seo/LocalPresencePage.tsx`; `tests/unit/google-business-profile-security.test.ts`; `tests/integration/google-business-profile-routes.test.ts`; `tests/component/ConnectionsTab.test.tsx`; `tests/component/LocalPresencePage.test.tsx`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`; `data/roadmap.json`.

### 603. Google Business Profile Phase 2B — authenticated reviews read model 2026-06-29
**Status:** Built behind `gbp-auth-reviews` (default OFF) and dependent on `gbp-auth-connection`. `local-gbp` remains the separate DataForSEO aggregate competitor/review benchmark layer.

**What it does:** Adds normalized authenticated GBP review storage and bounded per-location sync from Google's `accounts.locations.reviews.list` endpoint, keyed to workspace GBP mappings from Phase 2A. Local Presence Reviews now shows authenticated connection/mapping state, per-location review health, aggregate rating/count/unanswered/low-rating/newest-review summaries, recent review excerpts, and a manual sync action. Workspace-visible review sync changes broadcast `gbp:reviews_updated` and invalidate the authenticated review and aggregate local GBP query caches.

**Boundary:** Raw authenticated review text is stored for admin triage only and the read model exposes excerpts, not full comments. The copy policy explicitly sets `aiUseAllowed: false`; AI/copy generation, sentiment extraction, response drafting, client approval, and Google reply publishing stay out of scope until later phases. Sync is synchronous and intentionally bounded to one Google review page per mapped location in Phase 2B so no background job is introduced.

**Tests:** Unit coverage for the Google review-list client endpoint, auth header, ordering, default page bound, and optional pagination. Integration coverage for authenticated review summaries through the real admin route. Component coverage for Local Presence Reviews rendering the authenticated review panel while preserving aggregate GBP benchmark context.

**Files:** `shared/types/google-business-profile.ts`; `shared/types/feature-flags.ts`; `server/db/migrations/160-google-business-profile-reviews.sql`; `server/google-business-profile-client.ts`; `server/google-business-profile-reviews-store.ts`; `server/routes/google-business-profile.ts`; `server/ws-events.ts`; `src/api/googleBusinessProfile.ts`; `src/hooks/admin/useGoogleBusinessProfile.ts`; `src/components/google-business-profile/GbpAuthenticatedReviewsPanel.tsx`; `src/components/local-seo/LocalPresencePage.tsx`; `src/lib/queryKeys.ts`; `src/lib/wsEvents.ts`; `src/lib/wsInvalidation.ts`; `src/hooks/useWsInvalidation.ts`; `tests/unit/google-business-profile-reviews-client.test.ts`; `tests/integration/google-business-profile-routes.test.ts`; `tests/component/LocalPresencePage.test.tsx`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`; `data/roadmap.json`.

### 604. Google Business Profile Phase 2C — review response approval + publishing 2026-06-29
**Status:** Built behind `gbp-review-responses` (default OFF), dependent on `gbp-auth-connection` and `gbp-auth-reviews`.

**What it does:** Adds the authenticated GBP review response workflow for synced, unanswered reviews. Admins can draft or regenerate a public reply from Local Presence Reviews, edit it, send it to the client, approve and publish internally, or retry failed publishes. Clients see a dedicated Inbox Reviews approval card that makes clear approval publishes the reply to Google. Approved responses publish through a background `gbp-review-reply-publish` job using Google's v4 review reply endpoint, and successful publishes mirror the reply back onto the local authenticated review row.

**Boundary:** Existing Google replies remain read-only in this phase. Raw review text is used only by the named `gbp-review-response-draft` AI operation for that specific public reply, not by general copy/intelligence generation. No auto-publish, scheduled auto-responder, bulk generation, or reply updates for already-replied Google reviews were added.

**Tests:** Unit coverage for the Google `updateReply` request contract and the DB-backed response workflow approval/publish guard. Integration coverage for the admin review-response workflow route on top of existing GBP route coverage.

**Files:** `shared/types/google-business-profile.ts`; `shared/types/client-deliverable.ts`; `shared/types/background-jobs.ts`; `shared/types/feature-flags.ts`; `server/db/migrations/161-google-business-profile-review-responses.sql`; `server/google-business-profile-review-responses-store.ts`; `server/google-business-profile-review-response-ai.ts`; `server/google-business-profile-review-response-publish-job.ts`; `server/google-business-profile-client.ts`; `server/routes/google-business-profile.ts`; `server/domains/inbox/deliverable-adapters/gbp-review-response.ts`; `server/state-machines.ts`; `server/ws-events.ts`; `src/api/googleBusinessProfile.ts`; `src/hooks/admin/useGoogleBusinessProfile.ts`; `src/components/google-business-profile/GbpReviewResponsesPanel.tsx`; `src/components/client/inbox/GbpReviewResponseApprovalCard.tsx`; `src/components/client/inbox/UnifiedInbox.tsx`; `src/components/local-seo/LocalPresencePage.tsx`; `src/lib/queryKeys.ts`; `src/lib/wsEvents.ts`; `src/lib/wsInvalidation.ts`; `src/hooks/useWsInvalidation.ts`; `tests/unit/google-business-profile-reviews-client.test.ts`; `tests/unit/google-business-profile-review-responses-store.test.ts`; `tests/integration/google-business-profile-routes.test.ts`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`; `data/roadmap.json`.

### 605. MCP P1 — agent action surface: durable handles, per-workspace keys, schema / recommendation / grounded-generation tools 2026-06-29
**Status:** Shipped to staging across PRs #1410 (handles), #1411 (auth), #1412 (tools). No feature flag — server-side `POST /mcp` surface gated by `MCP_API_KEY`. Follows the P0 read-loop closure (#1403–#1408) and the content review loop (#1409: `advance_content_status` + `publish_post`).

**What it does:** Expands the MCP action server from read-only + content-loop into a fuller agent action surface, plus the durability/auth foundations for multi-instance and delegated use.

- **Durable handle store + paid counter (#1410):** `server/mcp/handles.ts` and `server/mcp/paid-call-counter.ts` now persist to SQLite (migration 162: `mcp_handles`, `mcp_paid_call_counts`) instead of process-local Maps, so large-tool-output handles survive restarts and work across instances. API stays synchronous (better-sqlite3) so no caller changed. TTL sweeper (gated off under tests, `.unref()`'d), `MAX_HANDLES` eviction by `rowid`, single-use consume. Paid counter is durable + informational-only (no hard cap); global aggregate under `'__global__'` preserves prior threshold semantics, optional `workspaceId` adds per-workspace attribution.
- **Per-workspace API keys + scope enforcement (#1411):** Additive on top of the retained env `MCP_API_KEY` master key. `mcp_api_keys` table (migration 163) stores sha256-hashed, revocable per-workspace keys (`server/mcp/api-keys.ts`). `mcpAuthMiddleware` resolves master → `scope: 'all'` (unchanged, timing-safe) or a per-workspace key → that workspace's scope + caller label, attached as typed `req.mcpAuth`. Security-critical workspace-scope enforcement in `handleMcpRequest` checks BOTH `workspaceId`/`workspace_id` casings and rejects no-`workspace_id` tools for scoped keys; fail-closed throughout.
- **Schema / recommendation / grounded-generation tools (#1412):** Three new tool categories — `schema-actions` (`generate_schema` / `validate_schema` / `publish_schema`, validate-first guard refuses invalid schema before any live write), `recommendation-actions` (`list_recommendations` + `apply_recommendation` send/throttle/strike via the `recommendation-lifecycle` single-writer API), `content-generation-actions` (`start_brief_generation` / `start_post_generation` — `[Paid API]` grounded jobs delegated to the background job platform).

**Boundary / trust posture:** Capable-but-guarded. Agents can advance + publish *approved* content and apply recommendation lifecycle actions, but cannot approve on the client's behalf, publish an un-reviewed draft, or publish invalid schema. Per-workspace keys are additive infra — only the master key is in use today. Tracked follow-ups: extract a `publishSchemaToLive` domain service (schema-publish parity + remove tool→route import), admin key-minting UI + caller-label activity attribution, per-workspace paid attribution at the call sites.

**Tests:** Unit coverage per category — `mcp-handle-store` (durability/TTL/eviction/paid-counter), `mcp-auth-perkey` (master/scoped/revoked/cross-workspace), `mcp-tools-recommendations` (12), `mcp-tools-schema` (16), `mcp-tools-content-generation` (16); `mcp-server-routing` exercises all new categories in `ALL_TOOLS` order; the `.describe()` contract test covers every new input schema.

**Files:** `server/mcp/handles.ts`; `server/mcp/paid-call-counter.ts`; `server/mcp/api-keys.ts`; `server/mcp/auth.ts`; `server/mcp/server.ts`; `server/mcp/instructions.ts`; `server/mcp/tools/schema-actions.ts`; `server/mcp/tools/recommendation-actions.ts`; `server/mcp/tools/content-generation-actions.ts`; `shared/types/mcp-action-schemas.ts`; `server/db/migrations/162-mcp-handle-store.sql`; `server/db/migrations/163-mcp-api-keys.sql`; `tests/unit/mcp-handle-store.test.ts`; `tests/unit/mcp-auth-perkey.test.ts`; `tests/unit/mcp-tools-recommendations.test.ts`; `tests/unit/mcp-tools-schema.test.ts`; `tests/unit/mcp-tools-content-generation.test.ts`; `tests/unit/mcp-server-routing.test.ts`; `tests/contract/mcp-tool-input-schema-properties.test.ts`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`; `data/roadmap.json`.

### 606. Client redesign "lightup" batch — cross-tab attention badge + verdict count-band MoM consistency + abort-log noise fix 2026-06-30
**Status:** Shipped to staging. Validation-and-polish batch produced while flag-ON smoke-testing the merged-but-dark client redesign (`strategy-the-issue` + `the-issue-client-spine` + `client-ia-v2` + `the-issue-client-measured-capture`) on real seeded workspaces. The full-program flag-ON smoke (client empty/establishing + rich verdict + admin Strategy cockpit) found no crashes; these are the three fixes/increments it surfaced. cda-sc6 carries no new flag (rides `client-ia-v2`). Adversarially reviewed (3 parallel reviewers + remediation) before PR.

**What it does:**
- **cda-sc6 — single cross-tab "needs your attention" badge (`ClientHeader`).** A Bell + teal count in the header that aggregates everything awaiting the client into ONE number — `pendingApprovals + pendingReviews + unreadTeamNotes + copyReviewCount + contentPlanSummary.reviewCells` — and deep-links into the Inbox (`setTab('inbox')`). Renders only when `client-ia-v2` is on, the tier is paid (the v2 Inbox tab is paid-only, so the badge never points at a nav-absent surface), and the count is > 0. The prior per-tab inbox count computation was de-duped into one hoisted value reused by both the per-tab badge and the new global one. Count + link only — deliberately not a revived action strip.
- **Verdict count-band month-over-month consistency (`server/roi.ts` + `TheIssueClientPage`).** The per-type outcome cards previously read "0 / establishing your baseline" while the aggregate verdict headline showed a real month-over-month delta — because the headline used the server's snapshot-based verdict while the count band was assembled client-side from *live* GA4 (which has no history). Now `outcomeVerdict.outcomeTypeBreakdown` carries per-type `baseline`/`priorPeriod` (re-aggregated from the same prior + engagement-baseline snapshots via `aggregatePinnedOutcomes`), and the count band sources from the verdict when present. Headline and per-type cards now reconcile (per-type currents sum to `outcomeCount`, per-type priors sum to `priorPeriodCount`).
- **Abort-log noise fix (`src/api/client.ts`).** `getSafe` no longer logs `AbortError` (React Query cancels in-flight fetches on unmount/refetch); the fallback return is unchanged. Removes console/Sentry noise; affects only `getSafe` callers that forward the abort signal.

**Why it matters to the agency:** Converts already-built-but-dark client-redesign work into shippable, de-risked value, and removes a trust landmine on the redesign's centerpiece (a client seeing "+10 vs last month" in the headline but "0 / establishing" per type would distrust the whole number). The attention badge means a client never has to hunt across tabs to know something needs them.

**Why it matters to clients:** A single, honest, glanceable signal of what's waiting on them, and a verdict whose breakdown reconciles with its headline — consistent, trustworthy reporting rather than contradictory numbers.

**Tests:** `tests/component/client/ClientHeader.test.tsx` (8 badge tests: render, content-plan aggregation, singular/plural, deep-link, flag-off + free-tier + zero-count gating, >99 cap); `tests/unit/compute-roi-outcome-verdict.test.ts` (+1: per-type priorPeriod populated + per-type sums reconcile with the aggregate). Full suite + typecheck + pr-check + lint:hooks + verify:feature-flags green; flag-ON browser smoke on seeded premium/empty workspaces.

**Files:** `src/api/client.ts`; `src/components/client/ClientHeader.tsx`; `src/components/ClientDashboard.tsx`; `src/components/client/the-issue/TheIssueClientPage.tsx`; `server/roi.ts`; `tests/component/client/ClientHeader.test.tsx`; `tests/components/client/client-components.test.tsx`; `tests/unit/compute-roi-outcome-verdict.test.ts`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`; `data/roadmap.json`.
