### 607. The Issue (Client) — "Your next bets" $-forecast band (P1) 2026-06-30
**Status:** Shipped to staging behind `the-issue-client-next-bets` (flipped reserved→active; default OFF / dark). Follow-up phase after the lightup batch (#1427), per phase-per-PR. Adversarially reviewed before PR.

**What it does:** A compact "Your next bets" `SectionCard` in the client verdict spine (above the content plan) that reframes the top ~3 recommended moves as a forward-looking **dollar forecast**. Each bet shows a conservative banded monthly $ range (from the client-safe `ImpactBand.monthlyRangeUsd` — already floored at $25, capped at $2,000, and stripped of the admin-only `emvPerWeek` server-side) plus an **outcome-unit equivalent shown only when it rounds to ≥1** (e.g. "≈ 1–2 new patients/mo", derived from the verdict's `valuePerOutcome`) — a deliberate framing decision so a "$300 ÷ $850 ≈ 0.4 patients" line never reads as noise. A header sums the combined projection ("We project ~$X–Y/mo from your next N moves"), and each bet carries an "Act on this" greenlight (the same `useActOnRecommendation` content-request path the plan uses). Renders nothing when no rec carries a $ band (below the display floor) — never a non-actionable placeholder.

**Architecture:** Pure, unit-tested forecast helper (`nextBetsForecast.ts` — filter to forecastable recs, sort by `opportunity.value ?? impactScore`, sum the bands, compute outcome-units with the ≥1 gate) + a prop-driven `IssueNextBetsSection` component, mounted in `TheIssueClientPage` behind a top-level (Rules-of-Hooks-safe) `useFeatureFlag('the-issue-client-next-bets')` read with a test-override prop. Flag-OFF the band never mounts (byte-identical legacy path).

**Why it matters to the agency:** Turns the vague per-rec `estimatedGain` strings into a credible, conservative $ story for the retainer conversation — "your next three moves project ~$X/mo" — sourced from the same floored/capped band the Health tab already uses, so it never over-promises.

**Why it matters to clients:** A glanceable, honest answer to "what are my next moves worth?" for a check-signer, framed in dollars (and whole outcomes when meaningful), with one-tap "Act on this" — without surfacing internal AI/EMV jargon.

**Tests:** `tests/unit/nextBetsForecast.test.ts` (6 — filter/sort/cap, $ sums, outcome-≥1 gate, no-per-outcome-value, sub-1 per-bet but combined ≥1) + `tests/component/client/IssueNextBetsSection.test.tsx` (6 — populated render, empty/null render, act-on, outcome display + omission, pending). typecheck + lint:hooks + pr-check + verify:feature-flags green; flag-ON browser smoke (no crash, graceful empty, correct gating — demo workspaces seed 0 recs so the populated state is test-covered + data-shape type-verified).

**Files:** `src/components/client/the-issue/nextBetsForecast.ts`; `src/components/client/the-issue/IssueNextBetsSection.tsx`; `src/components/client/the-issue/TheIssueClientPage.tsx`; `shared/types/feature-flags.ts`; `tests/unit/nextBetsForecast.test.ts`; `tests/component/client/IssueNextBetsSection.test.tsx`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`; `data/roadmap.json`.

### 608. Design cleanup sprint — operator + client surface hierarchy & system consolidation 2026-06-30

From the 2026-06-30 six-screen UX review (WorkspaceOverview / WorkspaceHome / The Issue client + cockpit / Content Pipeline / Client shell). 31-item sprint shipped as 7 staging-first PRs (#1429–#1435 + closeout). Sprint spec + audit + plan: `docs/design-cleanup/` and `docs/superpowers/{audits,plans}/2026-06-30-design-cleanup-*`.

**Shared primitives (Wave 0):** `NeedsAttention`/`AttentionRow` (one severity→token worklist; consumed by Command Center + Workspace Home), `Disclosure` (canonical `<details>` chrome; consumed by The Issue cockpit "Supporting detail" + client "Under the hood"), `Menu` (thin `items[]` wrapper over the accessible `Popover`; consumed by Content Pipeline Export + AiSuggested snooze), `SectionLabel` (t-label section kicker), `StatCard` `tone` prop + exported `cardToneClasses(tone)` (one canonical tinted-gradient definition, adopted by the verdict/outcome/ROI cards). All in `src/components/ui/`; `useClickOutside` hook extracted.

**Color/token discipline (Wave 0b):** repo-wide four-laws sweep — mint-on-static-data → blue/emerald, 3 Law-4 purple → blue, ~50 hardcoded hex → `CHART_SERIES_COLORS`, warning/danger surfaces (`InlineBanner` `TONE_STYLES`, trial banners, POV-staleness) → soft tokens.

**Screen hierarchy (Waves 1–5):** Command Center (header cluster + Needs-Attention hero + one hero metric + score-led workspace rows); Workspace Home (setup-task dedup, 4-health→1, 9-hero→3, worklist-first + tabbed sections); The Issue (plan-above-proof spine, single ROI mount, docked send, cockpit Disclosure split); Content Pipeline (stepper↔tabs unified, TabBar/Menu primitives, alert consolidation); Client shell (4-tab two-speed IA behind `client-ia-v2`, panel dedup, one notice region, no title echo).

**Why it matters:** operator screens surfaced everything at equal weight ("the eye has nowhere to land"); the sprint restores hierarchy and consolidates hand-rolled patterns into shared primitives so future screens inherit them.

**Tests/quality:** ~180 new/updated unit + component + contract tests; every PR merged on genuine-green CI (full suite ~4293 component + 1542 contract). Also fixed a repo-wide CI time-bomb (style-exception fixtures that expired at UTC midnight).

**Owner-pending:** `client-ia-v2` default-ON flip (Wave 5 shipped flag-gated, OFF byte-identical — flipping lights up all clients at once, no per-workspace pilot); `SectionLabel` renders `<p>` (3 migrated Issue kickers lost `<h2>` heading semantics — consider a heading element).

**Files:** `docs/design-cleanup/*`; `docs/superpowers/{audits,plans}/2026-06-30-design-cleanup-*`; `src/components/ui/{NeedsAttention,Disclosure,Menu,SectionLabel,StatCard,InlineBanner,TabBar}.tsx`; `src/hooks/useClickOutside.ts`; `src/components/{WorkspaceOverview,WorkspaceHome,ContentPipeline,KeywordStrategy,ClientDashboard}.tsx`; `src/components/client/the-issue/*`; + color-sweep across ~30 components; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`; `BRAND_DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md`.

### 609. Reconcile migration R10-PR1 — cron registry + stop-hook wiring (single execution surface for boot-wired schedulers) 2026-07-01

**Status:** Merged to staging (Reconcile migration Lane I, Task B16). Additive infrastructure — no data changes, no destructive migration. Registers every boot-wired scheduler and known module-level timer in one typed metadata map, wires startup + gracefulShutdown through single registry helpers, and closes the 9 stop-hook gaps the R10 audit found (`docs/superpowers/audits/2026-07-01-reconcile-plan-audit-inventories.json` §R10).

**What it does:** New `server/cron-registry.ts` exports `CRON_METADATA: Record<CronId, CronMetadataEntry>` (label, owning module, interval, description, `stopHook`-gated `start`/`stop` lifecycle pair, `exemptReason` when `stopHook` is false) — mirroring the shape of `shared/types/background-jobs.ts`'s `BACKGROUND_JOB_METADATA`. Registers all 19 boot-wired scheduler subsystems (`email-throttle`, `scheduled-audits`, `approval-reminders`, `monthly-reports`, `backup`, `trial-reminders`, `churn-signals`, `anomaly-detection`, `outcome-crons`, `data-retention`, `intelligence-cache-refresh`, `competitor-monitoring`, `insight-recompute`, `rank-tracking`, `ga4-conversion-snapshot`, `webflow-form-poller`, `briefing-cron`, `strategy-issue-cron`, `return-hook-cron`) plus 3 documented exemptions for module-level timers with no existing stop export (`server/middleware.ts` rate-limit + login-lockout cleanup, `server/ai-deduplication.ts` dedup cache cleanup — fire unconditionally on import, restructuring deferred) and the MCP TTL sweeper (`server/mcp/handles.ts`, already `NODE_ENV=test` guarded). `server/startup.ts` now calls `startAllRegisteredCrons()` instead of hand-listing 19 imports (`initEmailQueue`/`clearTestModeCustomerIds` remain hand-called — one-shot, not recurring). `server/index.ts`'s `gracefulShutdown` now calls `stopAllRegisteredCrons()`, closing all 9 stop-hook gaps in one commit: added `stopBackupScheduler` (didn't exist) and wired 4 previously-orphaned stop exports (`stopGa4ConversionSnapshotScheduler`, `stopWebflowFormPoller`, `stopStrategyIssueCron`, `stopReturnHookCron`) that existed but were never called from shutdown.

**Lazy construction (critical contract):** `cron-registry.ts`'s top-level scope never invokes any `start()`/`stop()` — only `startAllRegisteredCrons()`/`stopAllRegisteredCrons()` do, and only when a caller explicitly invokes them. This matters because `tests/unit/startup.test.ts` historically mocked only 15 of the 20 scheduler modules `startup.ts` imported; eager construction would have started 5 real timers inside vitest. The registry conversion structurally eliminates this hazard: `startup.ts` no longer imports scheduler modules directly at all, so `startup.test.ts` now mocks exactly the 3 modules it imports (`email.js`, `stripe.js`, `cron-registry.js`).

**Deferred / explicitly out of scope (owner-ratified R10 scope cuts):** the throttledUntil-to-cron conversion is DROPPED, not implemented (recommendations auto-resurface on read — see `docs/rules/strategy-recommendations.md`). `server/strategy-issue-cron.ts` and `server/outcome-crons.ts` are registered as metadata entries (fixing their stop-hook gaps) but their internal construction/execution-surface adoption is NOT refactored here — deferred to a follow-up after B6 lands (measured R4×R10 file collision). The 16 hand-rolled interval modules are not migrated onto the shared `createIntervalCron` primitive (`server/weekly-workspace-cron.ts`) — that consolidation is R10-PR2. The new pr-check rule ("new setInterval must register in CRON_METADATA") was evaluated and explicitly deferred — see `docs/rules/background-generation.md` §Cron Registry for the reasoning (a regex/customCheck can't reliably distinguish a boot-wired scheduler from a request-scoped `setInterval`, e.g. the SSE keepalive in `server/routes/keyword-strategy.ts`, without real scope analysis; the census contract test is the primary anti-drift guard). The R10 inventory's claimed `background-jobs-lifecycle.test.ts` gap (missing HTTP 409 non-cancellable pin) was found to already exist at `tests/integration/jobs-routes.test.ts:89` and was not duplicated.

**Why it matters to the agency:** Before this, a scheduler could ship a `stop*()` export that nobody ever wired into shutdown (5 of 19 cases existed) or, worse, ship with no stop export at all (`backup.ts`) — meaning a graceful shutdown could still let an in-flight backup or cron tick fire after `db.close()`. Registry-driven start/stop makes that a structural impossibility for anything in the registry. The census contract test then guards against two regressions: an already-registered scheduler losing its `CRON_METADATA` entry, and `startup.ts` bypassing the registry by importing a scheduler directly. It does NOT auto-catch a genuinely-new scheduler that is nowhere in the hardcoded census inventory — that requires manually extending the inventory, and the auto-catch is the deferred pr-check rule's job (see `docs/rules/background-generation.md` §Cron Registry).

**Tests (15 total across the 3 files):** `tests/unit/cron-registry.test.ts` (7 — metadata completeness, `exemptReason` required when `stopHook:false`, lazy-construction guard via `vi.spyOn` on every entry's `start`/`stop`, `startAllRegisteredCrons`/`stopAllRegisteredCrons` behavior). `tests/contract/cron-registry-census.test.ts` (7 — every inventory module has a `CRON_METADATA` entry; no unknown entries; `cron-registry.ts` imports every inventory module; `gracefulShutdown`/`startSchedulers` wired through the registry helpers; `startup.ts` does not bypass the registry). `tests/unit/startup.test.ts` rewritten (1 test, 3 mocks instead of 15). `npm run typecheck` clean; `npx tsx scripts/pr-check.ts` zero errors.

**Files:** `server/cron-registry.ts` (new); `server/startup.ts`; `server/index.ts`; `server/backup.ts` (new `stopBackupScheduler` export); `tests/unit/cron-registry.test.ts` (new); `tests/contract/cron-registry-census.test.ts` (new); `tests/unit/startup.test.ts`; `docs/rules/background-generation.md`; `CLAUDE.md`; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 610. Reconcile migration R10-PR2 — cancel-gate pin + system-job class (Lane I close-out) 2026-07-02

**Status:** Merged to staging (Reconcile migration Lane I, Task B17 — final ticket). Additive metadata + two defensive filter checks — no data changes, no DB migration.

**What it does:** Re-verified the ratified "job-cancel route must consult `BACKGROUND_JOB_METADATA[type].cancellable`" deliverable and confirmed it was already implemented at HEAD (`server/routes/jobs.ts:193` `getJobCancellationError`, landed `914b68f19` on 2026-06-08) with an existing HTTP 409 pin at `tests/integration/jobs-routes.test.ts:89` — nothing to build there, avoiding a double-implementation. The real remaining work: a **system-job class**. Added `BackgroundJobClass = 'user' | 'system'` plus a `class` field on every `BACKGROUND_JOB_METADATA` entry and a new `isSystemJobType(type)` predicate in `shared/types/background-jobs.ts` (mirrors `isBackgroundJobCancellable`). `class` is metadata keyed by job `type` — not a database column — so it applies retroactively to already-persisted job rows with zero migration. `BACKGROUND_JOB_TYPES.INTELLIGENCE_RECOMPUTE` is the sole `'system'` entry; all other 37 types default to `'user'`. It is created exclusively through `server/intelligence-recompute-job.ts`'s `enqueueIntelligenceRecompute` (flag-gated behind `signal-auto-recompute`, `hasActiveJob`-deduped), which is called from **three** producer paths — so removing any single caller does NOT retire the type: (1) `server/insight-recompute-cron.ts` (daily activity-gated cron), (2) `server/rank-tracking-scheduler.ts` (daily rank-tracking cron, refreshing signals after new rank snapshots land), and (3) `server/keyword-strategy-follow-ons.ts` (an on-mutation follow-on after a strategy/content update settles — not a cron). Because the class is a property of the job TYPE, all three paths are gated identically; which caller enqueued the job is irrelevant to client visibility.

**Where the filter lives:** wired `isSystemJobType` as a second, independent gate — layered on top of, and always overriding, the existing `CLIENT_VISIBLE_JOB_TYPES` allow-list — into both client-visibility surfaces: `server/routes/jobs.ts` `isClientVisibleJob()` (gates `GET /api/public/jobs/:workspaceId`, consumed by `AgencyWorkFeed.tsx` via `useClientWorkFeed.ts`/`useClientJobs`) and `server/websocket.ts` `resolveJobDelivery()` (gates the unauthenticated/client WebSocket delivery path for `JOB_CREATED`/`JOB_UPDATED`). This is the narrowest correct filter point: query/delivery-level, not a UI-level filter, so every current and future client-facing consumer inherits the exclusion automatically. Admin surfaces (`GET /api/jobs`, the authenticated admin WS path, `useBackgroundTasks.tsx`, `NotificationBell.tsx`) needed **no changes** — they were already unfiltered by job type, so "admin bell shows everything" was already true by construction.

**No WS payload/contract-test churn:** because `class` is derived client-side from `job.type` via the shared metadata table (exactly how `cancellable` is already derived), the `JOB_CREATED`/`JOB_UPDATED` payload shape is unchanged — no new field to add, no handler to update, no pinned contract test to touch. `JOB_CREATED`/`JOB_UPDATED` remain in `tests/contract/ws-invalidation-coverage.test.ts`'s `LOCAL_ONLY_EVENTS` unchanged.

**Why it matters to the agency:** without this, enabling the dark-launched `signal-auto-recompute` flag (or any future cron→job conversion) would have silently leaked automated system activity into a client's "What we're working on" feed — a client seeing a mysterious "Refreshing signals" task with no corresponding request from them is a trust problem, not just noise. The defensive double-gate (allow-list AND NOT system-class) also means a future engineer accidentally adding a system type to the client allow-list can't reintroduce the leak.

**Tests:** extended `tests/unit/background-jobs.test.ts` + `tests/unit/shared-types-validation.test.ts` (class/`isSystemJobType` classification, safe `false` default for unknown types), `tests/unit/websocket-job-delivery.test.ts` (system job reaches an authenticated admin, blocked from an unauthenticated/subscribed client even if hypothetically allow-listed), `tests/integration/agency-work-feed.test.ts` (system job absent from `GET /api/public/jobs/:workspaceId`, present via `GET /api/jobs/:id`). `npm run typecheck` clean; `npx tsx scripts/pr-check.ts` zero errors; targeted vitest 15 files / 418 tests green.

**Files:** `shared/types/background-jobs.ts`; `server/routes/jobs.ts`; `server/websocket.ts`; `tests/unit/background-jobs.test.ts`; `tests/unit/shared-types-validation.test.ts`; `tests/unit/websocket-job-delivery.test.ts`; `tests/integration/agency-work-feed.test.ts`; `docs/rules/background-generation.md`; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 611. UI Rebuild F3 — 18 net-new design-system primitives 2026-07-03

**Status:** PR to staging (UI rebuild, phase F3 — first parallel-lane phase after the F1 token + F2a gate foundations).

**What it does:** Builds the 18 design-system components that had no HEAD counterpart, ported from the `hmpsn studio Design System` kit under HEAD conventions (D1: kit `.d.ts` = prop floor, kit `.jsx` = pixel spec, HEAD props win — TS strict, `className`/`id`, tokens-only, `LucideIcon`, no purple, "mint"→"teal" vocabulary). Every file carries the `// @ds-rebuilt` marker so the seven F2a `ds-*` gates apply at error severity. The 18, by target dir: **overlay/** `Drawer`; **ui/** `Avatar`, `IntentTag`, `DataTable`, `MetricTile`, `Sparkline`, `Meter`, `KeyValueRow` (+`DefinitionList`), `BoardColumn` (+`BoardCard`); **forms/** `Segmented`, `LensSwitcher`, `FilterChip`, `SearchField`, `RadioGroup`; **layout/** `AppShell`, `PageContainer`, `Toolbar` (+`ToolbarSpacer`), `GroupBlock`. Kit Toast was NOT rebuilt — HEAD's `src/components/Toast.tsx` `useToast` is the canonical mutation-feedback primitive (adversarial review CP1); its kit-visual restyle is deferred as ledger row `DEF-foundation-004`.

**Production behavior beyond the kit's visual-only spec:** `Drawer` reuses Modal's focus-trap/scroll-lock via a new extracted `ui/overlay/overlayUtils.ts` (Modal refactored to import it, zero behavior change). A pre-committed `useRovingTabindex` hook backs the keyboard-nav bars (Segmented/LensSwitcher/RadioGroup/Toolbar) and DataTable rows — no lane hand-rolled a trap. DataTable rows are real `role="button"` + Enter/Space activatable (the kit's div-onClick was a confirmed launch-blocker); sortable headers cycle `aria-sort`. MetricTile composes `<TrendBadge>`; Sparkline is dependency-free SVG (empty/single-point safe); Meter has `role="meter"` + clamped aria values; RadioGroup/AppShell carry full WAI-ARIA + skip-link.

**Architecture / coordination:** built as a parallel-agent phase — F3.0 pre-committed shared contracts (18 typed stubs, 4 barrels, overlayUtils, roving hook) as a single-author blocking commit; then Lane A∥B (wave 1) and Lane C∥D (wave 2) with exclusive file ownership, no lane touching a barrel or running git (controller committed per-lane). The `ds-per-view-css-block` pr-check rule was narrowed to template-literal CSS + `<style>` only (it had mis-flagged the standard React style-map object every lane writes; review CP4) with regression tests.

**Verification:** all four barrels export the 18 + prop types (the Keywords pilot / F4 import only from barrels; `AppShell`'s `sidebar`/`topbar`/`rail` names are the frozen F4 wiring surface). 88 new component tests across `tests/component/ui/` (keyboard matrices for Drawer/DataTable/Segmented/LensSwitcher/RadioGroup/Toolbar/FilterChip). Static styleguide §05f specimens for all 18 (both themes); a DEV-only `/__ds-harness` route (`src/components/dev/DsHarness.tsx`, `import.meta.env.DEV`) renders the real primitives for the keyboard-walk smoke. Full gates green; scaled-code-review run before merge.

**Files:** `src/components/ui/{Avatar,IntentTag,DataTable,MetricTile,Sparkline,Meter,KeyValueRow,BoardColumn,useRovingTabindex}.tsx/.ts`; `src/components/ui/overlay/{Drawer,overlayUtils}.tsx/.ts` + `Modal.tsx` refactor; `src/components/ui/forms/{Segmented,LensSwitcher,FilterChip,SearchField,RadioGroup}.tsx`; `src/components/ui/layout/{AppShell,PageContainer,Toolbar,GroupBlock}.tsx`; the 4 barrels; `src/components/dev/DsHarness.tsx`; `src/App.tsx`; `tests/component/ui/*` (18 files); `tests/pr-check.test.ts`; `scripts/pr-check.ts`; `public/styleguide.html`; `data/ui-rebuild-deferred-ledger.json`; `CLAUDE.md`; `BRAND_DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 612. Provider readiness — deterministic local evidence and bounded staging verification 2026-07-10

**Status:** Complete in the isolated `codex/ui-visual-parity` worktree; live staging execution remains separately gated on dedicated credentials and staging authority.

**What it does:** Adds explicit `local-fake`, `local-live`, and `staging` environment contracts; a deterministic provider-rich demo workspace whose fake GSC, GA4, PageSpeed, DataForSEO-style SEO evidence, local visibility, SERP, listings, LLM mentions, competitors, ranks, and tracked keywords are available only in development with `LOCAL_FAKE_PROVIDERS=true`; and expanded integration health that distinguishes configuration, connection, verification, provider mode, and capability support for DataForSEO, GSC, GA4, PageSpeed, and GBP. A read-only staging smoke probes those same seams, refuses known production/non-HTTPS origins, redacts output, writes evidence with mode `0600`, and permits at most one paid DataForSEO probe by default.

**Boundaries:** The fixture does not create OAuth tokens, Google account/location mappings, GBP reviews, API keys, or fabricated provider actions. Arbitrary workspaces retain their real authentication and provider behavior. The staging smoke performs no sync, publish, disconnect, or mutation calls, and has not been run because dedicated staging credentials are not present in this worktree.

**Tests:** Environment-profile validation, local fixture identity/gating, fake SEO provider coverage, GSC/GA4/PageSpeed fixture interception, idempotent demo seeding, health-route verification semantics, and staging-smoke safety/call-cap tests. Typecheck and `pr-check` pass.

### 614. Content Performance receiving-home consolidation 2026-07-10

**Status:** `owner-approved` in the isolated `codex/ui-visual-parity` worktree. Joshua explicitly approved the combined Page Intelligence / Content Performance receiving-home bundle on 2026-07-11; it is not staged or shipped.

**What it does:** Makes Content Pipeline Published the flag-on receiving home for Content Performance while preserving the flag-off legacy route and canonical route id. The Published lens now uses the prototype's four-stat/result-card hierarchy with server-authoritative pieces/clicks/position-gain/outcome summaries, strong-win-aware verdicts, keyword/page-type/publish-age context, compact filters/sort, and a production detail Drawer for paired clicks/impressions trend, analytics, brief execution, evidence coverage, and joinbacks. Re-scan is a read refetch; the former nonexistent POST mutation is removed.

**Contracts and safety:** Request and matrix items expose stable `itemId`/`source`. `?tab=published&item=` opens a valid item, clears on close or invalid identity, and never auto-selects another. Matrix trends resolve their planned URL; missing configuration/mapping/data/provider connection returns typed availability instead of a guaranteed 404/opaque 500. Daily GSC trend is intentionally loaded only after opening one readback, preventing an N-item provider fanout from the result list. Public items omit full admin `OutcomeReadback` internals while retaining aggregate win counts and the existing coverage scrub.

**Compatibility:** With `ui-rebuild-shell` on, `/content-perf?item=X` redirects to `/content-pipeline?tab=published&item=X`, and the rebuilt sidebar shows only Pipeline. With the flag off, legacy Content Performance and its nav entry remain byte-compatible. `Page` and `NAV_REGISTRY` are intentionally retained.

**Verification:** Focused component, route, public privacy, matrix/request trend, API, and summary tests pass with hooks lint, typecheck, `pr-check`, and diff hygiene. The isolated worktree server/browser proves redirect/nav/empty-state behavior at 1440×900 and 1600×1000 without touching the existing local server. The copied owner workspace has no published items, so populated result-card order and Drawer states remain fixture-covered rather than fabricated. Evidence: `/tmp/asset-dashboard-codex-visual-parity/content-performance/`. That rendered PASS is supporting evidence only; Joshua's explicit combined-bundle approval is the acceptance source.
