### 675. Recommendation generation integrity S1 — revision-CAS lifecycle preservation 2026-07-13

**Status:** S1 / Phase A complete on the generation-quality program branch. The broader `genq-recommendation-integrity` roadmap item remains pending for S2 lifecycle reconciliation.

**What it does:** Adds a monotonic generation revision to each recommendation set and commits generated output transactionally only when the expected revision still matches. If an operator or client changes recommendation state during generation, the service reloads the latest set, re-finalizes a fresh clone of the already-generated candidates once, and retries without repeating provider work. A second conflict raises a typed failure instead of overwriting the newer decision. Concurrent sends, approvals, dismissals, strikes, and manual recommendation mints therefore remain authoritative. Durable recommendation generation also records internal run provenance and effective-input fingerprints while public recommendation payloads continue to omit those fields.

**Correctness details:** Finalization no longer mutates the frozen candidate snapshot, so retries receive identical inputs. The committed recommendation count — including carried, manual, signal-derived, and auto-resolved rows introduced during finalization — now drives both the success log and `RECOMMENDATIONS_UPDATED` payload rather than the pre-finalization candidate count. Migration 181 adds the revision column without changing public contracts.

**Why it matters:** Long recommendation runs can overlap real operator and client decisions. Version-conditional commits prevent stale generation from silently undoing those decisions, while the bounded retry preserves useful generated work and avoids another paid/provider pass.

**Tests:** Unit coverage pins canonical fingerprints, clone-safe one-retry behavior, typed second-conflict failure, storage revision/provenance mapper and CAS semantics, and finalization immutability. Integration seams cover concurrent send, approval, dismissal, strike, manual mint, public provenance omission, and finalized broadcast counts for carried recommendations.

**Files:** `server/db/migrations/181-recommendation-generation-revision.sql`; `server/domains/recommendations/{generation-service,finalization,storage}.ts`; `shared/types/ai-execution.ts`; focused unit/integration/contract tests; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 679. MCP workspace-alias scope hardening 2026-07-13

**Status:** Complete and merged to `staging` through PR #1529.

**What it does:** Closes a per-workspace MCP API-key isolation bypass caused by the platform supporting both legacy camel-case `workspaceId` and action-style snake-case `workspace_id` tool schemas. Scope enforcement previously inspected the first raw alias present, while the selected tool's Zod schema could consume the other alias and strip the decoy. A crafted call could therefore pass authorization with the key's workspace in one field and operate on another workspace from the field the handler actually read. Authorization now resolves the one workspace argument declared by the called tool's discovery schema, rejects conflicting non-empty aliases for scoped and master keys, and treats fake workspace fields on global tools as non-authoritative.

**Why it matters:** Per-workspace MCP keys are an external action boundary. The authorization layer and the tool handler must agree on the same canonical workspace before any read, write, or paid job starts.

**Tests:** Expanded `tests/unit/mcp-auth-perkey.test.ts` covers representative camel read, snake write, job start, snake cross-workspace access, global-tool decoys, equal aliases, and master-key conflicts. MCP auth/routing focused run: 24 tests pass; typecheck passes.

**Files:** `server/mcp/server.ts`; `tests/unit/mcp-auth-perkey.test.ts`.

### 680. MCP matrix + brand generation P0 shared contracts 2026-07-13

**Status:** P0 complete and merged to `staging` through PR #1530 with green post-merge CI; runtime generation phases remain dark and pending.

**What it does:** Precommits the cross-phase contract layer for two related MCP programs without opening a new endpoint, worker, paid AI call, migration, or rendered generation action. Matrix contracts now distinguish free structural resolution from generation-ready preview; freeze matrix/template/cell source revisions; require exactly one `system:introduction` and `system:conclusion`; carry explicit template/AEO/CTA roles; require exact brief/post generation revisions; address evidence resolution by workspace/matrix/cell; require a non-empty preview-fingerprinted paid selection; and model non-empty retry selections with operator-only replacement authorization. Shared evidence contracts distinguish factual/structural/creative claim kinds; prevent matrix/template labels from grounding factual claims; limit authentic voice samples to non-generated origins; make ready/blocked audit verdicts truthful; prevent auto-passing human-required checks; and cap the shared automatic revision loop at `0|1`.

Brand contracts now mirror the existing onboarding questionnaire as a schema-versioned immutable intake, make `voice_foundation` a singular atomic bootstrap target rather than a normal preset, split full-suite initial and resume targets, require operator-selected authentic anchors and operator finalization, freeze approved-identity approval/content fingerprints, model human-only item decisions, and expose only an approved client-safe summary shape. `naming` is additive durable vocabulary with premium metadata and explicit creative-proposal/no-clearance instructions, but the legacy paid generate/refine service, HTTP input, frontend API, 17-item generator census, rendered tier actions, and stale focus values all keep it closed. Typed onboarding gate evidence freezes voice/review/page approval identity down to matrix run/item/cell and post revisions and records human content authorization/page approval. Two reserved server/workspace flags (`content-matrix-generation`, `brand-deliverable-generation`) default OFF; no composite orchestration flag exists.

**Why it matters:** Later matrix, brand, Inbox, onboarding, job, and MCP phases now consume one source of truth for identity, revision safety, evidence cardinality, voice authority, retry semantics, and truthful completion. This closes the most dangerous class of multi-phase drift before persistence or paid work exists, while keeping today’s production behavior unchanged.

**Tests:** Contract coverage pins exact evidence/status/preset vocabularies, factual-source and authentic-sample exclusions, truthful audit/revision shapes, previewed non-empty paid selection, singular bootstrap policy, proposal semantics, anchored/finalized voice, immutable identity refs, stable system wrappers, exact CAS/retry/evidence ownership, and human onboarding proof. Feature-flag coverage pins both OFF reservations and lifecycle metadata. Brand compatibility coverage pins all 18 durable instructions, naming clearance guardrails, DB/read round-trip, rejection by both legacy paid generate/refine paths before usage, and runtime focus fallback. The prior post-merge CI mismatch is repaired by excluding ignored `.cache` artifacts from full scans, restoring verified-clean parity, and making repository-walk failures loud.

**Files:** `shared/types/{generation-evidence,matrix-generation,brand-intake,brand-generation,brand-content-onboarding,brand-engine,content,feature-flags,index}.ts`; `server/brand-identity.ts`; `server/routes/brand-identity.ts`; `src/api/brand-engine.ts`; `src/components/brand/IdentityTab.tsx`; `src/components/brand-ai-rebuilt/BrandAiSurface.tsx`; `scripts/pr-check.ts`; focused contract/unit/integration/component tests; generation plan/spec/contracts; `docs/rules/{mcp-deliverable-generation,verified-clean-rules}.md`; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 681. MCP canonical runtime registry + caller attribution foundation 2026-07-13

**Status:** R1 implementation complete; no matrix or brand product-generation tool is enabled by this foundation.

**What it does:** Replaces the MCP server's duplicated discovery array, workspace-field lookup, and 13-branch dispatch chain with one fail-fast canonical registry covering all 13 existing tool families and 61 tools. Registry construction validates unique family/tool identity, callable handlers, described object schemas, exact global declarations, one declared workspace field for scoped tools, and error-contract compatibility. Discovery, dispatch, schema census, and scope authorization now consume that same registry. The HTTP server remains a stateless transport adapter with a fresh SDK server/transport per request.

Authenticated execution now carries an immutable `McpToolExecutionContext` with a bounded request correlation ID, tool name, target workspace, and master/per-workspace key identity. A concurrency-safe request-local bridge enriches existing activity writes without changing all 61 handlers, while future durable generation runs are required to persist the explicitly passed snapshot rather than depend on ambient process state. Full admin activity retains the attribution; client activity and shared live payloads remove key identity, and admin-only key lifecycle events use opaque invalidations.

Request correlation is server-owned: the server-generated UUID is used for response attachment, HTTP logging, and MCP activity attribution, while every caller `X-Request-ID` is ignored. Registry rejection logs/results also exclude raw unknown tool names and mismatched workspace values. The production dispatch census pins exact family-handler identity and explicit handled-name manifests for handlers whose early workspace validation could otherwise mask a missing branch.

**Compatibility and safety:** All 61 existing registered handlers retain legacy text errors and behavior. Registry-owned unknown-tool and authorization rejections are deliberately generic so raw caller tool/workspace values cannot be reflected. The runtime also defines a per-tool `json_v1` contract for future tools, sanitizes and bounds safe details, rejects non-compliant handler errors at the registry boundary, and never returns raw arguments, prompts, evidence, secrets, exceptions, or stacks. Scoped keys fail closed when their authenticated key ID/label is absent. Conflicting camel/snake workspace aliases remain invalid for every caller. No migration, background job, AI call, new product tool, endpoint, feature-flag activation, lifecycle change, or UI surface ships in R1.

**Tests:** Shared runtime contract coverage; canonical registry construction, mutation resistance, mixed legacy/JSON policy, production definition-to-handler census, routing, caller request-ID isolation, secret-shaped tool/workspace rejection, scoped-key authorization, stable/redacted error envelopes, AsyncLocalStorage concurrency, durable activity attribution, broadcast/client privacy, and a real minted-key MCP write through `/mcp` into admin plus authenticated public activity reads. Phase verification also covers typecheck, production build, `pr-check` (including full scan), circular dependencies, feature flags, deferred ledger, lexicon, governance, style drift, and the full test suite.

**Files:** `shared/types/mcp-runtime.ts`; `shared/types/index.ts`; `server/mcp/{tool-registry,tool-execution-context,tool-errors,server,instructions}.ts`; `server/mcp/tools/{content,insights}.ts`; `server/mcp/README.md`; `server/{activity-log,request-correlation}.ts`; `server/middleware/request-logger.ts`; MCP registry/routing/auth/error/activity/request-logging contract, unit, and integration tests; generation plan/spec/contracts; `docs/rules/mcp-deliverable-generation.md`; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 682. MCP content-matrix M0 — revision-safe structural resolution foundation 2026-07-13

**Status:** M0 implementation complete on `codex/mcp-content-matrix-m0`; paid page generation, preview/start actions, and background jobs remain absent and pending M1–M4.

**What it does:** Makes content matrices safely addressable from MCP before any model or paid provider work exists. Matrices, templates, and individual cells now carry monotonic source revisions with version-conditional writes: display-only names remain generation-stable, definition/template changes stale the shared source, and sibling cells can commit independently from one source snapshot. Wholesale cell-array PUTs are rejected. Direct cell writes use one target-cell CAS path, preserve state-machine history, and run the established schema side effects; definition regeneration uses matrix CAS and intentionally clears lifecycle/artifact state that is stale for the new definition. The existing planner threads current template/cell revisions through its saves.

The new zero-cost resolver renders deterministic slug/prose substitutions, titles, metadata, headings, page type, schema types, keyword provenance, and a locked block manifest. It blocks unsupported page types, incomplete variable/dimension coverage, unsafe or ambiguous AEO/CTA roles, malformed or Unicode-confusable paths, traversal/full URL/query/fragment input, drifted planned URLs, and canonical collisions against every current/other matrix cell plus locally known workspace pages. Legacy templates return a deterministic upgrade proposal; one exact revision/fingerprint/idempotency-bound HTTP or MCP decision can accept it, while rejection is a no-op and a later edit prevents a false replay.

**MCP and storage:** Adds a dedicated four-tool `content-matrix-actions` family (`list_content_matrices`, `get_content_matrix`, `resolve_content_matrix_cells`, `accept_content_template_generation_upgrade`) using bounded `json_v1` results, moving the production census to 14 families / 65 tools. Matrix and cell pagination are cursor-bound; cell cursors include an exact snapshot fingerprint so cell-only edits cannot mix pages. Migration 184 adds normalized run/item/attempt and append-only evidence ledgers for later phases. The M0 repository only accepts a non-empty already-previewed selection, compares exact persisted selections on replay, validates/serializes secret-free MCP attribution before storage, retains source identities after matrix/template deletion, and keeps evidence supersession inside one workspace/matrix/cell/requirement chain. No M0 route or tool can start a run, call AI, write a brief/post, approve, send, or publish.

**Adversarial closure and verification:** Independent resolver, read/MCP, persistence, and integrated reviews closed snapshot-mixing, cross-matrix collision, Unicode path, prototype-key, keyword-evidence, impossible contract, caller-shaped idempotency, private-evidence, stale whole-array, forged-history, semantic-key-order, cross-chain FK, stalled-response-body, future-page-type rewrite, and public-run credential projection failures. The final independent verdict is clean with no P1/P2 blockers. The broad M0 suite passed 36 files / 776 tests; post-review focused runs passed 287/287 and 105/105. CI-parity local gates passed typecheck, manifest build, the intentional `ContentPlanner` bundle rebaseline, bundle budget, changed and full policy censuses, hooks lint, all 13 quick platform stages, both unit shards (15,254 tests), both component shards (4,995 passed plus one skip/three todo), contract (1,863), and Playwright component tests (10). Changed integration surfaces pass in bounded serial runs; authoritative isolated GitHub integration shards remain the PR gate before merge.

**Files:** `server/domains/content/matrix-generation/*`; `server/content-{matrices,matrix-read-model,templates}.ts`; `server/routes/content-{matrices,templates}.ts`; `server/mcp/tools/content-matrix-actions.ts`; `server/mcp/{tool-registry,instructions,README}.ts`; migration 184; shared matrix/MCP/action contracts; planner/API CAS wiring; focused unit, contract, integration, and component tests; generation plan/spec/contracts; `docs/rules/mcp-deliverable-generation.md`; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 683. MCP brand B0 — durable typed intake and evidence-resolution authority 2026-07-13

**Status:** B0 implementation complete on `codex/mcp-brand-intake-b0`; voice finalization and paid brand-deliverable generation remain absent and pending B1–B3.

**What it does:** Replaces the questionnaire's append-only side channel with one immutable, schema-versioned brand-intake authority. Migration 185 stores fingerprinted revisions, bounded evidence snapshots, explicit compatibility-projection ownership, contiguous predecessor identity, and append-only database guards. The shared domain service normalizes and validates the existing questionnaire, binds identical retries to the same source and actor identity, commits durable state before compatibility projection, and supports A→B→A history without mutating prior revisions. A typed evidence resolution addresses exactly one stable `brand-intake:<fieldPath>` requirement and factual source, uses source-revision plus idempotency concurrency controls, and creates or reuses a superseding revision.

The compatibility projection remains intentionally narrow: it preserves calibrated voice as the canonical authority, maps historical questionnaire fields without duplicate persona/knowledge-base blocks, retains authentic reference provenance, and removes only competitor domains explicitly owned by the prior intake revision. The stranded Brandscript label parser is retired rather than maintained as a second authority.

**HTTP, MCP, and data flow:** The existing public onboarding POST now delegates to the domain service while preserving its response and authentication contract. New authenticated admin GET and evidence-resolution POST routes use the same service. A dedicated `brand-intake-actions` MCP family exposes `get_brand_intake` and `resolve_brand_intake_evidence` with snake-case schemas and bounded `json_v1` errors, moving the canonical registry to 15 families / 67 tools while preserving 61 legacy-text tools. Newly committed mutations emit their distinct activity type, one workspace update, and one intelligence-cache invalidation after commit; exact replays emit no phantom side effects. The shared client questionnaire type now comes from the contract boundary, and raw intake is not added to any client/public serializer.

**Safety boundary:** B0 performs no AI call, provisional voice generation, voice finalization, brand-deliverable run, Inbox delivery, client approval, or publication. Placeholder text and deletion cannot resolve evidence. Client submission, admin pre-seed, MCP mutation, and migration actors remain traceably distinct, and calibrated voice cannot be downgraded by compatibility projection.

**Tests and review:** Contract, migration, domain, HTTP, MCP, public-compatibility, query-key, intelligence-authority, registry, dispatch-census, and instruction coverage pin immutability, revision/idempotency conflict behavior, evidence identity/value pairing, multibyte bounds, rollback, actor/source policy, projection ownership, no-op side effects, cross-workspace access, and absence from public output. The integrated focused suite passed 17 files / 143 tests, and an independent final adversarial review passed 11 files / 88 tests with a SHIP verdict. Typecheck, production build, hooks lint, changed-file policy checks, and all 13 quick platform stages passed. The all-at-once Vitest sweep passed 2,108 files / 29,611 tests; its only failures were three unrelated child-server startup timeouts under full local concurrency, and those exact files then passed serially (3 files / 33 tests).

**Files:** `shared/types/{brand-intake,brand-intake-schemas,mcp-brand-intake-schemas,index}.ts`; migration 185; `server/domains/brand/intake/*`; `server/routes/{brand-intake,public-portal}.ts`; `server/mcp/tools/brand-intake-actions.ts`; `server/mcp/{tool-registry,instructions}.ts`; `server/mcp/README.md`; `server/{brandscript,activity-log}.ts`; intelligence/query invalidation wiring; focused contract/unit/integration tests; generation plan/spec/contracts; `docs/rules/{brand-engine,mcp-deliverable-generation}.md`; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 684. MCP brand B1 — immutable operator-finalized voice authority 2026-07-13

**Status:** B1 implementation complete on `codex/mcp-brand-voice-b1`; paid brand-deliverable generation, audit/revision runs, and Inbox delivery remain absent and pending B2–B3.

**What it does:** Turns brand voice from a mutable status label into a revision-safe authority boundary. Every effective voice edit advances a monotonic profile revision and reopens a calibrated profile; finalization freezes the exact DNA, guardrails, context modifiers, authentic anchor content/evidence, and calibration selections into an immutable versioned snapshot. Readiness distinguishes missing, finalized, and stale authority, so a legacy `calibrated` label or a later edit cannot satisfy downstream generation preflight. Durable manual samples and exact immutable B0 intake samples are eligible anchors; generated calibration variations remain preference evidence only and can never masquerade as authentic source material.

**Operator and MCP boundary:** Authenticated operators may finalize directly or issue a short-lived one-time authorization bound to the exact command, expected revision, idempotency key, and operator identity. Storage retains only the authorization-token digest. MCP exposes `get_brand_voice` and `finalize_brand_voice`, but the MCP key is execution provenance only: it cannot supply, forge, or replace the human finalizer, and its key identity is omitted from tool responses and shared broadcasts. Exact retries replay the immutable result without duplicate activity; stale revisions, cross-workspace tokens, changed commands, expired authorizations, and reused idempotency keys fail closed.

**Authority cleanup and intelligence:** Site-scraped voice drafts no longer emit false `voice_calibrated` outcomes or broadcasts. Generic profile updates cannot set `calibrated`, and approved legacy voice-guideline/tone deliverables cannot compete with a current calibrated Layer-2 profile. The brand intelligence slice now carries finalization readiness, profile revision, and voice version separately from its legacy prompt-compatibility status, enabling B2/M1 consumers to require the exact finalized authority instead of re-deriving it.

**Safety boundary:** B1 performs no paid AI call, provisional foundation generation, brand-deliverable batch, automatic approval, client send, publication, or dashboard pre-seed. Finalization records human authority; it does not claim client approval. Private intake fields, bearer tokens, MCP key identity, raw commands, and stored execution provenance remain behind their intended boundaries. Its truthful internal `voice_calibrated` outcome remains available to admin learnings, but catalog-hidden outcome actions are excluded before every client win/value/digest/annotation cap. Public intelligence and client chat consume a separately recomputed, fail-closed `clientProjection`, never the admin learnings slice. Shared outcome WebSocket events also apply the catalog boundary: hidden actions and mixed aggregate events carry only `{}` invalidation payloads, preventing UUID, score, checkpoint, delta, or internal-count correlation from bypassing the safe read model.

**Tests and review:** Contract, migration, domain, HTTP, MCP, mutation-lifecycle, prompt-authority, intelligence-slice, registry, dispatch-census, and real `/mcp` coverage pin immutable snapshots, content and command fingerprints, CAS/idempotency, token hashing/consumption, authentic-source eligibility, frozen calibration text, reopen-on-edit behavior, legacy compatibility, exact-replay side effects, cross-workspace isolation, sanitized persistence failures, client-safe outcome projection, and response/WebSocket privacy. Two independent final reviews returned SHIP after rerunning 145 and 215 focused tests respectively. Typecheck, production build, hooks lint, PR/full-scan policy checks, exact CI quality gates, and all 13 quick-platform stages passed. The all-at-once Vitest sweep passed 2,118 files / 29,787 tests; its only failures were two unrelated integration-server startup timeouts under full local contention, and both exact files then passed serially (2 files / 8 tests).

**Files:** `shared/types/{voice-finalization,voice-finalization-schemas,mcp-brand-voice-schemas,intelligence,index}.ts`; migration 186; `server/domains/brand/voice-finalization.ts`; `server/{voice-calibration,voice-profile-read-model}.ts`; `server/routes/voice-calibration.ts`; `server/schemas/voice-calibration.ts`; `server/intelligence/brand-slice.ts`; `server/mcp/tools/brand-voice-actions.ts`; MCP registry/instructions/README; voice/MCP/intelligence contract, unit, and integration tests; generation plan/spec/contracts; `docs/rules/{brand-engine,mcp-deliverable-generation}.md`; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 685. MCP brand B2 — grounded, audited brand-suite generation 2026-07-13

**Status:** B2 implementation complete on `codex/mcp-brand-generation-b2`; grouped Inbox/client review and dashboard pre-seeding remain pending B3. The rollout flag remains default OFF.

**What it does:** Adds the first durable paid generation path from an exact immutable B0 intake revision to review-ready brand work. Atomic targets and the identity/messaging, audience, and full-brand-system presets run through one domain service, normalized run/item/command/attempt ledgers, hard provider/token/cost/concurrency ceilings, and version-conditional artifact commits. A full-brand-system start produces only a structured provisional voice foundation, ends its generic job truthfully at `awaiting_voice_finalization`, and creates no durable dependent deliverables. If authentic samples are missing, the grounded foundation is preserved as `needs_attention` with typed requirements rather than inventing voice evidence; resume still requires authentic anchors and an immutable operator-finalized B1 voice version created after that foundation completed. Every direct durable target likewise binds the exact current voice version and fingerprint.

**Grounding and quality:** Deterministic preflight freezes the complete intake-evidence census, exact historical approved-brand inputs, exact voice authority, and the deliverable create/update expectation before paid work. Generate, refine, and audit are named structured operations with dispatcher retries/cache disabled, pessimistic per-dispatch reservations, one explicit OpenAI recovery for a reserved provider failure or invalid candidate, typed claims/evidence/placeholders, naming-clearance guardrails, and sanitized failure records. Rendered factual and inferred claims both require non-empty, fact-capable, non-structural accepted evidence; factual accuracy stays human-required for either classification, and no-hallucination review stays human-required for every generated candidate because a model-authored claim ledger cannot prove its own completeness. Each paid attempt fingerprints the final provider-rendered instruction envelope after JSON-only/research wrappers, provider-specific system placement, and structured response mode; successful provenance must match the successful durable reservation. Acceptance proves the complete required-stage provider closure before creating a job, so JSON escaping or the bounded automatic revision cannot turn accepted work into a guaranteed pre-dispatch failure. The provider instruction ceiling is 40 KiB with a 512-byte acceptance safety margin; base generation is capped at 24 KiB, the raw candidate core and compact refine/audit prompt projection at 4 KiB, the resolved durable candidate at 256 KiB, the full related-candidate context at one 3 KiB identity/fingerprint/excerpt digest, and automatic audit-derived revision direction at 512 bytes. The full-run input ceiling is 5,000,000 tokens. The worker holds a whole-set candidate barrier, persists deterministic and model-audit checkpoints, permits at most one automatic revision, then commits only a final audit-linked `ready_for_human_review` candidate. Attention/blocked candidates remain item-only, never leak through the legacy deliverable approval path, and no path auto-approves, sends, publishes, or claims naming clearance.

**Integrity, recovery, and MCP:** Immutable start/resume/revision commands are accepted before their exact generic job is created, and resume/revision acceptance atomically proves its whole pessimistic command estimate still fits the run's remaining durable budget. Snapshot hydration recomputes the canonical self-hash of the immutable frozen input and verifies every approved-input fingerprint before paid dispatch. Exact replay/restart recovery can repair either a missing job or only the exact `initJobs()` restart tombstone when the current command has no attempts and its own items remain at the accepted boundary; prior-command paid work does not incorrectly block a never-started resume. Recovery keyset-pages beyond 100 active and terminal candidates, preserves both ready and `needs_attention` foundations at the resumable human gate, terminalizes interrupted paid work honestly while preserving completed candidates, and reconciles an already-terminal durable run back into its bounded generic-job result without touching artifacts. A cancelled, interrupted, or ordinarily failed human-directed revision preserves its content/version but returns to retryable `changes_requested`, never an unaudited ready or dead-end failed state; late human edits and approvals still win as explicit conflicts. Four snake-case `json_v1` MCP tools and matching authenticated HTTP routes start, read/page, resume, and revise runs while redacting idempotency keys, raw evidence/prompts, and MCP key identity. HTTP and MCP share the exact signed-cursor shape (two non-empty base64url segments joined by one dot). The source transaction enqueues `command_accepted`, `artifact_committed`, and `command_completed` effect events; durable effect keys make activity writes and MCP paid-call metering exactly-once, while retryable workspace broadcasts and intelligence-cache invalidation are at-least-once. Generic job results contain only the run ID, counts, and terminal status.

**Tests and review:** Contract, migration, preflight, prompt/output, operation, repository, snapshot, service, worker, recovery, HTTP, MCP adapter/registry/scope, job metadata, AI reliability/quality, and real-DB integration coverage pin replay-before-flag, cumulative/remaining budgeting, exact provider-input provenance and pre-acceptance envelope bounds, bounded full-suite audit context and linear UTF-8 truncation, no dependents before authentic voice finalization, exact voice timing, provider reservation, candidate/audit lineage, required-stage failure without phantom artifacts, CAS loss, bounded concurrency, missing/tombstone job repair, recovery pagination and terminal reconciliation, resumable attention foundations, retryable revision failure/cancellation, signed cursors, exactly-once MCP metering, partial outcome truth, public redaction, and summary-only jobs. Independent repository and orchestration reviews were closed before PR publication. A 2026-07-15 real local MCP canary used an immutable Rinse intake, authentic voice samples, client foundation review, operator voice finalization, and an atomic `tone_examples` run. It exposed and closed GPT-5.5 temperature incompatibility, foundation prompt/schema drift, audit truncation, invalid-candidate recovery, and exact per-dispatch reservation accounting. The final canary consumed the exact six-call / 13,000-output-token envelope, completed its single automatic revision and final audit, passed every deterministic guardrail, and stopped truthfully at `needs_attention` for two human-review warnings without committing, approving, sending, or publishing an artifact.

**Files:** migrations 187–188; `shared/types/{brand-generation,brand-generation-schemas,mcp-brand-generation-schemas,background-jobs,feature-flags,index}.ts`; `server/domains/brand/generation/*`; `server/routes/brand-generation.ts`; `server/mcp/{paid-call-counter,tools/brand-generation-actions}.ts`; MCP registry/instructions/README; app/websocket/job/state-machine/AI-operation wiring; B2 contract/unit/integration tests; AI reliability registry; generation rule/brand-engine docs; `data/{roadmap,features}.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 686. MCP brand B3 — grouped Inbox review and approved client brand summary 2026-07-13

**Status:** B3 implementation complete on `codex/mcp-brand-generation-b3`; B0–B3 now close the roadmap item. The rollout flag remains default OFF pending staged activation.

**What it does:** Projects one exact B2 run into the canonical `ClientDeliverable` spine as either an advisory voice-foundation review or a grouped durable brand-suite review. The existing MCP `send_to_client` action accepts an exact run ID/revision/review kind, performs all readiness and placeholder checks before notifying, suppresses exact pending retries, and preserves terminal siblings and child chronology across legitimate same-run revisions. Client decisions are strictly item-scoped: one immediate transaction CASes the frozen BrandDeliverable, generation item/run counts, and Inbox mirror, while foundation feedback remains advisory evidence and never finalizes voice or mutates the provisional B2 item.

**Concurrency and recovery:** Public items carry an opaque review token bound to the exact sent projection even though their stable mirror IDs survive resends. A stale tab therefore cannot approve newer content it never saw; terminal lost-response retries remain idempotent. Honest parent aggregation supports awaiting, partial, all-approved, all-changes-requested, and the valid partial-to-awaiting revision edge. Send and decision boundaries also prove exact source ↔ generation ↔ mirror byte parity, not version identity alone. Team notifications, exactly-once/reconcilable activity, workspace events, and cache invalidation are isolated after commit so one failing effect cannot turn a committed mutation into a false HTTP failure or suppress later effects.

**Client and privacy boundary:** The Inbox renders independent approve/change-request controls with required notes, keyboard/focus recovery, token-aware conflict and unknown-outcome locking, safe prose-only rendering, visible URL fidelity, naming-clearance warning, and explicit foundation copy that approval does not finalize voice. The Brand tab reads a dedicated approved-only summary of durable deliverables plus voice derived only from the current immutable finalization; legacy calibrated or stale edited profiles are omitted. Parent/child mismatch fails closed. Public reads and mutation receipts strip run/source IDs, revisions, evidence, provenance, and actors; client-visible activity strips its private ledger envelope and reviewer identity; every `BRAND_IDENTITY_UPDATED` producer uses one canonical empty shared-channel payload.

**Tests and review:** Service and real-HTTP coverage pins atomic approve/change requests, stale token/source conflicts, workspace isolation, replay truth, activity reconciliation, notification dedupe, partial-state transitions, rollback, public redaction, and shared-event privacy. Contract/unit/component coverage pins adapter projection, MCP target exclusivity, cache/event wiring, grouped status, malformed rich fences, URL visibility, accessibility focus, note preservation across sibling updates, error recovery, and stale-response races. Independent backend and UI adversarial reviews drove the concurrency, privacy, notification, and state-honesty closures before publication. Fresh `origin/staging` and B3 manifest builds measured the complete client-review feature at +6,212 gzip bytes (1,803,670 → 1,809,882 across all budgeted assets); the ratchet updates only the three B3-owned entry chunks and raises the aggregate baseline by that exact measured delta, leaving unrelated entries un-rebaselined.

**Files:** `shared/types/{brand-generation,mcp-action-schemas}.ts`; `server/domains/brand/{review-contracts,review-service,client-summary}.ts`; unified Inbox adapter/read/send services; brand identity CAS/event wiring; deliverable/brand-summary routes; MCP send action/instructions; client Inbox/Brand surfaces, API/hooks, and focused contract/unit/integration/component tests; `docs/rules/mcp-deliverable-generation.md`; `data/{roadmap,features,bundle-budget-baseline}.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 687. Content generation integrity C2 — edit-safe saves, durable provenance, and resource-scoped execution 2026-07-13

**Status:** Complete and merged to `staging` through PR #1537 with required CI and post-merge checks green. This is an unflagged correctness phase: it changes no prompt/content semantics and adds no client-visible provenance fields.

**What it does:** Migration 189 gives briefs, posts, and copy sections a monotonic generation revision plus typed, provider-neutral durable provenance. Generation, regeneration, AI review/fix, operator edits, client decisions, lifecycle transitions, scheduling, revert, send, and publish paths commit against the exact revision they observed. The revision comparison and artifact/lineage/status mutation share one transaction, so newer human state wins without rerunning paid work. Full brief replacement cannot leave an orphan successor, copy generation protects the complete stable section census instead of deleting protected rows, and no-op writes leave revisions unchanged and emit no mutation side effects. Linked content-request send/review decisions advance the affected artifact authority atomically. Admin, client, job, and MCP callers carry the same numeric revision contract instead of relying on a preflight hash check with a race window.

**Provenance, source authority, and execution:** Adopted output records the accepted run, named operation, provider/model, exact effective-input fingerprint, execution-chain identity, and bounded ordered contributing executions; rejected or superseded attempts remain trace evidence rather than artifact provenance. For MCP prose generated outside the server, the platform fingerprints the exact complete server-prepared context and truthfully stores `provider: 'external'` plus `model: 'unreported'`—it never fabricates attribution. Optional parent content requests are selected during `prepare_brief_context` / `prepare_post_context`, with `{id, updatedAt}`, target-keyword, and brief-lineage authority frozen into that exact fingerprint and one-time handle. Lifecycle eligibility is checked before context work, again in the final prepared snapshot, and during adoption, so impossible transitions fail before external paid work and later drift cannot be adopted. Save-time parent IDs can only repeat the prepared identity, never introduce or replace it. Adoption atomically re-reads parent/source authority, consumes the handle, inserts the artifact, and links the request; correctable payload validation or a transient write failure can reuse the rolled-back handle, while source/parent/lifecycle/durable-link drift requires a fresh prepare rather than rebasing a stale authorization. Direct send actions likewise treat an explicit request ID as exact workspace-scoped authority: a missing or cross-workspace request aborts without creating or retargeting review work. Saved brief/post handles are consumed inside the same transaction as that durable send, so a failed send retains the handle and a successful retry consumes it exactly once. Post adoption also requires the exact prepared brief generation revision. Migration 191 carries provenance into post-version snapshots so revert restores the snapshot's attribution instead of borrowing newer provenance. Generic jobs support normalized atomic resource claims: overlapping brief/post/copy work returns a deterministic conflict before provider work, while disjoint resources in the same workspace can run concurrently. Multi-entry copy batches claim the whole selection atomically; done/error, drained cancellation, restart recovery, and final manual-worker drain release claims without reopening a duplicate-spend window, including after both terminal bookkeeping writes fail. Detached C2 workers observe and log their outer promise rejection with job/resource identity instead of leaking an unhandled process rejection.

**Edit, completion, and privacy boundaries:** Independent rich-text debouncers share one artifact-scoped serialized save authority. Only accepted responses advance it; an externally observed revision/review-token change invalidates stale queued work, failures are never retried invisibly, and a failed flush blocks Done/approval/change-request/context-switch actions until explicit recovery. Explicit retry replays the retained payload against the exact failed authority and rejects locally if canonical authority changed. Client copy suggestions bind the claimed original text to the authoritative section copy inside the same revision-conditional boundary, so callers cannot persist a false “Original” history. Required artifact/domain commits precede terminal-job bookkeeping, and optional activity, cache, broadcast, notification, outcome, reconciliation-cleanup, and follow-on effects wait for a verified durable terminal and remain independently guarded. A terminal-bookkeeping failure is recorded as `completion_tracking_failed` where possible and never relabels the committed artifact/domain outcome as generation failure. Migration 190 retains Webflow item identity/state when external success is followed by a lost local CAS; retry reuses the item and resolves reconciliation after the local stamp succeeds. Publish acceptance freezes the effective site/collection/field map, one-way token identity, and exact brief-summary revision, revalidates them at every external boundary, and fails closed on partial, cross-collection, or unresolved external identity so target drift cannot create a duplicate. Post deletion is blocked while a resource-scoped post/publish job is active or reconciliation remains unresolved. Content-request events invalidate every linked brief/post authority reader across admin and client caches, so request-driven revision advances do not strand an editor on a stale token. Public brief/post projections omit generation authority; public briefs additionally omit raw source evidence and public posts omit internal QA diagnostics. Authenticated brief exports intentionally retain source evidence while omitting generation authority. Client copy/unified-deliverable projections omit internal generation fields, and public content-request reads/mutation receipts use an explicit projection that excludes workspace identity, operator reasoning/notes, decline reason, targeting IDs/slugs, and recommendation context while preserving status-gated artifact visibility.

**Why it matters:** Operators and clients can safely edit, approve, request changes, or publish while long-running generation is active without having their decisions silently overwritten. Automation receives deterministic stale-write and duplicate-work outcomes, independent work remains parallelizable, and every adopted AI artifact can be traced internally to the execution that actually authorized it without exposing prompts, secrets, or implementation metadata to clients.

**Tests:** Migration preservation and mapper coverage, including publish reconciliation and post-version provenance; canonical and external provenance validation/fingerprinting; job-claim overlap, cancellation, recovery, terminal-write-failure drain, and route parity; brief/post/copy mutation-race and no-op coverage; client decision, serialized autosave/explicit-retry/flush, send, schedule, revert, publish, active-job/reconciliation delete blocks, cross-collection identity, configuration/brief authority drift, and external-success/local-conflict handling; MCP source/parent revision, parent target-keyword/brief-lineage/lifecycle, correctable same-handle retry, changed-authority reprepare, atomic handle-adoption/send contracts, and exact TTL-boundary expiry; terminal-bookkeeping plus post-commit effect injection; linked-artifact event/cache coherence; public content-request/copy/unified-deliverable redaction and authenticated export evidence retention; and frontend caller/component revision threading. Independent adversarial review found no remaining P0/P1. Fresh `origin/staging` and C2 manifest builds measured 1,809,882 → 1,815,469 aggregate gzip bytes (+5,587); the ratchet raises the aggregate by that exact delta and updates only C2-owned serialization/content-editor/review entries, leaving unrelated pre-existing chunk drift untouched. Local closeout is green: 1,906 contract tests across 164 files; 5,083 passing component tests across 392 files (plus one skipped and three todo); 15,697 unit tests across 1,008 files; and 7,685 integration tests across three 198-file CI-equivalent shards. The last integration shard exposed and closed an authority-precedence/lifecycle-response defect plus two invalid async mocks; the 12-test affected cluster, typecheck, independent remediation review, and the complete 2,444-test shard rerun all passed. GitHub CI and post-merge staging checks also passed before C3 began.

**Files:** migrations 189–191; shared content/copy/AI/job contracts; `server/generation-provenance.ts`; `server/schemas/generation-provenance.ts`; `server/jobs.ts`; `server/content-publish-reconciliation.ts`; brief/post/copy storage, generation, review, publish, request, route, MCP, API, and UI callers; `src/hooks/{useAutoSave,useSerializedArtifactSave}.ts`; explicit content public projections and shared send services; focused unit/integration/component tests; `docs/rules/{generation-runtime-integrity,mcp-deliverable-generation,rich-text-content,background-generation}.md`; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 688. Content generation context v2 — bounded evidence projections and captured voice authority 2026-07-14

**Status:** Implementation complete behind the default-OFF, workspace-aware `content-generation-context-v2` flag. Staging flag-ON quality and runtime p95 evidence remains required before promotion or flag retirement.

**What it does:** Extends the existing content intelligence builder with one per-run context assembly that returns three bounded projections: a brief projection with only the matching page-map row, a smaller draft projection, and a voice-review projection. It captures calibrated system voice/guardrails and operator notes once, then reuses that authority across initial brief generation, all initial post stages, section regeneration, voice scoring, and MCP brief/post preparation instead of re-reading mutable brand state. MCP v2 responses separate system and user prompt context and include the exact context fingerprint; flag-OFF response shape and call order remain unchanged.

**Grounding and performance:** SERP, reference-page, and style text is timestamped, sanitized, and wrapped as untrusted data. Untimestamped stored keyword metrics and legacy SERP-feature flags are omitted; absent metrics or SERP evidence produces explicit unknown/needs-research instructions rather than fabricated numbers or questions. Required context fails before paid work if it cannot fit its stage budget, while optional intelligence is included only when space remains. A deterministic 20-row matrix fixture reduces the page-map projection from 723 to 57 estimated tokens (92%), and the consumer tests prove one v2 intelligence build replaces the legacy repeated SEO/page-profile/insight/learning/brand reads.

**Tests:** Builder coverage pins one assembly, target-only row selection, timestamp-authorized metrics, stage budgets, exact-once user/system voice separation, frozen authority reuse, untrusted wrappers, honest missing evidence, stable fingerprints, and pre-dispatch budget failure. Brief, post-stage, voice-score, prompt-assembly, MCP, flag-catalog, and deterministic AI-quality tests pin direct consumer wiring and legacy flag-OFF compatibility. The bounded adversarial pass found and fixed the two provenance leaks above; focused tests, AI-quality verification, and typecheck pass.

**Files:** `server/intelligence/generation-context-builders.ts`; `server/prompt-assembly.ts`; brief/post/voice generation consumers; MCP content preparation actions; feature-flag and AI-quality registries; focused unit/integration/contract tests; context-builder rules and generation-quality plan; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 689. MCP content-matrix M1 — grounded one-cell preview and atomic generation seam 2026-07-14

**Status:** M1 implementation complete on `codex/content-matrix-generation-m1`. Free preview and evidence-resolution actions are exposed through HTTP and MCP; the paid one-cell execution seam remains internal and protected by the default-OFF `content-matrix-generation` flag. Deterministic audit, bounded revision, batch jobs, review delivery, and matrix creation remain pending M2–M4.

**What it does:** Turns one explicit matrix cell into a generation-ready target without treating matrix labels as facts. Preview freezes the exact matrix, template, cell, voice, approved-identity, evidence, block-manifest, and content-context revisions; reports typed service/location/CTA requirements; and returns a stable effective-input fingerprint plus bounded cost estimate before any paid work. Evidence resolution uses durable factual source or artifact references, source/artifact/current-cell revision checks, and idempotency. A newly verified fact advances only its target cell revision; exact retries are side-effect-free, structural sources cannot satisfy factual requirements, and unresolved facts remain typed `[NEEDS CLIENT INPUT: …]` placeholders.

**Generation integrity:** The internal one-cell seam revalidates the exact preview selection, generates brief and post candidates without premature artifact writes, then atomically commits the brief, post, exact generation revisions, cell links, and item status. Late operator edits win as conflicts with no partial artifacts; failed, cancelled, and conflicting attempts stay truthful in the run ledger; and an exact retry can replay a completed result after the successful commit advances the cell revision. M1 stops at `auditing_deterministic`, leaving the actual audit/readiness verdict to M2. Nothing auto-approves, sends, publishes, or replaces an operator-owned artifact.

**MCP, review, and tests:** Adds `preview_content_matrix_generation` and `resolve_content_matrix_evidence` as bounded `json_v1` tools, with matching authenticated HTTP adapters and exactly-once post-commit activity/broadcast/cache effects. The bounded adversarial pass found and fixed three production-relevant defects: wall-clock assembly time destabilized otherwise identical fingerprints, successful commits prevented exact idempotent replay, and CTA placeholders were omitted when the CTA lived in the locked system conclusion. The consolidated relevant suite passes 12 files / 154 tests, covering preview/evidence behavior, candidate no-write generation, atomic commit/rollback, replay, stale authority, MCP mapping, and HTTP side effects.

**Files:** matrix-generation evidence, preview, stage, repository, and single-cell domain modules; brief/post candidate and persistence seams; content-context fingerprinting; matrix HTTP and MCP actions; shared matrix/MCP contracts; focused unit, integration, and contract tests; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 690. MCP content-matrix M2 — grounded item audit and bounded revision 2026-07-14

**Status:** M2 implementation complete on `codex/content-matrix-generation-m2`. The service remains internal behind the default-OFF `content-matrix-generation` flag; M3 still owns batch orchestration, MCP start/get/retry tools, set-level audit, and the admin action.

**What it does:** Advances one M1 page draft through deterministic and model audit without changing the locked template or adding a public surface. Deterministic checks cover the exact block/heading census, canonical URL, locked metadata and hard metadata limits, primary-keyword positions, complete internal-path census, typed placeholder count, verified local evidence, AEO roles, CTA clarity plus verified destination/instruction, within-page duplication/thinness, and lexical voice guardrails. Factual accuracy and no-hallucination checks always remain human-owned.

**Revision and integrity:** Named schema-validated audit/revision operations receive the exact finalized voice snapshot, approved identity versions, typed evidence, frozen block manifest, and current draft. A model finding reaches automatic revision only when the model explicitly recommends it; human-only findings preserve the unused allowance. At most one revision can commit, it cannot add or change links, block IDs, headings, metadata, or typed placeholders, and every accepted revision reruns deterministic checks before a final model audit. Attempt fingerprints match durable provider provenance, post adoption is revision-conditional, and a concurrent operator edit wins without being overwritten.

**Tests and review:** DB-backed service coverage pins the three-call maximum (audit → revision → audit), zero-spend evidence blocking, attention-only findings, revision regression without a third call, exact attempt/provenance history, and operator-edit conflict. Pure coverage pins all deterministic gates, structured-output rejection, unknown model targets, frozen revision structure, and invented-link rejection. The bounded adversarial review found and fixed missing metadata limits, CTA evidence drift, and unnecessary revision spend for human-only findings.

**Files:** `server/domains/content/matrix-generation/{audit,item-audit,operations,output-schemas,repository}.ts`; `server/{ai-operation-registry,content-posts-db}.ts`; `scripts/ai-reliability-registry.ts`; focused unit tests; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 691. MCP content-matrix M3 — durable bounded page-set generation 2026-07-14

**Status:** M3 implementation complete on `codex/content-matrix-generation-m3` behind the default-OFF `content-matrix-generation` flag. M4 still owns matrix creation/linking from service/location or collection-blueprint inputs.

**What it does:** Turns an explicit previewed cell selection into one durable parent generation job rather than nested per-page jobs. The run records per-cell checkpoints and outcomes, bounds concurrency, reconciles restarts and cancellation, deduplicates same-command starts, conflicts overlapping different commands, and retries only explicitly selected failed or attention-required items against exact run, item, source, artifact, and checkpoint authority. Every provider dispatch reserves its pessimistic call/token/cost ceiling in the durable accepted budget before paid work, so failures, fallback attempts, and retries cannot silently exceed the operator-approved limits.

**Audit, review, and delivery boundaries:** Item audits now feed deterministic set checks plus a named schema-validated model set audit for cross-page uniqueness and consistency. Missing, stale, or item-affecting set verdicts demote pages from review-ready; retry clears the prior set report. Human review-only approval requires exact run/item/post revisions and a current passing set audit, records approval evidence atomically, and advances only the post's export readiness. It never creates a send or publish job.

**MCP and admin flow:** Adds paid `start_content_matrix_generation`, free paged `get_content_matrix_generation`, and paid `retry_content_matrix_generation` `json_v1` tools with durable exactly-once paid-command metering. Matching authenticated HTTP adapters expose start/read/retry/approval. The existing Matrix Generate Brief(s) action now performs exact-cell preview, explicit budget confirmation, background start, live compact status, and selected retry; workspace events invalidate the matrix/content readers. Output remains at most `ready_for_human_review` until a human acts.

**Review and tests:** The bounded adversarial review closed stale set reports on retry, unenforced runtime reservations, restart-stranded review-ready items, response-size cap bypass, persisted 0/N progress, and a preview/runtime budget mismatch that blocked a normal 10-page set. Focused coverage also pins exact concurrent start collapse versus same-cell command conflict, paid MCP metering, post-commit schema queueing, fallback reservation, and pessimistic preview ceilings against every real generation stage. The relevant M3 batch, recovery, audit, MCP, AI fallback, component, contract, and feature-flag suites pass.

**Files:** migration 192; `server/domains/content/matrix-generation/{batch-service,budget,worker,recovery,retry-repository,set-audit,approval-service}.ts` plus the M1/M2 seams; matrix HTTP and MCP adapters; background-job, feature-flag, event, shared matrix/MCP contracts; `src/hooks/admin/useMatrixGeneration.ts`; existing Content Planner/Matrix UI wiring; focused unit/contract/component tests; `server/mcp/README.md`; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 692. MCP content-matrix M4 — pSEO blueprint-to-matrix creation bridge 2026-07-14

**Status:** M4 implementation complete on `codex/content-matrix-generation-m4`; M0–M4 now close the content-matrix generation roadmap item. The existing rollout flag remains default OFF pending staged activation.

**What it does:** Adds bounded `getPseoMatrixPlan()` source discovery plus one domain-owned `createMatrixFromPseoPlan()` mutation shared by HTTP and MCP. A collection blueprint entry supplies the durable source and linked template; the caller reads and returns exact entry/template authority plus explicit service/location or other template dimensions. The bridge validates exact page-type/variable/value alignment, derives URL/keyword patterns from the linked template, generates the deterministic Cartesian cell set, and links the resulting matrix ID back to the blueprint entry in one immediate transaction. The singleton entry link and exact definition fingerprint make retries resource-idempotent; a stale source or changed/missing linked matrix conflicts instead of creating a second set.

**Safety and delivery boundary:** Creation blocks duplicate planned URLs against other durable matrices and exact keyword cannibalization, rolling back both the matrix and source link on failure. Exact conflicts use a linear, short-circuiting guard, so the valid 2,500-cell ceiling cannot allocate a quadratic advisory report while holding SQLite's writer lock. Normal service/location siblings are not rejected by the legacy detector's medium-overlap heuristic; the existing generation structural preflight and set audit remain authoritative for live-site URL census and nuanced overlap. Typed service availability and local-relevance requirements remain generation preflight evidence, not claims inferred from dimension labels. Materialization creates no generation run, job, AI call, approval, send, or publication action.

**MCP and tests:** Adds bounded snake-case `get_pseo_matrix_plan` and `create_content_matrix_from_pseo_plan` `json_v1` tools with canonical registry discovery/dispatch/workspace scope, sanitized errors, durable MCP-key activity attribution, and the same compact matrix/source identity as HTTP. Real spawned-server coverage reads source authority and creates/replays the same plan across HTTP and `/mcp`, pins exact 2×2 and legal 2,500-cell Cartesian output, one durable link, zero generation runs, stale-source and confused-deputy rejection, incomplete-dimension rejection, URL/cannibalization rollback, committed-success semantics when optional effects fail, and replay side-effect suppression. Focused MCP schema/registry/instructions tests and typecheck pass.

**Files:** `server/domains/content/matrix-generation/pseo-bridge.ts`; the linear exact-match helper in `server/cannibalization-detection.ts`; thin Page Strategy HTTP and content-matrix MCP adapters; shared page-strategy/MCP schemas; MCP registry documentation/instructions and focused integration/contract tests; `data/{roadmap,features}.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 693. MCP brand-to-content onboarding — one resumable intake→page-set workflow 2026-07-14

**Status:** O1 implementation complete behind the existing default-OFF `brand-deliverable-generation` and `content-matrix-generation` flags. No composite flag or new UI was added; staging canary evidence remains the next rollout phase.

**What it does:** Adds one monotonic `BrandContentOnboardingRun` that coordinates the existing immutable intake, full-brand-system generation, grouped brand review, operator-finalized voice, approved identity, and a required non-empty matrix page set without copying their state. Standalone brand-only work remains in the existing brand-generation tools. Three snake-case `json_v1` MCP tools start, read, and resume the workflow one gate at a time. Authenticated HTTP adapters expose the same operations plus the human-only content-authorization boundary. Waiting on voice review/finalization, operator review, client review, content authorization, or page review leaves no generic job running.

**Authority and safety:** Start and resume require both underlying flags and exact revisions/idempotency. A cheap current-source check rejects mixed, duplicate, missing, or stale matrix selections before paid brand work. The coordinator freezes finalized voice and approved brand-deliverable fingerprints before content work; human authorization re-previews the selected matrix cells and exact budget, compares each page against the canonical page-type identity subset, and reconciles an already-accepted exact matrix child before any live re-preview. Partial/cancelled children remain truthful. A rejected voice foundation terminalizes the coordinator because that child has no revision path; a new run is required. Every selected page must carry the existing version-safe M3 approval evidence and pass a fresh read-only publish preflight before the workflow reaches `ready_to_publish`. The coordinator never creates a review, decides one, approves a page, dispatches a publish job, or calls the CMS—even when ordinary post auto-publish is configured.

**Tests and MCP:** Migration/repository coverage pins single-row persistence, the normalized immutable command ledger, workspace/intake idempotency, optimistic revisions, restart readback, historical command replay, immutable child refs, and paid-child replay. Service coverage walks every gate and tests terminal voice rejection, early brand approval, stale source rejection before paid brand work, exact matrix-child crash reconciliation, stable child-error translation, cancelled and partial children, page-type identity authority, and a 10-page set with one missing approval. Safe projections redact nested MCP/system attribution. Spawned-server tests pin workspace scope, validation, and disabled-flag closure; registry/schema/instruction tests pin the 18-family / 83-tool MCP census and the absence of an MCP human-authorization action.

### 695. MCP generation R2 one-page rollout canary and audit corrections 2026-07-15

**Status:** Local R2 canary complete. The single page reached human-approved export readiness through the real MCP and browser paths. Bulk fan-out remains intentionally held until generation runtime, provider spend, and review burden are economical enough for a page set.

**What it proved:** An isolated Rinse Dental matrix cell for “Teeth Cleaning in Sarasota, FL” moved through exact-cell MCP generation, locked-template audit, the existing post editor, and the revision-safe Planner approval action. The final draft preserved all six locked headings, used only grounded Rinse service/location/booking evidence, and retained the existing human provenance warnings instead of pretending they were machine-resolved. Human approval changed the post to `approved` and recorded exact operator, run, item, source, and post-revision evidence.

**Corrections from the canary and adversarial review:** Automatic revision now unwraps any invented or retargeted link while preserving its text and every unchanged approved link, instead of rejecting an otherwise useful prose repair. Link identity is the decoded href plus normalized anchor text inside its frozen block, so a model cannot move an approved target, replace its anchor under the same URL, or lose an unchanged query-string link through entity encoding. Human-only, non-revisionable item or set warnings remain visible and advance to the human-review gate; actionable non-human warnings and errors still stop in `needs_attention`. The server and Planner review UI now consume the same shared blocking predicate, so a visible human-only warning cannot hide Review/Approve. Restart reconciliation uses the worker's normal terminal mapping, preserving `completed` when every recovered page is review-ready instead of reporting a false error. An unavailable internal-page census no longer dead-ends a page that contains no relative links, while any relative link still requires the authoritative census. A one-page run keeps deterministic set safety checks but skips the cross-page model audit and removes all corresponding input/output/cost reservation. The item budget now reserves the actually reachable audit → at-most-one revision → re-audit path rather than a second impossible revision cycle. The original selected-page census remains authoritative, so a partial multi-page result cannot masquerade as a one-page run; that global approval block now preserves already-audited pages while failed peers retry, allowing the restored full set to close in one audit instead of cycling. Multi-page set auditing and reservation are otherwise unchanged.

**Economics and publication boundary:** The clean initial pass reserved 14 provider calls, 259,483 input tokens, 38,400 output tokens, and $1.925041, and took about 2m54s. Canary debugging and exact retries ended at a cumulative 20 calls, 376,107 input tokens, 55,900 output tokens, and $3.033161; those retries are diagnostic overhead, not the expected steady-state page price. That evidence was not strong enough to justify multiplying the run across the remaining matrix. After browser approval, the isolated database showed no Webflow item/collection identity, no publish timestamp or slug, zero publish reconciliations, and zero `content-publish` jobs.

**Tests:** The focused component, audit, budget, and recovery safety cluster covers shared UI/server warning disposition, partial candidate census and retry closure, exact reachable reservation, deterministic/model behavior, block-and-anchor-identity revision-link filtering, query-link preservation, internal-path census, approval, and restart terminal handling. The real browser path showed `Approved for export`, and direct durable-state checks verified the approval and zero-publish boundary. Independent adversarial review findings were fixed rather than waived.

**Files:** `shared/types/matrix-generation.ts`; `server/domains/content/matrix-generation/{approval-service,audit,item-audit,operations,preview,recovery,set-audit,worker}.ts`; `src/components/matrix/MatrixGenerationStatus.tsx`; focused matrix-generation component, audit, budget, approval, recovery, and set tests; `docs/rules/mcp-deliverable-generation.md`; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 696. MCP generation R2 two-page rollout economics and grounded-prose closure 2026-07-15

**Status:** Two-page local MCP canary complete; the rollout-quality corrections are ready for staging review. The rollout flag remains default OFF. The canary intentionally stopped both pages at `needs_attention` for explicit human review rather than weakening model findings to manufacture a green verdict.

**What changed:** Service/location generation preflight now requires substantive source-backed service details separately from a service-availability confirmation. A service name and “available here” assertion can no longer fund a full page. The exact resolved fact block is re-read and carried through the brief-to-post adapter, so factual authority cannot disappear inside an AI-authored brief summary. Transient brief/post provider inputs plus audit and revision prompts share one reader-facing contract: state supported facts naturally, do not expose evidence/context/verification mechanics, do not repeat contact/CTA details across blocks, and preserve exactly the authorized typed-placeholder census; internal instructions are not persisted into the human-visible Tone & Style field. Evidence freshness comes from each source's capture time rather than its later resolution time, and the conservative fresh-through timestamp survives in brief/post provenance. The brief internal-link renderer also normalizes protocol-prefixed workspace domains, preventing malformed `https://https://...` links.

**Canary result and economics:** One real MCP run generated Dental Exams and Digital X-Rays pages from separate ADA/FDA service-detail sources plus existing Rinse availability, identity, location, and CTA authority. After one bounded revision per page, both drafts had zero deterministic audit failures and zero reader-facing evidence-mechanics signals. The clean two-page run reserved 28 provider calls, 578,312 input tokens, 76,800 output tokens, and $4.071766; one targeted durable re-audit brought the cumulative reservation to 30 calls, 631,088 input tokens, 81,800 output tokens, and $4.485646. The free preview remains a deliberately pessimistic hard ceiling, not a spend forecast.

**Truthful boundary:** Residual conservative model findings—including provenance-sensitive amenity/experience wording and one CTA/link issue exposed by the malformed-domain bug—kept both items in `needs_attention`. The deterministic URL defect is fixed and pinned by unit coverage; no further paid canary or stochastic audit heuristic was added. Both posts remain `draft`, with no Webflow item or collection identity, publish timestamp, publish slug, publish reconciliation, or `content-publish` job.

**Files:** matrix-generation evidence, stage, audit, and revision operations; content-post brief link rendering; focused evidence, stage, audit, item-audit, and content-quality tests; `docs/rules/mcp-deliverable-generation.md`; `data/roadmap.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 697. MCP chat control plane for intake, pSEO discovery, and voice drafting 2026-07-15

**Status:** Complete on `codex/mcp-chat-control-plane`; human approval UX remains the isolated next PR.

**What it does:** Makes the existing guarded generation pipeline reachable from MCP chat without weakening its human gates. `submit_brand_intake` normalizes questionnaire fields into the immutable intake service and durably replays caller-keyed submissions. `list_pseo_blueprint_entries` returns bounded collection-entry identities plus linked template/matrix IDs before authoritative `get_pseo_matrix_plan` validation. Voice chat can idempotently ensure a profile, revision-safely replace DNA/guardrails/context modifiers, and add proposed samples.

**Safety:** Missing intake facts remain empty, and caller-stable submission keys replay the original revision even after intervening intake edits. Voice writes use exact profile revisions, so stale chat state cannot overwrite newer operator work. Every MCP-added sample persists as `mcp_proposed` and is structurally ineligible for finalization until a human explicitly attests it in the platform; a human must still select and authorize exact anchors. MCP cannot mint its own operator authorization or finalize without the one-time human-issued token.

### 698. Chat-driven brand voice review and human approval 2026-07-15

**Status:** Complete. MCP can draft the full mutable voice profile, while final authority remains an explicit authenticated human action in the platform or a one-time delegated completion.

**What it does:** Makes `Review & approve` the first voice-calibration section and shows an always-visible warning until the current voice is finalized. The checklist exposes missing DNA, guardrails, authentic anchors, and chat-proposed samples. Operators can inspect the exact DNA, terminology rules, tone boundaries, context modifiers, and anchor examples before locking the revision. MCP-proposed samples remain ineligible until an operator confirms each one as authentic; that revision-safe attestation records human activity and reopens any stale finalized authority.

**Approval boundary:** `Approve and lock voice` finalizes directly in the authenticated platform. As a chat-native alternative, the operator can create a plaintext bearer code that expires after 15 minutes, works once, and is cryptographically bound to the exact profile revision, DNA, guardrails, context rules, selected anchors, and idempotency key they reviewed. MCP can consume that code but cannot mint it, attest its own samples, or substitute a reusable password. The token is shown only at issuance and only its digest is stored.

**Tests and delivery:** Unit, integration, contract, component, query-key, and workspace-event tests cover attestation provenance, anti-forgery, revision conflicts, finalization eligibility, exact approval payloads, token disclosure, and cache invalidation. Independent adversarial review found and closed omitted tone-spectrum review, an over-25-anchor rejection path, and snapshot-codec rollback drift by introducing frozen V2 writes alongside V1 reads. The existing brand/content generation gates remain unchanged.

**Files:** `shared/types/brand-engine.ts`; voice calibration domain/schema/HTTP adapter; brand voice finalization read model; Brand Voice approval UI and API client; query/event invalidation; focused unit, integration, contract, and component tests; `BRAND_DESIGN_LANGUAGE.md`; `data/{roadmap,features}.json`; `FEATURE_AUDIT.md`.

### 699. Direct MCP template-to-matrix surface and discoverable validation 2026-07-15

Direct creation defaults URL, keyword, and schema targets from the template, validates template ownership, materializes the Cartesian cells, and stops before preview or paid work. No agent approval, voice-finalization, content-review, send, or publish authority was added.

Focused HTTP, domain, MCP schema/registry, validation, brand, and pSEO regressions preserve the existing callers.

### 700. Unified Brand & AI pending review and MCP approval reads 2026-07-15

**Status:** Complete. Human approval and finalization gates remain intact.

**What it does:** Adds `get_pending_approvals(workspace_id)` as the single read for voice finalization, full chat-proposed voice samples, and full draft brand deliverables, with a reason for every pending item. `get_brand_voice` now exposes all pending proposals and reports the actual missing DNA, guardrails, or authentic-anchor prerequisite instead of repeating only the finalization state. `add_brand_voice_samples` accepts a bounded sample set at one expected profile revision and persists it with one revision bump. `get_brand_identity` always reports approved/pending/total deliverable counts and returns `pending_approval` instead of `ready` beside an empty approved identity.

The Voice review surface also offers `Approve all N proposals` above the complete untruncated set. Voice finalization remains a separate explicit human lock because it establishes downstream generation authority; MCP still cannot attest, approve, mint authorization, or bypass the one-time token boundary.
